### Inger — 2012-12-17T17:32:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gunman-was-vegan.5197/post-67743>

Too sad. An organic vegan because of moral/believes. It sound so healthy and good doesn't it?
I can tell it touches something in me too, as I have a mental ill younger brother that I whitnessed getting SO MUCH BETTER with an epi paloe lfestyle last summer, but as he got talked around from some vegan friends it went south and fast. Now he is hospitalized and very bad off.

I could cry out loud when I see, again and again, how the ignorance and numbness of folks around contribute to the suffering in our communities. The unwillingness to recognize that what you eat matters HUGE!
My own parents believe all what I tell them, and do some stuff too, but they still are, in practice, totally ignorant of the fact that what we eat forms who we will be. They plain do not get it, like serious. They do not see, how darn serious it is.

Sometimes I get such a strong feeling.. people are SLEEPING...?!
WTH.. they are like half dead, really. I see them around me all the time. Sometimes in the train, I look around at peoples faces when I have my dark glasses on at night. It is almost like in a horror movie... I mean, there do are sunshines in between, but the majority.. it is something very wrong.
And then, people are suprized when stuff like this happends? I am not one bit. I am suprized, they are not happening more often!

### caroline — 2012-12-17T17:54:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gunman-was-vegan.5197/post-67745>

I was asking my Doctor about this yesterday. I am the only one in his practice with all these questions - so we are learning together. He said - mostly patient's eyes just glaze over and they want a quick fix. How can you not want to know about things that can and will make your life better? It is exciting for him \[his words\] and he says we are learning together. It is kinda scary that in some things I know as much as he does and some things we just have to leave for Dr.K. to sort out. I guess - that in itself is remarkable - he wants to learn from JK as well.
I think that is what makes us all unique and alike here - even tho we are all so, so different. Most of us have been fighting for years for more information to make our life better and we never gave up and may have taken lots of detours......but now JK has given us the tools and we just have to do the work. I am having the time of my life!

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-18T09:40:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gunman-was-vegan.5197/post-67801>

That's really a good description DD and Ingar....people being asleep or the waking dead. I find it most difficult to be around large groups of people, the lack of health and vitality seems overwhelming then. It is important to maintain really clear boundaries so that we don't get sucked into the vortex of despair that is all around us....and we have to be vigilent to see the light and be the light because that is always there too...we just don't feel it when we're clobbered with the reality of our fellow humans.

Â Without shame, I've chosen to not engage in all the news about the shooting. I learned the basic facts and have not listened to anything since. I need to be able to feel my feelings rather than be fed a whole lot of sensationalized language and visuals. If we can bring the experience back to ourselves and feel our own responses we stay true to what it means for us. That leaves us open to reaching out in a way that isn't shaped by a kind of "group think" mass process. Grief and sorrow are powerful and healing emotions and I don't fear them....despair isn't, so when I start to despair or feel depressed (lots of experience with that) I know I have to review what's going on with me and change something up.Â

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-21T10:52:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gunman-was-vegan.5197/post-68117>

The first moment I heard about this tragedy, I wondered what the gunman's diet was like. I just KNEW it had to be something like this. Not enough DHA, not enough MCT, too many carbs. Myelin sheaths not optimal. I wonder if he was breastfed or given soy formula, like so many of us? We'll probably never know, unless his father talks about it, but not getting mother's colostrum is the first thought.

I immediately came to this forum because I wanted to re-read what Dr. K. has been telling us for a year about how autism is formed by our lack of proper epi-paleo nutrition, not the vaccines. He's said it over and over. I bet he feels sometimes like he's screaming his head off in a cave. Read Dave Asprey's story; fascinating.

People keep asking WHY. But it's the wrong WHY. They want to know WHY he did the shooting. They should be asking WHY was his brain so messed up in the first place? Go back to his birth and follow his nutritional patterns. Oh, and let's not forget the thousands of hours he played video games. I'd bet my next paycheck he stayed up till all hours of the night. He lived in the basement, so very little sunlight. I could go on.

My heart just breaks for all these poor little schoolchildren.

And I, too, feel sometimes elitist when I walk through a mall and look at all the sad-looking, bloating, inflamed people. Until I remember my own d\*mn self a very short time ago.

### Greg rx — 2013-01-17T18:44:07+0200

I love blaming the veggie diet but I have some observations that NOBODY is talking about.
All/most of these gunmen have no fathers who taut them the real use of firearms and respect for their power.
All/most of these gunmen are already being treated with SSRIs witch are famous for causing suicide in teens. Thanks big pharma
These shootings are a suicidal acts so prevention after they have made the decision is futile.
Having played these shootem up video games with my kids, I know that weapon selection is a big part of the game. I predict that if and when assault weapons are denied, the next option is going to be a bolt action sniper rifle from a high perch with good cover. Mark my words. Assault weapons are just for fun, a hand gun or riot shotgun would be more efficient.

# Thread: Something a little sad
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2012-12-17T16:53:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/post-67734>

I got an e-Christmas card the other day from my medical practice and then .....the next day.....
there is another email cancelling the Christmas wishes!

I guess it isn't politically correct to wish someone Merry Christmas any more. That is a big deal in the U.S. I think??

### diane — 2012-12-17T18:36:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/post-67751>

It seems to be circling back a little bit here - at least the Facebook sentiment is LOL. Â Although most companies will use Happy Holidays to be politically correct.

I read a story a couple of years ago in the Toronto Star by a Muslim woman who supported Merry Christmas and her point was it didn't offend anyone she knew. No one else changes their holidays to be politically correct, so her point was why should Christians?Â 

Â 

I love helping other celebrate their holidays - I always learn something about other cultures!Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-17T19:11:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/post-67754>

Not everyone celebrates Christmas, that's why. Â And it's not "political" correctness. Â Wishing someone happy holidays covers all the bases ... Christmas, Kwanza, Hannukah, Saturnalia. Â I don't know why people freak out about this so much. Â If it's a personal friend whom I know to be Christian I wish them Merry Christmas. Â Businesses who don't know their clients faiths (or lack thereof) play it safe by using Happy Holidays. Â WHY is this an issue?

Short answer it isn't. Â Unless you're looking to start a fight.

Having said all that:

Â 

"Happy whatever does not offend you."

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-17T22:16:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/post-67770>

I think it actually is sad that we've become so politically correct we can't wish someone a Merry Christmas.Â  I happen to be Jewish, and I say Merry Christmas all the time.Â  And when someone says it to me, I say "Merry Christmas to you too".Â  I'm not sure I get what the big deal is.

Â 

When I was little, I remember saying to my dad, "But dad, we're Jewish, why did you say Merry Christmas back to that man?" and my dad would say, "Because he was saying something nice, and we'll say something nice back.Â  There's no reason to go any further when you are exchanging nice greetings".Â  So I learned from that, and all these years later, we've become so overly politically correct it's crazy.

Â 

If a Christian person was wandering in a heavily Jewish part of a city and someone wished them a Happy Hannukah, do you think they'd be offended?Â  Or pleased that someone took the time to wish a nice greeting?Â  I prefer to think the latter.

### diane — 2012-12-17T23:01:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/post-67774>

Totally agree Pam. I think the issue is that people feel guilty or that there is something wrong saying "Merry Christmas". If they choose "happy holidays" no problem. It's just that "Merry Christmas" or "Happy Hanukkah" shouldn't be a problem, no matter the beliefs.

Â 

Heck, when I first moved to Toronto I learned how to say "kung hoy fat choy" (which I probably just butchered the spelling LOL). It doesn't matter that I may not celebrate it, but I wish that everyone be happy and merry. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/post-67845>

### caroline — 2012-12-17T18:06:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-an-observation-for-newbies.5199/post-67749>

I have been struggling getting the appropriate labs done in Australia. It occurred to me yesterday - better late than never -
That it would be so much better to have all the necessary labs done at around the same time so you have a definite bench mark to go from - especially with a doc who is learning along with you. My labs are spaced out over 6 months - mostly because I couldn't get some that Dr. K. wanted at the time. Now we have some crazy numbers and don't really know what is affecting what - it is such a hormonal dance.

### nonchalant — 2012-12-17T21:08:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-an-observation-for-newbies.5199/post-67762>

Yeah, and you get fewer needle sticks.Â  One jab for several tests.

# Thread: I think I'm getting adapted to eating Epi-Paleo after less than 3 weeks!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-im-getting-adapted-to-eating-epi-paleo-after-less-than-3-weeks.5201/>

### furface — 2012-12-17T21:51:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-im-getting-adapted-to-eating-epi-paleo-after-less-than-3-weeks.5201/post-67767>

I've been on the Leptin Rx since Dec. 3rd, and met my dad for lunch today at a local supermarket which has a built in cafe. So before I was eating Paleo, we'd have potato wedges, and chicken wings. Which is what my dad had. I could have "cheated" since it would have been the first time since I began Leptin Rx, but NO! I'm determined to stay on course.

So I had some fresh B.C. dungeness crab meat, and a couple of squares of 90 % Dark Chocolate. And a glass of ice water. And I was full! This was 6 hours after my BAB. I actually had no craving for the wedges or wings.

But damn was the crab expensive! $89.90 a KG!! Nearly $30.00 lb.

Had a crustaceous dinner as well...fresh steamed Atlantic Lobster (The whole thing) $8.98 lb. dipped in homemade garlic butter! ### Rachelle — 2012-12-18T16:13:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-im-getting-adapted-to-eating-epi-paleo-after-less-than-3-weeks.5201/post-67843>

Getting healthy is really enjoyable isn't it Furface?

Â 

And yeah, talk about expensive. I ordered just 1 lb of Alaskan Red King Crab for $40 a lb and the rest will be Alaskan Blue Crab for $29 a lb.Â 

**He sent us this warning**. "This is 100% U.S. Caught and Processed King Crab. It is not Russian King Crab which floods the market and generally costs much less (inÂ many cases, Russian crab is sold as Alaskan crab). The problem with Russian crab is that much of their population is in decline and theyÂ don't have the means to enforce fishing quotas. Purchasing Russian Crab here in the U.S. drives the poaching and over fishing in theÂ Western Bering Sea. For what it is worth, much of the Russian crab is processed in China. Alaska monitors crab populations very closelyÂ and sets tight regulations on the catches to prevent over fishing.There is also King Crab caught in the Barents Sea (not to be confused with the Bering Sea). The crab there is not native, and may beÂ considered a pest. However, there is nothing to say that threatened Russian Bering Sea King Crab is not being labeled as Barents Sea KingÂ Crab.

Â 

Sometimes it's better to be uninformed, maybe? However, since I am informed, he will be my only trust worthy source when it comes to purchasing some seafoods.

Â 

It's nice reading the progress you are making in such a short time. Same happened to me when I started to eat, mainly, fish.

Â 

I started Iodine with cofactors and Selenium etc.... and haven't noticed a big change.Â  I really thought my energy issue was a Thyroid one and still nothing.Â  Its driving me crazy thinking that this is another big letdown waiting to happen.Â  I suppose its possible something is improving with the added Iodine...but I'm certainly not noticing much on the positive side...

Â 

One of the possible "detox" effects was twitching eye....which I have sometimes...and it did seem to start happening more so than normal after I started the Iodine...but that's not a positive.Â  That is a negative...its not really annoying but its not a good thing either.

Â 

So tired of trying to figure this out...so much work and everything seems to be going no where.Â  Maybe its my failure to keep my diet going.Â  I keep falling off the wagon eating junk and probably haven't even been in ketosis or even in line with standard Paleo for two weeks in a row....I'm not the motivated sort.Â  This is hard.Â  So tired....

Â 

Thanks for any thoughts or ideas.Â  What exactly is eye twitching caused by?Â  Anyone know?Â  I think it had something to do with a possible detox side effect but it doesn't really hurt or anything...not sure if I'd call it a negative its more of a neutral.Â  Its a strange sensation.

Â Paying attention to the light cycles is probably the most important Â - also C/T. Â  It all ties together perfectly and you just have to dive in and go for it if you want to see results. ### furface — 2012-12-18T15:45:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-success-how-long.5202/post-67840>

As both Inger, and Caroline have said, Raiken...just take it one step at a time. Baby steps. Start slowly, and before you know it, you will build up so much momentum. Not to speed the results, but progressing along the road seems to follow naturally.

When I started my Leptin Rx on Dec 3rd, I weighed 360 lbs, had no energy, was always tired, and also loved junk food etc. I also had a freezer full of not totally Epi-Paleo approved food. Some Grass-Fed lean ground beef patties (Well that was E-PA), and some salmon and tilapia filets, but also quite a few ground turkey burgers. (I'd just gotten paid a week or so before and stocked up on what I thought was "healthy" meats.)

Anyway, I didn't want to throw that away since I'm on a fixed income, so I incorporated it into an semi Epi-Paleo diet I guess you could call it.

I did the BAB, bought plenty of bacon, some coconut oil, had some free run eggs...which some may like, some may not, but they help bring up the 50-75 grams of protein required for the BAB pretty fast. Bought some broccoli, asparagus, mushrooms, and my fave, baby bok choy.

I cut out all snacks between meals, started taking 5,000 i.u. of Vitamin D3 per day, 3 gr of Vitamin C, some B12, and B complex vitamins.

I also began doing face dunks in ice water a week ago today, and having Cold Water showers a week ago Sunday.

When I started the face dunks, I couldn't hold my breath longer than 28 seconds. This morning, only a week later I did two dunks of 1 minute each.

Same with the showers. My first one lasted 15 minutes. Last night it was 40.

It's surprising what the body can really do when motivated, and when you feel great!

I've also found that after only a week and a half of eating the BAB, my appetite has really decreased. I no longer crave snacks, and at times I don't even want to eat lunch.

The reason I'm saying all of this is because while the first week or so may be hard to adjust to, please do not give up, and give it another try, and stick to it. It truly does become easier, and all the baby steps put together actually make it easier to continue. You will feel so much better! You will sleep much better! CT is so refreshing, invigorating, and energizing, it's like you're "high" without the drugs (or so I assume), or crash.

Also once you start to see some results...either increased energy levels, more vigor, less appetite, better sleep, etc, it will motivate you to keep going, and increase your capacity and desire to add more to the whole LeptinRx/CT/Epi-Paleo recipe!

I weighed myself this morning, and currently weigh 236 lbs so in 15 days, I've lost 24 lbs. and it hasn't felt as if I've been deprived of anything!

Of course we are all different, but I'm just saying, as Caroline, and Inger have, please do not give up. Put forth the effort, and you will be thankful that you did!

Best of luck! We're all here to help!

I've learned so much from everyone here, and they have all been so helpful, and supportive!

### caroline — 2012-12-18T15:52:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-success-how-long.5202/post-67841>

So take one day at a time Â - have you thought of starting a journal? Â

### caroline — 2012-12-18T17:11:57+0200

### furface — 2012-12-18T17:25:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-success-how-long.5202/post-67863>

That's awesome, Caroline! I wish fruits were in season here as well. But then again since I still have a lot of weight to lose, maybe it's a good thing that they're not. haha

You are so right about the "cheating". Once you get healthy, having an ice cream cone, or even a slice of pizza...if you even desire it, won't feel like "cheating" but rather maybe a "reward" or even just something different or "new" even though it's old, to try. But it most likely won't even be something we really desire. Not that a person still won't find enjoyment in it, because in all honesty they still taste great, but it's not like it's a MUST HAVE, and we'd feel deprived if we don't have it. It's a take it or leave it thing I think, and we most likely wouldn't even feel guilty if we did. (at least we shouldn't IMHO)

Hey, heavy whipping cream, a sprinkle of stevia, and you have an awesome, and sweet treat for desert, and a great topping for fruit. Or just straight out of the bowl. haha

Enjoy!

# Thread: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome  Inflammation IL-6  mAChR

### Thor — 2012-12-18T03:06:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-fatigue-syndrome-inflammation-il-6-machr.5203/post-67787>

.

I haven't seen anything on here talking about Chronic Fatigue Syndrome & it's role with inflammation.Â  The more I read - the more I'm floored how bad this gets.Â  My CFS is even more fuel to the "fire" /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_sad.gif Â Â  ugh...Â Â Â Â  Looks like the fall out of long term inflammation can be the fire that starts CFS.

Â 

This article contains lots of "_geek_" to show it's role:

Â 

Â [http://www.plosone.org/article/info:doi/10.1371/journal.pone.0051515](http://doi/10.1371/journal.pone.0051515")

Â 

**Reduction of \[\[sup\]11\[/sup\]C\](+)3-MPB Binding in Brain of Chronic Fatigue Syndrome with

Â Â  Serum Autoantibody against Muscarinic Cholinergic Receptor**

Â 

Chronic fatigue syndrome (CFS) is a heterogeneous disorder characterized by persistent fatigue accompanied by rheumatologic, cognitive, and infectious-appearing symptoms. CFS research showed abnormal cytokine levels including tumour necrosis factor, interleukin-1, interleukin-6, increased markers of inflammation and stressful life events prior to CFS onset

Interesting look into WAT and how it plays a role into metabolic syndrome - looking at the Nrf2 activity....

Â 

Nuclear factor E2-related factor 2 (Nrf2) is a transcription factor that functions as a master regulator of the cellular adaptiveÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  response to oxidative stress.Â  Nrf2 plays a critical role in adipogenesis by regulating expression of CCAAT/enhancer-binding protein Î² and peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor Î³.

Â 

[http://diabetes.diabetesjournals.org/content/early/2012/12/11/db12-0584.abstract](http://http://diabetes.diabetesjournals.org/content/early/2012/12/11/db12-0584.abstract)

I would do leptin Rx, the adrenal Rx (the latest) and work on the leaky gut.Â  A consult with Jack would be a great start.Â  I can offer you lots of advice but that is based on my own N=1.Â  Jack has made it very clear that everything is in context.Â  Even some of the things in the blogs might not be for you.Â  Do you have labs confirming what you think you are dealing with?Â  If so, great!Â  Otherwise, time to get those and then figure out why you are in the state you are in.Â  Sometimes we think "this" is the reason why and it ends up to be something completely different.Â  I am becomingÂ a lab queen but I am also finding a lot of things out.Â  I am dealing with everything you are and now more; I wouldn't have had a clue had I not done all of those tests.Â  It might cost you out of pocket but if you truly want to get to optimal, testing is theÂ best way to really get you there.

I would put a lot of effort into fixing the gut no matter what since everything stems from the gut!Â  You can do all 3 though pretty easily.Â  You can eat the BAB, get the morning sun in your eyes, wear blue blockers at night and eat a lot of bone broth.Â  This will work on all 3 areas and it is pretty simple to implement.Â  I kinda freak now in the evening with too much light.Â  Gotta wear them glasses. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. >
> > What about ghee, duck fat andÂ bacon fat?Â  You can make your own ghee pretty easily.Â  As long as you only get the oil, you are good to go with it.Â  I, like you, have had the mood thing.Â  I am finally making some headway and having some happy days.Â  It is a wonderful feeling.Â  Keep at this and just do the best you can even if you side step and fall back a bit.Â  Get right back on track and before long, you will be feeling good and things will right itself.Â  Any more questions, just ask.
>
> Thank you, thank you! Â 
>
> I can't have ghee, as I have a lactose AND protein allergy, also with the autoimmune eczema stuff dairy is no good.
>
> But I think duck and bacon fat will be ok.
>
> One other thing, I do train/exercise, I find it helps greatly with mood etc, however from what I have read training with LR is no good, due to "using up" stem cells. Â Any thoughts on this? Â In my experience NOT training sends me into a very sad state.
>
> Is it possible with only having a few kgs to loose and hair/nail health improved that training would be ok?

I do eat ghee and I make it myself.Â  The president of our company just happens to be a chef and he taught me how.Â  He said to cook it until all the milk particles sink to the bottom.Â  I do this on very low heat and I do scrape off the top too in the mean time.Â  I have a dairy AI issue and can't eat it either.Â  I don't seem to have any problem with what I make.Â  There are also places to buy it and it is supposed to be virtually free.Â  Not saying you should, just that you might be able to.Â  You can also make your own and strain it thru cheesecloth or something.Â  Duck fat is very expensive, at least where I have bought it.Â 

If you have adrenal fatigue you are not really supposed to exercise, as well as until you are reset.Â  But I really don't know where you stand so it is hard to say.Â  You will use up your stem cells if you train too hard.Â  If it wears you out, then you are over training.Â I stillÂ have a little adrenal fatigue. Â You could walk or keep your training on theÂ mild side.Â Â Hard to say when I don't know howÂ hard you push.Â 

Jack talks a lot about sprints and lifting weights when seeing signs of LS.Â  No cardio.Â  I don't have weight to lose either and I have been doing this for 1.5 years.Â  I am hoping I can finally start exercising after I have my consult.Â  I know it use to make me feel better too.Â  You sound like you really need a consult.Â  Even a half hour to ask all your questions which are really good ones.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-08T10:26:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-protocol.5205/post-69847>

Hi vkierman, I have a question for you. I know back in Ocotober you were very concerned about gaining weight and not fitting into your clothes any longer since starting Progesterone. Is this issue cleared up now? Was it a temporary push on estrogen which then leveled out again? Do you still continue with P? Will you be able to stop it once your labs point to healthy ratios? Or do you have to stay on it forever due to your age?

Thanks.

# Thread: I found heaven....!!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-heaven.5207/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2012-12-18T08:48:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-heaven.5207/post-67799>

I am dancing, dancing.... just so full of joy I could explode.. HUH!!!! A new word is lately singing in my ears.

Serendipity.

It happends all the time now!!!!

WTH? I am living in my parents house for half a year now (I am allowed to live here for free in an own appartement upstairs when I take care of the animals, help a bit in the house and do snow work and in summer garden work -wich I all love!), that has been cursed by so many of my sibling, saying it kills them, the athmosphere.. and here am I living, and THRIVING...? I could not last time I lived here years ago. Now, everything have changed?!

Â 

This makes me even more then 100% sure, we are 100% responsible for our own happiness, and **everyone** can find heaven. Maybe it is already around?

Got to get some more fish and cold and dark in..lol. oh wait.. this is already too good! I just cannot take more for now! I love this!!!

Now out to do some snow work! { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### vkiernan — 2012-12-18T14:29:27+0200

Inger, thanks for always being an inspiration to all of us!Â  I will be as happy as you one day!

### caroline — 2012-12-18T16:56:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-heaven.5207/post-67853>

That is so how I feel - I am litterly bursting at the seams. It is like I can't contain myself. Everything is in focus.
I really think it is a lot to do with the raw food - I am no expert tho for sure - but everything is so radically different. It must be a combination of everything. When I eat raw - I have absolutely no digestion issues what so ever - quite amazing. No heaviness, no bloating. I do think too - that my bodies signals are much clearer. When dieting - we always used to be told - just eat until full......WELL...I was never full. I wouldn't have had the foggiest notion what my body was trying to tell me. Now it seams very clear. I know when I am full...I know when I am tired and .... I listen! It could just be Kruse magic! LOL

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-18T17:20:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-heaven.5207/post-67862>

I was thinking about this kind of wild happiness that seems to jump off the pages with you two (Caroline and Ingar)..I've had some inklings of it too this fall...I'm sure you are right that the raw food is just so full of what we need we soak it up instantly. I'm easing into the raw world by eating most everything raw first and then cooking the rest of it.

Â 

One of the things that interests me is how we can feel so great and energized but it isn't the same energy that stimulants give us...at least not for me...I'm buzzing, but I don't feel disconnected the way alcohol, caffeine or drugs made me feel. There is a bubbling quality to this kind of joy...not Â forced but just there, it feels like a gift every time it happens...maybe Â it has to do with balanced neurotransmitters...and proper energy production in our cells

Â 

For me, on the days that the thought of breaking the ice on our pond and wading in gets me all excited, I know i'm already feeling great and I'm going to feel better when I get out. It's all about temperature...when I feel cold, I know I'm not functioning well and my thoughts/emotions are more confused and despairing. I think I'm feeling myself at a cellular level...if that makes any sense...

### caroline — 2012-12-18T18:03:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-heaven.5207/post-67867>

Seahorse - you said that so perfectly! It is just a bubbling under the surface - like I can't contain myself. Everything looks the same and yet - somehow - much difference. Everything is surrounded/bathed in light. I can't be around people with no energy or those who lack interest in anything. I need to be around motivated, kind generous people now - I don't have time for anything less. And - another thing - I seem to attract those kind of people lately. People are always smiling and saying hello when I am out walking so they are obviously reacting to me. I was in an upmarket department store in sydney last week. I hadn't been for awhile and at least 5 people had big smiles on their faces when they saw me and called me by name and said they were so happy to se me. It blew me away that hey remembered my name. I asked one lovely young woman about it and she said - of course - we love it when you come in. Now - you have to understand - I don't have much money and these are just part time staff - so it's not like they get a huge payday!
anyway - I couldn't be more grateful

### caroline — 2012-12-18T18:07:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-heaven.5207/post-67868>

Anyway.......I guess that Dr. K. will explain this to us one day soon ..... when he gets done dancing thru the streets of New Orleans Naked. Why do we miss all the good stuff????

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-12-18T18:11:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-heaven.5207/post-67869>

caroline, what a great experience!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-18T19:39:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-heaven.5207/post-67885>

Dakota's Â now.......New Orleans was a few days ago.

### Inger — 2012-12-19T13:23:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-found-heaven.5207/post-67963>

> Seahorse - you said that so perfectly! It is just a bubbling under the surface - like I can't contain myself. Everything looks the same and yet - somehow - much difference. Everything is surrounded/bathed in light. I can't be around people with no energy or those who lack interest in anything. I need to be around motivated, kind generous people now - I don't have time for anything less. And - another thing - I seem to attract those kind of people lately. People are always smiling and saying hello when I am out walking so they are obviously reacting to me. I was in an upmarket department store in sydney last week. I hadn't been for awhile and at least 5 people had big smiles on their faces when they saw me and called me by name and said they were so happy to se me. It blew me away that hey remembered my name. I asked one lovely young woman about it and she said - of course - we love it when you come in. Now - you have to understand - I don't have much money and these are just part time staff - so it's not like they get a huge payday!
> anyway - I couldn't be more grateful

Caroline...!!! I cannot tell how happy I am to hear this.
It makes me just too happy!!! I have tried it so many years now, to feel the difference between raw and cooked, and it is just so big to me. I sometimes wonder if it is all imagination?! I try again.. both ways.. but there is always the difference you describe above!

Here in Finland many are barfing their pets (mostly dogs) now. They feed them raw. They say it makes a huge difference. Why wouldn't it in human? In my own experience it is a huge difference too. Mostly how my mind and spirit feels. I so much would like to know WHY? I just know, it works. And I feel stupid always telling people.. try more raw... raw animal foods! Because it is so tabu in many modern cultures today. But I so know, everyone would get benefits! IF THEY ONLY KNEW. But Caroline, you know now! You know what I mean. You have felt the joy too, the lifted spirit, the so much "alive" feeling. OMG I do not want to miss this ever! But it is exacty what happends if I begin to eat too much cooked..

I need 70 to 100% raw depending on my stress level and issues. The more isues and stress the more raw. It saves me totally! I am not talking about raw fruits and such. No, it must be animal foods. Fish, organs, fat.. wild herbs / seaweeds.

I am in love with reindeer meat now. I air dry it in body temps over night. I get it quite cheap, prefrozen, pieces with bone marrow in them. It is so crazy good. I eat them for breakfast. I just read, reindeer meat it very high in Q 10... no wonder I am so in love with it. And it always have to be raw. I need no spices, nothing.. just plain, warm, crispy, fatty right out of the dehydrator. Makes me feel I am in heaven too...

And I feel people smile at me all the time too..lol They are just so very nice and polite! I sometimes wonder, what lucky girl I am, everyone are threating me so very well.. it fels nice for sure!

### Inger — 2012-12-19T13:33:16+0200

> Â

# Thread: Question for the Thyroid Experts
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-thyroid-experts.5208/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2012-12-18T10:28:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-thyroid-experts.5208/post-67804>

Ok wondering about a few things...

on Friday I got my updated thyroid meds - and added 30mg of compounded Armour in the afternoon. I'm taking 60mg in the am - around 530 am

I feel great in the afternoon now - no slump... but now in the AM I feel like I'm dragging...

wondering if adding the afternoon dose is causing the am fatigue? or if its just remnants from having the flu last week. I just feel like I'm not quite awake...just general sluggishness and I feel like I could take a nap, at least from my body's perspective - though my brain feels sharp.

any thoughts on this???

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-18T14:17:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-thyroid-experts.5208/post-67822>

I would venture a guess that it's leftover from the flu as your body is still trying to regenerate. I would continue on for at least a week or two and see how you feel then. I haven't heard of anyone having issues with adding that second dose.Â  You are kinda the exception though!!!!Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Also watch the times you take it.Â  If you don't start to feel better than maybe you need to change the dosing time in the afternoon.Â  Watch how tired you get in the evening as maybe you need to take it later.Â  I found that 2pm was good for me even though I got tired in the evening.Â  But I couldn't go until 3pm or I was wasted in the afternoon.Â  This could affect your morning feeling a little because of the T4 that's in there still converting to T3 (hopefully converting).

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-18T14:32:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-thyroid-experts.5208/post-67829>

Thanks Patty.

the 1st 2 times I took it... at 4pm or later... and discovered I had a hard time falling asleep - so I moved it fwd btwn 2pm - 3pm.

so I know I'm definitely sensitive to it...

going to grab lunch here in a few...then take my armour closer to 3pm and see how things go tonight...

I'm inclined to think left over flu - but not 100% sure...

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-19T09:25:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-thyroid-experts.5208/post-67938>

I'm interested in your experiment with timing too...I've been taking 60 mg Armour in the am and adding another dose in the afternoon an hour or so after lunch. To complicate things I'm also trying to replace the Armour with Thyro-Gold. (Long story, but basically I haven't been able to get the prescription I want) Â So far things are ok, I an now taking 30mgArmour and I empty the thryro-gold out of two capsules. I can really feel that working...in the afternoon I'm taking two more Thyro-Gold.

I am finding that I want to go to sleep earlier...but that's ok at this time of year...Solstice on Saturday! Â I'm curious to see how I'll react to the light changing....first time in many years that I've had any energy during the lead up to Christmas, I know supporting my thyroid is a big part of this difference.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-19T10:16:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-thyroid-experts.5208/post-67941>

I took my 30 mg of armour around 3pm yesterday. Â got home after work and hopped in to the Â CT Tank for 30mins... I was passed out asleep by 9pm (I lay down with the kids when they go to bed) Â  woke up at 1030... got ready for bed and was back out by 11. Â woke up refreshed when my alarm went off this am.Â 

Â 

going to do the same thing tonight and throw in a BRTV/Daily HIIT workout while grilling pork chops for dinner...Â 

Â 

We'll see how things go tonight....Â

# Thread: Hello from Northern California
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-northern-california.5209/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-12-18T11:04:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-northern-california.5209/post-67806>

I'm Susan and I'm a 42 year old lady from NorCal. I'm here because I'm always on a quest to improve my diet and get healthy, but this year I have completely fallen off the good health wagon and I'm desperately trying to get back on.

I'm 5'6" and now weigh 134, but I am a very small boned lady with a ruptured disc so added weight and inflammation is really bad for me. I am hoping by visiting this site often and hearing other people's stories that it will strengthen my resolve to go back to healthful eating. I am trying to eliminate added sugar and most grains from my diet and it's tough during this time of the year especially, but I must do it.

Â 

Oh, I also have a bum thyroid and on thyroid medication. It must run in the family because my sister also has to take thyroid medication. In general I have messed up hormones due to an unexplained pituitary disorder that makes it so that I have too much prolactin in my system, throwing off all my other hormones. I take bromocriptine for this.

Â 

Glad I found this site!

### vkiernan — 2012-12-18T14:27:09+0200

# Thread: Lifting weights is preventing me from losing weight?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifting-weights-is-preventing-me-from-losing-weight.5210/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eve — 2012-12-18T11:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifting-weights-is-preventing-me-from-losing-weight.5210/post-67807>

Background: I am going on 12 months doing the Leptin Reset. Before that I wasÂ 1 year on gaps, and gluten- and mostly corn-free since May 2009.

I have a really bad leaky gut and in October 2012, I finally got in to see a specialist and I have a tentative diagnosis of Gastroparesis (slow stomach emptying) and Chronic Gastritis. I also have an IgA deficiency, which the specialist explained causes inflammation as well (forÂ reasons I can't remember)....So I have lots of inflammation going on.Â I am taking meds now for both and it is about 5% better. However, since October, I still cannot eat anything other than fish...literally. Fish cooked in a little coconut oil is all I have been eating for the past 2.5 months...not fun. Next on the list is to see an immunologist but am still waiting for an appointment.

Since the Fall, I've been trying to get back into strengthtraining. Since the Spring I've been doing off and on, maybe 1x a week or nothing at all. In the past few months, **I thought I was ready because I had most of the LS signs, except for feeling refreshed in the morning. After I lift weights, with the exception of my first day back, I do not get any muscle soreness really. I may feel a little more awake/wired after doing it. Lately I've been trying to go 2-3 times a week (40-45min) and I go around 7-7:30pm (last meal is usually 3-5 pm). I should mention that a few weeks ago I tried doing sprints...well more like walk/jog for 30min and that took it out of me for a few days and I had DOMS in my legs for the rest of the week. In the Fall, I was able to drop 7lbs in about a month or so. I went from 185 to 178lbs. (I am 5'7). During this time I was only playing recreational soccer 1x/wk.Â  The first few lbs came off quick as I eliminated meat from my diet (which is very acidic, not good for gastritis). But in the past few weeks, I'm back up to 182 and it is not budging. Do you think the weightlifting is causing it??Â  I'd hate to take it out because I like doing it. Sure,Â I don't really feel like going to the gym but once I am there, I get into it and it's nice to get out of the apartment. Alternatively, I could switch to walking........but only if I have to. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**

Â 

My thyroid is fine according to recent Labs. fT3 upper quartile and fT4 mid-range. I am on 3 grains Erfa thyroid.Â  Before this, I was severely undermedicated and both ft3 and ft4 were below range according to labs in the summer.Â  Interestingly, I don't have a reverse t3 problem. In Canada, we don't have a RT3 test but Dr. Kruse suggested alternative tests and they didn't indicate an issue.

My FBG is also <100 so I don't think I am LR at the liver. My HS-CRP is 1.6, so I know I have inflammation. I have low P and low-normal Estrogen, but I've been taking transdermal P since August and my periods are getting better and ligther.

### Inger — 2012-12-18T11:34:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifting-weights-is-preventing-me-from-losing-weight.5210/post-67812>

Eve.. are you serious about, all you have been eating is fish cooked in coconut oil?
If, your poor gut want get much bacteria.. I would eat a big part of the fish raw. Raw animal food is easier on the digestion, believe me. I am not thinking a 100% cooked food diet is very healing..
Arn't you eating any seaweeds either? What a bout raw organs? Can you eat meat at all, what about game? Raw oysters?
If you could tolerate beef / bison fat I would eat some raw too. Very healing in my opinion and very tasty, I just cut some thin slices and eat like that, or sprincle some salt on it and raw onion slices...
People have eaten fat like that forever in Russia and Estonia. I always found it yucky until I tried it myself!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-19T08:45:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifting-weights-is-preventing-me-from-losing-weight.5210/post-67933>

# Thread: Are pickles (the regular Vlasic type) and liverwurst Epi-Paleo approved foods?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 43
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-18T16:47:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67852>

Just wondering if pickles and liverwurst are Epi-Paleo approved foods?

If they are, I'm thinking that liverwurst spread on celery sticks might make for a quick, tasty "light" meal in place of a "full" meal on occasion.

Also, pickles would be fun to munch on during a meal if you don't want to have a "regular" vegetable.

Also to add some variety. And crunch. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-18T17:06:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67856>

liverwurst is always a win! Â 

as for pickles - you want fermented ones such as bubbies. Â skip the vlasic - no live cultures that are good for you. Â  of course you can always learn Â how to make your own...

Â 

I make my own saurkraut

### furface — 2012-12-18T17:15:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67859>

Thanks Shijin13.

I hope I can find Bubbies pickles somewhere here in the Vancouver area. As long as they're not like $20 for a miniscule sized jar.

I might learn how to make my own.

As far as sauerkraut goes...never was a big fan of it...too sour. LOL.

Great news on the liverwurst though.

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-12-18T17:46:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67866>

Learn to make your own!
Can't imagine it's a good time to find cukes for pickling in Vancouver though... probably not till summer.

Furface, not sure what kind of kraut you've had in the past, but if it was made with vinegar it would have tasted completely different than homemade. When I was a kid we would age our kraut on the cellar steps in a crock and it had a much stronger (and yummy to me) taste than the kraut I ferment on my counter top for 4 to 6 days (depending on the temp). My husband also says that he thinks that kraut made from red cabbage tastes less "krauty".

One of my new favorite things is to mix seaweed in with my cabbage for kraut (I ferment all of it together). Right now I'm having "Christmas Kraut" because I made a batch with red cabbage and seaweed. Who says we can't have special holiday treats!?!

But if you still aren't on board with the kraut you could try something like a giardiniera mix (but ferment it instead of putting it under vinegar and oil). Fermented ginger carrots are also good as well as garlic cloves.

This guy has some good recipes (you don't have to have the water lock): http://www.perfectpickler.com/pages/Recipes.html

### furface — 2012-12-18T18:25:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67872>

I've just had the regular type of sauerkraut before. Hey who knows, I might try and make my own. As I've said to Caroline, I may not delve fully into every aspect of this, but if I can get into 80-85% of this whole shebang, then that's better than nothing, and if that means I'll be 80-85% healthier than I would have been had I not, then that's fine with me. No one is going to live forever, so I don't expect "perfection", and am not looking for it either. To me life is too short either way to always be worried about every little detail about everything.

We can't always be "on guard", we have to chill and relax once in a while. "All work and no play makes Jack a dull boy." (As in Jack Nicholson from the movie, "The Shining." haha

Â 

Oh I will try fermented veggies, and that red cabbage and seaweed sounds not too bad. I still need a place where I can find sea veggies. I asked at a SuperStore grocery store yesterday, and the produce manager had no idea what I was talking about. And that's one of our largest food chains here.

### diane — 2012-12-18T18:56:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67876>

You may have to go to a health food store to find the sea veggies. Or one that sells Japanese food as a lot of the sea veggies are used in Japanese cuisine. I have also found Bubbies pickles in certain health food stores. And luckily there's a store across the street from me that would order them for me. They hadn't realized that they were fermented, so were really grateful that I asked for them!Â

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-18T19:26:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67879>

> **furface said**
> Just wondering if pickles and liverwurst are Epi-Paleo approved foods?
>
> If they are, I'm thinking that liverwurst spread on celery sticks might make for a quick, tasty "light" meal in place of a "full" meal on occasion.
>
> Also, pickles would be fun to munch on during a meal if you don't want to have a "regular" vegetable.
>
> Also to add some variety. And crunch.

Yes, they absolutely are.....and I pound them both regularly

### furface — 2012-12-18T19:31:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67883>

Good idea, Diane!

Â 

Awesome! Thanks Dr. Kruse! I love liverwurst...so squishy!

### endless — 2012-12-18T20:42:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67894>

Try the Tand T market...I think that's what it's called. The large asian grocery store. There used to be one in Metrotown mall and I'm sure there's got to be one in Richmond.

I made red cabbage sauerkraut and I definitely think it is mellower.

### furface — 2012-12-18T21:11:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67898>

Thanks for the suggestion, Endless. I'll give T&T a try.

### vkiernan — 2012-12-18T23:00:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67905>

These guys out here taught me how to make my own sauerkraut and it is easy.Â  You can also save a little of the juice from the bubbies store bought sauerkraut and add it to your batch made from home to get it started.Â  They also taught me to leave the bubbies kruat and pickles on the counter for a few days to get things rollin' inside againÂ as Bubbies has to lightly process it with heat.Â  I have also added pickling cukes to the leftover bubbies pickles juice to make another batch at home and just added more salt.Â  I did make the mistake of slicing the small cukes and that made them sort of mushy on the inside but still crunchy on the outside.Â  Next time I will not cut the little cukes first.Â 

Lots of great recipes on the net for making your own.Â  Â Â

### furface — 2012-12-19T00:17:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67911>

Awesome! Thanks for the tips, Vkiernan! I'll check out some of our health food stores here when I get a chance.

### Charlotte — 2012-12-19T16:37:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67975>

@ furface

so here comes the original version of sauerkraut from a real "Kraut"

This is how my great grandmother, grandmother and mother and now me made it:

Add water and pork ribsÂ  to the Sauerkraut and cook it until it is soft (takes a while). when it is it done, cut onions in small pieces and sautee them until they are soft and sweet in pig fat or ghee. Then add locust bean gum to it to make it thick or any other thickener you prefer and add it so the sauerkraut. The sauerkraut then becomes sort of creamy. If you want and if it is allowed, add some white wine, champagne or Marsala and cook, until the alcohol is gone. You can also cook some juniper berries with it. Gives it a nice tang.

It does not taste sour anymore at all. My mother used to add a little sugar, I add stevia but you don't really need it. It is fantastic with the ribs or also pigs feet. its phantastic.

### Inger — 2012-12-19T16:43:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67976>

I would be careful with store bought liverwurst

. Check out the ingredients. Here I can only get pig liver liverwurst with all kind of additives in it.. so I eat only self made liverwurst from beef liver, should be grassfed at best. Very easy and delicious! Check out on google how to make it, it gives lots of different recipes, with precooked liver, raw and then in the oven baked.. whatever, they are all good!

### furface — 2012-12-19T17:40:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-67981>

Thanks for the recipe, and info C.Adler, and Inger.

Â 

That sauerkraut does sound good. I might give it a try.

Â 

I'll be sure to check the label on the store brand liverwurst. If it doesn't look too bad though, I still may buy it if the price of the "specialty" type is something like $20 for a 2 inch tube, compared to $5 for the "regular" one at the store. I value my health, but I also value my $$ lol...esp being on a fixed income.

### endless — 2012-12-19T21:46:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-68019>

Inger, do you have a favorite link for making your own liverwurst? I don't like buying the commercial stuff either. I do have access in the fall to local pastured pig livers and might try that next year! I have lots of bison liver in the freezer to use for now.

### furface — 2012-12-19T22:13:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-68023>

Since we've been hit with a major snowstorm here in Vancouver, I wasn't able to go to a place like Whole Foods, or any other "specialty" type food store, so I had to settle for a small chub of Freybe's Fine Liverwurst. Not the best, but I don't think it's going to kill me. If the weather clears up, I'll look for a "better" option.

Anyway, just had my dinner, and I think I've found a new quickie meal...liverwurst spread over celery stalks. OMFG sooo Good, and so quick!

### endless — 2012-12-19T22:58:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-68032>

Hmm, I'll have to try that. I have been just eating it with a spoon.

### furface — 2012-12-19T23:16:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-68036>

> **endless said**
> Hmm, I'll have to try that. I have been just eating it with a spoon.

It was just one of those things I thought I'd try. People spread cheez-whiz on celery all the time, so I figured, I'd give liverwurst a shot. I just used a teaspoon, scooped up some liverwurst, and spread it into the groove of the celery. I've been out of veggies for a few days, and was tired of eating just protein and fat, and wanted a quick healthy meal. The crunchiness of the celery makes a nice contrast to the smooth liverwurst. LOL

Â 

If you do try it, I hope you'll like it.

### diane — 2012-12-19T23:18:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-68037>

I found foie gras in the grocery store near me (picked the one that didn't have added sugar, etc), and I spread it both on carrot sticks and bratwurst. Both were delicious, and was dinner for a few nights!

### furface — 2012-12-19T23:27:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-68038>

It's great to try and come up with interesting ideas for quick, healthy, meals, that are not only healthy, but also tasty, fill you up, but don't make you feel as if you've overeaten. You can even make extra, and keep them in the fridge for later.

### vkiernan — 2012-12-20T14:42:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-68073>

@ furfaceso here comes the original version of sauerkraut from a real "Kraut"This is how my great grandmother, grandmother and mother and now me made it:Add water and pork ribsÂ  to the Sauerkraut and cook it until it is soft (takes a while). when it is it done, cut onions in small pieces and sautee them until they are soft and sweet in pig fat or ghee. Then add locust bean gum to it to make it thick or any other thickener you prefer and add it so the sauerkraut. The sauerkraut then becomes sort of creamy. If you want and if it is allowed, add some white wine, champagne or Marsala and cook, until the alcohol is gone. You can also cook some juniper berries with it. Gives it a nice tang.It does not taste sour anymore at all. My mother used to add a little sugar, I add stevia but you don't really need it. It is fantastic with the ribs or also pigs feet. its phantastic.

If you cook it, then you have just destroyed all the wonderful things it holds for your gut.Â  I would cook with some but def. eat a lot raw and fermented.

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-20T20:25:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-68083>

I just made liverwurst with deer liver and pork fat 50/50 with a lot of spice. The basic process is:

Google liver recipes for spice. I did traditional german spice mix.

Get a meat grinder

Slice liver and fat to size that fits into your grinder

Freeze liver and fat

Run frozen liver and fat through grinder, mix ground liver and fat with spice and cure(nitrate) by hand. Refreeze to stiffen. Run all through grinder 1 or 2 times until you like the consistency. I baked this in wide mouth pint ball jars in the crockpot on low with a thermometer until center is 160 deg. When cooked you want to cool it soon so the fat does not separate. I eat it with a spoon every morning with BAB. This stuff is a choline fatfest and it tastes good.

The best commercial liverwurst I have found in NY is Boar's Head Brand. It's the only one I have found that isn't full of ridiculous additives like sugar, milk and gluten

### furface — 2012-12-20T20:58:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-68086>

> **vkiernan said**
> @ furfaceso here comes the original version of sauerkraut from a real "Kraut"This is how my great grandmother, grandmother and mother and now me made it:Add water and pork ribsÂ  to the Sauerkraut and cook it until it is soft (takes a while). when it is it done, cut onions in small pieces and sautee them until they are soft and sweet in pig fat or ghee. Then add locust bean gum to it to make it thick or any other thickener you prefer and add it so the sauerkraut. The sauerkraut then becomes sort of creamy. If you want and if it is allowed, add some white wine, champagne or Marsala and cook, until the alcohol is gone. You can also cook some juniper berries with it. Gives it a nice tang.It does not taste sour anymore at all. My mother used to add a little sugar, I add stevia but you don't really need it. It is fantastic with the ribs or also pigs feet. its phantastic.
>
> If you cook it, then you have just destroyed all the wonderful things it holds for your gut.Â  I would cook with some but def. eat a lot raw and fermented.

Sounds awesome, vkiernan! I just may have to try that. Thanks for the recipe!

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-22T15:21:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-68197>

I've always hated Sauerkraut as well. Too bitter. But when I started reading about how good it is for you, I started eating the Bubbie's brand. I still don't love it, but I eat it with my seafood as in, on the same forkful. So it sort of "cuts down" the bitter taste. I also drink the Bubbie's Sauerkraut water and the pickle water as well, as I figure there's a lot of good stuff probably going down the drain.

I've learned that I don't have to absolutely love every single thing I eat. Dr. K. has said to stop using food as entertainment, which really hit me. So while I still don't eat certain things that I find extremely distasteful, if something like sauerkraut is so good for you and it's, say, a 4 out of 10 for me on the taste scale, well, then, I'll eat it. Almost every day.

### furface — 2012-12-22T15:28:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-68199>

That's a great attitude to have, Pam! I'm usually a fast eater, so maybe I'll just shovel the sauerkraut into my big yahoo, and wolf it down. Or sprinkle some cinnamon and stevia on it. LOL

At times I still like to enjoy, and savour my food...I don't drink, don't smoke, don't party so having a nice tasty meal is the only "fun" I let myself indulge in. No one says you can't enjoy it, and still eat healthy at the same time. haha.Â  I figure if you enjoy it, you might actually eat it more often...esp if it's healthy for you.

So while I still don't eat certain things that I find extremely distasteful, if something like sauerkraut is so good for you and it's, say, a 4 out of 10 for me on the taste scale, well, then, I'll eat it. I always hated it, too. We started eating it after learning about fermented foods and I could tolerate it with pork, even beef, just as long as it was some kind of meat to cut the sourness. I grew to almost, just maybe, like it.

Then I found Hengstenberg.

It's a lot milder and has no sugar and other crap. It does have white wine and I need to investigate sulfites - red wine only?

I crave it now. It may be that thru my Paleo eating, my tastes are changing.

I just found where I can order a case, but my ultimate goal is to grow my own cabbage and make my own. I do have an organic farm, after all!

### endless — 2013-01-08T14:47:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-69886>

I wish that the difference between vinegar pickles/kraut and naturally fermented was made more clear in each blog that mentions them. There is such a huge difference! I have had to explain this to a few people who started buying sauerkraut for health reasons....unfortunately they were buying the vinegar/wine type...no beneficial bacteria at all!

I am still working on making a kraut I enjoy...for some reason I find it makes my teeth hurt!

### Charlotte — 2013-01-08T15:31:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-pickles-the-regular-vlasic-type-and-liverwurst-epi-paleo-approved-foods.5212/post-69893>

They have Hengstenberg without white wine also. It is a german company that makes it.

Foie gras. Don't eat that. If it is the stuff you get from France and Poland it is really gros. I do love liverwurst but did you ever see how they make foie gras?
They stuff the geese, which means they put a fat pipe down their throat and that throat is loooong and then they pour mashed food down their throat until their stomachs almost explode. This stuff makes their liver become really fat 3-4 times their normal size. They sometimes also hurt their throats when they are not careful.
You should see the geese after that torture. They gag and get all worked up. Can you imagine the stress? I cannot imagine that that stress level produces healthy livers.
And while I do not mind eating all kinds of meat I do mind torturing animals to get a fatty liver patÃ©.

Sorry if I desillusion you here a bit, but that is really terrible and when I saw that I swore never to eat that again.

### caroline — 2013-01-08T16:08:15+0200

Charlotte - do you have a good recipe for liverwurst? One of my favorite things...

> Also to add some variety. And crunch. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**I was in a "Pickle" Today".** I cleaned out the fridge recently (during 9 weeks I did OMAD carnivore) and tossed out two very old jars of _Bubbies_ pickles and relish. My husband missed the relish today and I thought I'd try and research before I ran out to pick some up. Came to research **_Pickles Jack Kruse_** and found this thread. I started to get hungry for Pickles.

I also went to Bubbies web site to see what I could learn. Poo,no phone number to call.
**So I wrote Bubbies:**

**I need to know about the 'spices'** in both the _Bubbies Kosher Dill Pickles_ and the _Bubbies Kosher Dill Relish_. I am **sensitive to MSG** and **nightshade foods and spices** and my husband cannot handle **MSG (aka flavor enhancers like 'Accent' Monosodium glutamate, also known as sodium glutamate and also celery powder, celery seed, celery leaf powder, celery root powder, or celery salt which trigger him to have bouts of Afib (irregular heartbeat)** MSG does go by some **other names** shown at this web site:
_**Truth in Labeling**_ [https://www.truthinlabeling.org/hiddensources.html](https://www.truthinlabeling.org/hiddensources.html)
I'd like to know exactly what those spices are for these reasons of our health. If they are 'top' secret please just have a manager call me with information and for our assurance. Thank you. ​

Because I was in the 'dog house' for throwing out the relish I did not wait for a reply and ran out and bought _Bubbies Kosher dill pickles_ in **33 oz** **glass jar $7.49** and _Bubbies Kosher dill relish_ **$6.29 in 16 oz glass jar**. By the time I got back home I was hungry for a pickle and ate a small one and it was very hot, spicy and tangy I knew I was toast...when I got back to my computer Bubbies had responded what I already knew.

**Bubbies wrote back:**
Hello Martha,
Thank you very much for writing to Bubbies, we would be delighted to answer your question. Our primary concern here at Bubbies is customer satisfaction while making some of the finest pickled products on the market today.
I can assure you that Bubbies products are all **free of MSG**. However, the spice mix in our Kosher Dill Pickles and Kosher Dill Relish **does contain crushed red chilies, which is a nightshade.**
In addition to being MSG-free we also **use no preservatives and all of our products are gluten, rice, carrot, fennel seed, and celery free and are processed in a gluten-free environment.**
Hopefully, this information will help in your continued enjoyment of our products and make it easier for you to remain a loyal customer.
We wish you the very best in Food and Health!
**Heather Walea**
Katalina Holding Company, Inc.
Bubbies Fine Foods, LLC
**P: 805-947-4622 x104**
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
Bubbies Web Site [https://bubbies.com/](https://bubbies.com/)

I will say I love the taste....and the companies fast pleasant response back...now I'll see how my joints respond.
Nice that they come in glass jars and I also like _Bubbies marketing Tag Line Advice..._.

**Eat my pickles.**​

**Wear clean underwear.**​

**

**Marry a doctor.**​

**

# Thread: Strategies for Christmas???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2012-12-18T17:02:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/post-67854>

Any thoughts on how to survive the Christmas festivities? Â So much food ..... so much alcohol .... so much excess......what to do???

of course I know the simple answer is to take your own stuff. Â What coping strategies do you have?

### Rachelle — 2012-12-18T17:20:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/post-67861>

Â I, finally. made my mind over and decided there is no excuse for carbohydrates or starches in my diet. I went through a period not long ago where I allowed them in and it quickly got out of hand--I suffered for it. I do not have the luxury of thinking Â about holiday food.Â 

I keep looking forward to all the changes I am seeing.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-18T19:35:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/post-67884>

Xmas is no longer a problem for me.......Epi paleo Rx works yr round, no matter the holiday

### freesia — 2012-12-18T23:38:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/post-67909>

I'm just focusing on seafood and pork. Mushrooms too I think, andÂ macadamias...and some coconut in various formsÂ b/c it's summer here. I'm good with that for myself. My immediate family will have berries, figs, cream and chocolate.
Extended family....you don't want to know!! (But I won't be having any of it!).
I'll probably have a glass of wine on Christmas day.
It's easy for me in some ways....I'd been sick so long (decades) and what I eat has been fairly critical for me for most of that time, so people are used to me not partaking in the usual festive junk fest.Â 

Best coping strategy I findÂ is a BAB (!),Â take something special you _can_ have, and a warm response to anything you're offered, but a firm "no, I do better if I don't..."

Good luck. (And don't be hard on yourself!)

### caroline — 2012-12-19T00:55:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/post-67915>

The problem is when you are in someone else's home. Â This year will be really different. Â Last year I just had some turkey and ham and vegetables. Â But this year Â - I don't even want that stuff. Â I want raw meat and sushi - period!! Â  Â I guess I will have to settle for some turkey but I am not happy about it! Â  Â I usually stay over as well - now that just multiplies my problem. Â My gorgeous friend has now figured out that at least she can make me scrambled eggs and I am good. Â But now - I have stopped eating eggs - and feel much better Â ... so what to do......

### caroline — 2012-12-19T00:56:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/post-67916>

The funny thing is - I used to compromise ....but now I am not willing to do that. A lot of things have changed lately.

### vkiernan — 2012-12-19T08:44:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/post-67932>

If they are your true friend then there shouldn't be a problem.Â  You can bring your own food.Â  Explain again how you have had to really tighten things up further and how great your are feeling and don't want to blow in 1 day.Â  Remind them of that -Â Yes, you can blow it in 1 day!Â  I have a Christmas dinner to go to that evening and she told me she was serving pulled pork which means pork (off my list) and a lot of sugar (off my list). I asked her if she would mind if I brought a bison burger to fry up and she said she could make BBQ's insteadÂ (see it is really hard for them to understand), ok, no pork - beef but still lots of sugar.Â  I told her thanks but make whatever you like and I will bring my burger.Â  Not really sure what I will take yet but the point is, I will take what I need and make it work.Â  We can't keep putting our dreams on hold just cause someone else doesn't get it.Â  We have to help them get it.Â  They are not going to be out of our lives anytime soon (hopefully) and we might as well train them so everyone can be happy.Â  It is about the event not the food.Â  My sis-in-law actually did say at the end of the conversation, Yay, You can come!!!!Â  So she remembered too it is about family and friends and not what one can eat or not eat!

### Rachelle — 2012-12-19T09:33:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/post-67939>

> **caroline said**
> The funny thing is - I used to compromise ....but now I am not willing to do that. A lot of things have changed lately.

Stick to your guns, Caroline. In the long run it's about your health and well being and not what others do with the choices they make. It's gonna seem awkward for sure, if you are like me and not use to having your mind made over. I really would suggest just taking your own food. Who knows what you are doing may rub off on your good friend. I wish you the best for the holidays.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-19T10:40:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/post-67946>

I bring my own food.

### Inger — 2012-12-19T13:50:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/post-67966>

> I bring my own food.

That is what I do too, since years.
I aways tell, do not worry about me, I'll take my own.. (it is too complicated anyways..lol I am sooo picky) Sometimes I make something that I can eat too, for sharing. Everyone loves my salmon tartar, or my graved lachs. Or my marinated herrings. Or my dark chocolate covered, homemade marzipan confettis with only a touch wild raw honey. I can eat a few too without much ill effect.
I never had an issue so far with eating my weird foods on parties. It really is not all about foods.. it is about love. They all feel, I love them. I listen. I care. I am there for them 100%. That is what counts!

### Charlotte — 2012-12-19T17:26:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/post-67978>

I know exactly how you feel Caroline. My children are not coming home this Christmas, so I am staying with some friends. I just told them what I can eat and what not and so we worked out a plan. I bring my own food and something to share with them which is really good. Then I make a cake they can eat but not me. I have not eaten eggs in 4 months because I am highly sensitive (IgG4) and I have been longing to eat eggs so much. I dream about an omlette. Now I complained to my doctor about how hard I find this and he recommended me to eat quail eggs. I checked in the internet and found an organic farmer who ships these eggs like on a regular basis to his customers. Now I am so happy. So now I can have my BAB with bacon and eggs. This is true Christmas for me. I have now lost 25 kg (55 pounds) in the last 4 months and want to lose another 50 pounds within the next 4 months. So I am clear on my goal and no-one (except myself) can lead me astray, not even an invitation for Christmas dinner. If I can do it and I have a history of 60 years of obesity (I was already fat when I was a baby) you can do it too. People understand and if they don't then it is their problem, not yours. I know that we do not want to be rude, but I rather be rude than put my health and wellbeing in jeopardy. I am 60 years old now and I feel I have no more time to waste to make the wrong choices out of the wrong reasons. And I know from past experiences (more than I wish to remember) how it weakens my resolve when I give in to a moment of neglect of my goal. So think of you when you go there and how much of a delight you are to others when you are happy with yourself.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strategies-for-christmas.5213/post-68003>

I was reading that a substance called Haplex increases absorption of oral Hyaluronic acid.Â  Haplex is a sonicated combination of HA (hyaluronic acid) and phospholipids.Â  If iodine is absorbed via the selective power of leaky gut I wondered if combining iodine with liquid HA would increase bioavailability of HA.Â  Any thoughts?

Â 

I don't have a sonic machine so I was brainstorming alternate ways of absorbing HA.

Functional medicine provides a powerful new operating system and systems-based clinical model to replace the outdated and ineffective acute-care models carried forward from the 20th century.
