# Thread: Exercise in LR or slight LS, when is it ok?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-in-lr-or-slight-ls-when-is-it-ok.5218/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lauren_r — 2012-12-19T03:48:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-in-lr-or-slight-ls-when-is-it-ok.5218/post-67921>

I was wondering about training and exercise in LR shifting to LS.
I am 162cm (5'3) and around 52kg (114lbs). Although I am small I have body fat that I have been unable to shift, and could loose 4-5kgs of fat with an increase in mass without any issue (used to weigh 48kgs).
I have eaten primal for 2 years, dairy free a year, allergy-food (tested) free for 4 months.
Also have leaky gut, eczema, IgG food intolerances.

I do have low cortisol in AM and high in PM, therefore classified as "LR", however I have had big changes in hair and nails in last 4 months, and find that I am able to get up in the am without too much trouble.

Is it possible to be between LR and LS?
Is it possible that training is ok at this point?

I have begun the LEptin Rx anyway, which is not much of a change for me, less salad and less steamed veg.

I actually find training (heavy lifting or HIIT) in the morning gives me a lift, it also works wonders on my mood and view of life/world etc! Training at night obviously with the cortisol is not good for me.
And when I don't train I get pretty flat and sad.

The Leptin Rx requires no training and I am concerned about the effect this will have on my mood, outlook on life etc, but don't want to be using up my mitochondria too early!!!

Does any one have any thoughts or experience being at this point?
Thank you for your time and energy and any response.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-19T10:49:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-in-lr-or-slight-ls-when-is-it-ok.5218/post-67947>

great question and it will be fully answered in the Dec Webinar

# Thread: Has anyone ever thought to do some sort of pledge drive for charity doing CT during a "polar bear" s
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-ever-thought-to-do-some-sort-of-pledge-drive-for-charity-doing-ct-during-a-polar-bear-s.5219/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-19T05:34:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-ever-thought-to-do-some-sort-of-pledge-drive-for-charity-doing-ct-during-a-polar-bear-s.5219/post-67924>

I was thinking that it might be a great way to raise money for a charity, esp one that isn't doesn't already have people donating up the wazoo, like Cancer does. (I myself think that they do have a cure, but they'll never release it because they'd stop getting all the donations in, and the economy would just plummet worse than the depression.)

Something like Multiple Sclerosis, or Cystic Fibrosis, or even Diabetes Research etc.

What someone who is extremely good at lengthy CT sessions could do, is take pledges for each minute they stay in the water of a "polar bear" swim. If someone pledges $5 per minute, and the person, like for instance, Dr. Kruse stays in for one hour, that's $300 from that one pledge alone. If 100 people were to sign up for the same amount, that would be $30,000! etc.

I just thought it might be a novel idea since this is the time of year...esp New Year's Day, that a lot of places tend to have Polar Bear swims.

I think it would look so comical too...in a good way. You always see news footage of people running into the cold water, and quickly running back out, but then if we'd see someone who is adapted to CT, just non chalantly stroll out into the water as if it's 80Â°, and begin to frolic in the water, would be hilarious! And all for a good cause.

Just a thought.

That's how the polar plunges are organized in Vermont...the Special Olympics gets "plungers" to raise pledge money. I think they only expect a brief dip...I like your idea about endurance=more money! ### Lyndra — 2012-12-19T13:07:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-ever-thought-to-do-some-sort-of-pledge-drive-for-charity-doing-ct-during-a-polar-bear-s.5219/post-67962>

I used to do that here on January 1 as part of the polar bear plunge. A few years ago, the event was taken over by a non-profit that does not allow anyone to use the event to raise funds for another cause.Â 

Â 

At the event here, they do not let people stay in for very long. First, there are 400+ people waiting to get their turn and only 5-10 jump at once so it takes quite a while to get through everybody. Second, they don't want the liability that someone could get hurt by staying in too long.

Â 

One benefit of the organized event is they have trained divers in the water ready to rescue anyone who has trouble. If you do it on your own, be aware of your limits and don't get trapped under the ice (that's my biggest fear). And have fun!Â

# Thread: Started BHRT Today....Here we go...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-bhrt-today-here-we-go.5224/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2012-12-19T11:34:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-bhrt-today-here-we-go.5224/post-67952>

Got my prescriptions and started BHRT last night with my new doc.Â 

Â 

Started with 50,000 IU of Vitamin D3 1x per week for the next 15 weeks.

Topical Estrodiol and Estriol - once in the morning and once at night

100mg Progesterone capsule 1x at bedtime

Â 

For the next few days I am overlapping my BC pills with this regimen per my docs recommendation.Â  Then I will stop BC pills completely.

Multi-Vitamin pack for 2x per day is ordered and on the way

Pro-biotic is ordered and on the way

Â 

Had a few more labs drawn yesterday, including Zinc and Magnesium.

Â 

I am on Day 3 of a constant migraine.Â  I am looking forward to some relief in the next few weeks.Â  The headache is starting in the back of my neck behind my ears at the trigger point - which actually hurts to the touch, as do the muscles in my shoulders.Â  Acupuncture session is scheduled for this afternoon to try to help with that.

Â 

Progesterone is at night for drowsiness, but I actually had insomnia last night - more likely than not it's cause is anxiety from starting a new treatment plan.

I post my progress.

Â 

Karen from Arizona

### mafixit — 2012-12-24T08:27:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-bhrt-today-here-we-go.5224/post-68294>

An update....Â  As the days go on, I feel worse.Â  The pressure in my head has increased and I am more and more dizzy.Â  If I touch my head, it hurts.

Went back on a short course of Dexamethasone given to me by my headache neurologist. I am on day 4 of 6 and still feel no relief - in fact, the dizziness is worse.Â  I am on Day 6 of my hormone therapy with no real help, and Day 4 of being off my birth control pills (which also make me feel worse AFTER I stop taking them - it seriously seems that they keep much of this heavy duty dizziness at bay.).Â  And to top it off, I am on Day 2 of my first period in at least 6 months.

I really wish someone could figure out what the heck is going on here.Â  Everything I do as advised seems to make my symptoms worse.

Had to vent.Â  Thanks for listening.

I will leave messages with both my headache doc's office and my MD hormone doc.

I hope everyone has a fantastic holiday!

Karen from ArizonaÂ

# Thread: A Owl was sitting outside the window.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-owl-was-sitting-outside-the-window.5226/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2012-12-19T15:59:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-owl-was-sitting-outside-the-window.5226/post-67974>

Yes. Today I came home from work.. and my mom says.. what.. isn't it a owl right there outside the window? It was. It sat there in the lighted snow, with its back to the window, at the terrace. It was beautiful.
We thought it was injuried, as it did not moved when we stepped up the stairs to the front door. I was hungry so I ate my dinner fast and ran downstairs to check out the owl. It was still there. I was sure it was injuried and thought it might have been sitting there the whole day in the snow and cold. I took some organic raw minced beef and stepped out on the terrace. I said.. owl, do you want anything to eat? I was thinking about taking it inside and check if I could do anything to help. The owl looked at me and the lifted its wings and flew away.

It left me so amazed. Why was it sitting there? On the terrace? With her back turned to the lights from the window?

I felt so thankful after. It felt special. I never saw a owl so close. And it was an exhausting day at work.

I need to tell one more story. My parents went to a Norwegian Christmas party in the home of a Stainer School owner. For Norwegians in Finland. My mom told me yesterday a lady at the party told her about her mom that grew up and lived in Norway It was about 100 years ago.. in a town my parents know so well. Her mom, a young girl by then,Â  was sitting at a stone close to a forest, outside in the wild nature, knitting. Then she all of a sudden saw a bear. She thought.. OMG.. I will just sit here and knit, all will be good. The bear came closer and closer. It come so close it put its paw on her knee. She looked at it and it had an wood splinter in it! She was able to remove it. The bear went away. It came back with a dead animal it laid to her feet.. and then.. went into the forest again.

This is a real story. I was just amazed when I heard it.To talk to people that live in the wild gives one amazing insights. They have natural insticts how to behave with wild animals and cope with them. I think it is so beautiful. This is sadly lost to the most part in todays world.

I am addicted to this Christmas song, from Carola HÃ¤ggkvist a Swedish artist. I find it so beautiful. I am maybe thinking about totally different stuff as the song was ment when listening to it..lol.. but I always do like that when I like songs. I give them my own meaning.

Â 

\[video=youtube\_share;xtTX\_QmRTiw\]http://youtu.be/xtTX\_QmRTiw\[/video\]

### caroline — 2012-12-19T21:36:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-owl-was-sitting-outside-the-window.5226/post-68016>

how beautiful Inger - I am totally rapt by this - such important and amazing words to live by all year. It is like the universe is opening up and we can see all the possibilities. Â It couldn't be more beautiful. Â Thankyou Inger for being you. Â xoÂ

### ashryn — 2013-02-11T11:15:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vap-nmr-test-in-australia-where-who-help-please.5228/post-75373>

You can get the vap elements done here mostly, but the pathologist I went to said I might get a bill for the apolipoprotein part of it
i suspect there is someone who does a full lipid profile here, but they have a different name for it..

i there's what to get your dr to order:

Total VLDL: Elevated VLDL levels correspond to an increased risk of heart disease and diabetes.

Sum Total Cholesterol: The sum of HDL, LDL and VLDL levels.

Total non-HDL: The sum of only LDL and VLDL levels; a higher value indicates a greater risk for developing heart disease.

Total apoB100: Apolipoprotein B100 helps create, carry and deliver "bad cholesterol" to cells; measuring apoB100 levels aids in determining the type and/or cause of high cholesterol.

Lp(a) cholesterol: Research suggests that Lp(a), which is similar to LDL, is an inherited risk factor for atherosclerosis.

IDL: A lipoprotein of intermediate density; according to Atherotech, this number is elevated among individuals with a family history of diabetes.

LDL-RC: LDL that is bound to C-reactive protein; this type of LDL is found at the site of atherosclerotic plaques in the body, which are one of the key features of artery disease and -- when they rupture -- the primary initiator of heart attacks.

Sum Total LDL-C: The sum of Lp(a), IDL and LDL.

LDL Size Pattern: Reported as one of three categories -- A, A/B or B. In pattern A, the LDL molecules are larger and less dense, making them easier for the body to remove. In pattern A/B, there is a combination of light and dense molecules. In pattern B, smaller, high-density molecules predominate. According to Atherotech, patients with small, dense LDL particles (pattern B) have a four-fold greater risk of developing heart disease than patients with LDL size pattern A.

HDL-2: A subclass of "good cholesterol" that is particularly protective against heart disease. A low number here could mean an increased risk of coronary artery disease, even in those with otherwise normal cholesterol levels.

HDL-3: Another subclass of HDL, which does not protect against coronary artery disease to the same degree as HDL-2.

VLDL-3: A triglyceride-rich very low-density lipoprotein; some studies suggest a potential correlation between higher VLDL-3 numbers and the development of diabetes.

In addition, the VAP test provides individuals with a better idea of their vulnerability to the metabolic syndrome, a combination of factors that significantly elevate the risk an individual will develop diabetes or cardiovascular disease.

Interpret results [http://www.livestrong.com/article/38446-interpret-vap-cholesterol-profile/](http://www.livestrong.com/article/38446-interpret-vap-cholesterol-profile/)

hope this helps someone..

### mouk — 2013-02-15T23:03:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vap-nmr-test-in-australia-where-who-help-please.5228/post-76014>

Advanced lipid testing ( NMR, VAP , ..) remains a continental US based technology.

The samples have to be managed ( clotting and centrifugation) by a local lab before reaching the lab settings in 24 hours on ice but not frozen.

[VAP specimen collection - Atherotech](<http://VAP specimen collection - Atherotech>)

NMR - Liposcience
[Preparation and shipment](<http://Preparation and shipment>)

### Rayken — 2013-04-08T02:41:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vap-nmr-test-in-australia-where-who-help-please.5228/post-83313>

I just found this forum today,
You can get a VAP blood test from www,atherotech.com. and get all the history on VAP & LP(a)
From, Patented Cure For Heart Disease, By David H Leake. & Practicing Medicine Without a License,
By Owen Fonorow, these books also have information on lots of good website
Rayken

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-20T09:27:56+0200

Â The adventure and value in life is in the jumping off the cliff and using your skill to repel down safely to help you and others. Try not to become a person of success only. Rather, become a person of value. We increase our personal value when we "add value" to others. It is the greatest Xmas gift you can give to people in your life and in your network. How do you do it? Teach them a skill they do not have, help them accomplish a goal, challenge them to be their best, be supportive, lend them your support, listen attentively and show them you care, respect their unique abilities, and share a few laughs with them. The major value in life is not what we get. The major value in life is what we become. Today, begin to work harder on yourself than you do on your job. It is the best investment plan available to you right now........and the best Xmas gift you can give anyone.

Karen from Arizona

### freshveggies — 2012-12-19T19:50:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerance-tests-are-back-no-coconut-or-olive-oil-now-what.5230/post-68002>

### nonchalant — 2012-12-19T21:13:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerance-tests-are-back-no-coconut-or-olive-oil-now-what.5230/post-68012>

Maxfit, since it is Winter for you, it's a good time for animal fats anyway.Â  Perhaps you can use this time to heal your gut with bone broths and such, and by the time next Summer rolls around, perhaps you will be able to tolerate coconut oil and olive oil.

Personally, I don't really care for coconut oil (don't tell anybody please!) and was glad to leave it behind this Autumn.Â  (But I love coconut meat-I swear!)Â  I seldom eat olive oil either, and I don't usually cook with it.Â  Clarified butter or ghee is great to cook with; bacon fat is king; beef or pork fat can be rendered into tallow or lard.

HTH.

this is awesome!

I pumped for Chaos for 14mths b/c she couldn't nurse. I wish I could have pumped longer for her. w/Havoc - nursing was so successful, that when I pumped milk for him while at work - I made so much extra - that I was able to supply a local mom w/IGT with emb for her little guy! so happy that he got not only my milk, but other mom's milk too....

its quantum deep! not to mention it will be more valuable that oil at some point

### SCRN2007 — 2013-03-13T15:31:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-proof-of-brain-gut-series-what-you-eat-is-extremely-important.5233/post-80346>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=80339)
>
> this is awesome!
>
> I pumped for Chaos for 14mths b/c she couldn't nurse. I wish I could have pumped longer for her. w/Havoc - nursing was so successful, that when I pumped milk for him while at work - I made so much extra - that I was able to supply a local mom w/IGT with emb for her little guy! so happy that he got not only my milk, but other mom's milk too....

That's great, Gretchen! I pumped for 8 months after every nursing session because my daughter was not a great breastfeeder either. However, thanks to hypothyroid, PCOS, and probably also my then-belief that organic whole grains were healthy for me, I had to take a max dose of domperidone just to make 22 oz per day. Without the domperidone I only made a couple oz per day. At 8 months my milk dried up even with the domperidone--I think it was because I was exhausted from going back to work. I have no doubt that my not having a full supply to feed my daughter, until she was of a proper age to be weaned, paved the way for what she suffered at 18 months . . . especially since she turned out to have agammaglobulinemia. I am so happy to hear that you were able to supply your little guy and help another mom too!

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-13T15:44:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-proof-of-brain-gut-series-what-you-eat-is-extremely-important.5233/post-80349>

> [SCRN2007 said:](/goto/post?id=80346)
>
> That's great, Gretchen! I pumped for 8 months after every nursing session because my daughter was not a great breastfeeder either. However, thanks to hypothyroid, PCOS, and probably also my then-belief that organic whole grains were healthy for me, I had to take a max dose of domperidone just to make 22 oz per day. Without the domperidone I only made a couple oz per day. At 8 months my milk dried up even with the domperidone--I think it was because I was exhausted from going back to work. I have no doubt that my not having a full supply to feed my daughter, until she was of a proper age to be weaned, paved the way for what she suffered at 18 months . . . especially since she turned out to have agammaglobulinemia. I am so happy to hear that you were able to supply your little guy and help another mom too!

Domperidone was my friend - Its the only way I was able to pump for 14mths. when I stopped taking it - I dried up immediately. I took it for 2 yrs while nursing havoc. I've been off of it for almost a year now - and he's still nursing. occasionally Chaos will ask to nurse - its funny b/c Havoc shows her how, and tells her what she needs to do... its very sweet!

### SCRN2007 — 2013-03-13T16:05:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-proof-of-brain-gut-series-what-you-eat-is-extremely-important.5233/post-80350>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=80342)
>
> [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/art...-sodas-decade-reign-No-1-beverage-choice.html](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2292328/WATER-claims-spot-Americans-favorite-drink--ending-sodas-decade-reign-No-1-beverage-choice.html)
>
> There is a deep reason this is going on.........

All this talk about water has me wondering if part of the reason I couldn't make breastmilk was because I was dehydrated at a cellular level. I do remember that, despite drinking water at room temperature my whole life, I craved water so cold that, literally, only freshly melted ice could satisfy me when I was pregnant and nursing. Now that I have read about the structure of that type of water it seems as though I should have stuck with it. I also couldn't look at chicken and eggs without wanting to hurl--and now I have learned that "fowl is foul" and that folks with Hashi's have no business eating eggs. Seems like actually having hormones for a time (I was on Prometrium when pregnant due to my PCOS) gave me intuition that I lost when I lost the hormones.

### SCRN2007 — 2013-03-13T16:12:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-proof-of-brain-gut-series-what-you-eat-is-extremely-important.5233/post-80351>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=80349)
>
> Domperidone was my friend - Its the only way I was able to pump for 14mths. when I stopped taking it - I dried up immediately. I took it for 2 yrs while nursing havoc. I've been off of it for almost a year now - and he's still nursing. occasionally Chaos will ask to nurse - its funny b/c Havoc shows her how, and tells her what she needs to do... its very sweet!

So glad to hear that you are doing well with breastfeeding off of domperidone! That is great. I often wonder if it didn't do bad things to my brain. I know it is not supposed to cross the BBB, but, given what Dr. Kruse has said about adults with AI having compromised BBBs, my having been on a high dose, it having made me very sleepy, and my having turned out to be homozygous for both COMT V158M and COMT H62H (which affect dopamine recycling), I think there is a good chance it could have messed with my dopamine in some sort of a lasting way . . . hopefully not permanently.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-13T16:20:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-proof-of-brain-gut-series-what-you-eat-is-extremely-important.5233/post-80352>

> [SCRN2007 said:](/goto/post?id=80351)
>
> So glad to hear that you are doing well with breastfeeding off of domperidone! That is great. I often wonder if it didn't do bad things to my brain. I know it is not supposed to cross the BBB, but, given what Dr. Kruse has said about adults with AI having compromised BBBs, my having been on a high dose, it having made me very sleepy, and my having turned out to be homozygous for both COMT V158M and COMT H62H (which affect dopamine recycling), I think there is a good chance it could have messed with my dopamine in some sort of a lasting way . . . hopefully not permanently.

yeah - I hear you about its impact on dopamine among other things... but considering that my Prolactin levels, even while lactating -are below 4ng/mL, when the range is 4-15.2 ng/mL for non -lactating women... it all goes back to the disruption of the pharmacy in my brain....

### SCRN2007 — 2013-03-13T16:38:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-proof-of-brain-gut-series-what-you-eat-is-extremely-important.5233/post-80353>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=80352)
>
> yeah - I hear you about its impact on dopamine among other things... but considering that my Prolactin levels, even while lactating -are below 4ng/mL, when the range is 4-15.2 ng/mL for non -lactating women... it all goes back to the disruption of the pharmacy in my brain....

I never actually got my prolactin levels tested. If I had a do-over I would. Now, in our NICU, lactation consultants are urging moms with supply issues to get prolactin levles; however, they are over a barrel because as nurses we put our licenses in danger if we suggest something outside the standard of care like domperidone--even though it is technically OTC, we have been warned. If the moms are lucky they will get a two-week course of Reglan (which, last I heard, is known to cross the BBB) from their OB if their prolactin is low. If that doesn't work they are on their own with just Fenugreek, but lots of OBs warn moms who have diabetes not to take the Fenugreek. My OB-GYN, who has PCOS herself, told me that she could not officially condone domperidone since it was not the standard of care, but then she said, "that stuff made me really sleepy, but I couldn't make milk without it." I wonder if she would have felt comfortable saying that last part if I were not a nurse. Sad world.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-13T20:05:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-proof-of-brain-gut-series-what-you-eat-is-extremely-important.5233/post-80394>

Now, in our NICU, lactation consultants are urging moms with supply issues to get prolactin levles; however, they are over a barrel because as nurses we put our licenses in danger if we suggest something outside the standard of care like domperidone--even though it is technically OTC, we have been warned. If the moms are lucky they will get a two-week course of Reglan (which, last I heard, is known to cross the BBB) from their OB if their prolactin is low. If that doesn't work they are on their own with just Fenugreek, but lots of OBs warn moms who have diabetes not to take the Fenugreek. My OB-GYN, who has PCOS herself, told me that she could not officially condone domperidone since it was not the standard of care, but then she said, "that stuff made me really sleepy, but I couldn't make milk without it." I wonder if she would have felt comfortable saying that last part if I were not a nurse. Sad world.

you should know there is an underground supply chain for dom. some mw will rx it to a compounding pharmacy... I've even known women who've taken in Dr. Newman's and Dr. Hale's research in on it, and gotten it prescribed it is possible if you arm your self with knowledge... but not everyone is ready, and willing to do that...

### SCRN2007 — 2013-03-13T20:56:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-proof-of-brain-gut-series-what-you-eat-is-extremely-important.5233/post-80402>

Yes, I know exactly where to get it on the internet--that is where I got it, because no one around here will order it to be compounded. The docs consider it a risk to their licensure. The compounding pharmacies I called around here had never even heard of it. The lactation consultants know about it, but, like I said, they do not talk about it because they consider it a threat to their RN licenses and their lactation certifications to talk about it.

Interesting - the IBCLC's I've worked work all talked openly about it. Maybe is location dependent. of course if we're all running on a "Bad Clock" then there's probably a larger need for using Dom to support Lactation... there are times I consider trying to figure out how to become an IBCLC - while still doing my day job. This is something I'd be really good at and have a passion for.... not sure how I'd swing it though....

I have considered it too, since I have all of the clinical hours supporting moms in breastfeeding already, but I figure I would just get myself into trouble with my mouth. It is very hard as it is for me to "know better" and work in conventional medicine. Cognitive dissonance is a daily part of my job now:-( At the BSN-level, nurses really have to keep their mouths shut most of the time in order to stay out of trouble.

# Thread: Found a tasty way to prepare oysters.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-a-tasty-way-to-prepare-oysters.5238/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-20T22:51:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-a-tasty-way-to-prepare-oysters.5238/post-68099>

I bought some fresh oysters today, and planned to have them for dinner. I spritzed some fresh lime juice onto one, tried to eat it raw, but had to spit it out. NO FREAKIN' WAY!! But instead of giving up, I decided to try and heat them up a bit.

So I put a little bit of kerrygold butter into a pan, sprinkled some garlic powder in, mixed it, added the oysters, a few squirts of fresh lime juice, and a few drops of Chinese rooster chili sauce. Heated them for about 5-7 minutes, and they were actually pretty good.

Maybe not the best way to eat them, but it's better than not eating them at all.

On a separate note, I found a butcher shop in Vancouver that not only sells Grass Fed & Finished beef, but also GF Beef Tallow, soup bones, Bison, GF Lamb, and Double smoked Chilliwack Farmer's bacon.

It's Market Meats 2200 block West 4th Ave in Vancouver.

### diane — 2012-12-20T23:00:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-a-tasty-way-to-prepare-oysters.5238/post-68100>

I've never eaten oysters with lime juice - but lately with some lemon and horseradish. I love them raw - I have never cooked them. Yum!

### furface — 2012-12-20T23:12:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-a-tasty-way-to-prepare-oysters.5238/post-68102>

Hey as long as we eat them, to me, that's all that counts.

Next time I'll try them with lemon juice...cooked of course. Do not like horseradish.

### Rachelle — 2012-12-21T17:34:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-a-tasty-way-to-prepare-oysters.5238/post-68144>

Hi Furface,

I don't eat them, I swollow them.Â _My ritual is to shuck them, smell them and if they smell nice as the sea, spoon a little mignonnette on them and swollow. No eating.

**Â**_

### Awainer1 — 2012-12-21T20:33:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-a-tasty-way-to-prepare-oysters.5238/post-68152>

Lately I shuck them, put them in a jar, and then just eat them by the fork full.Â  Takes too long to shuck and eat one by one.Â  I swear it is like natures Viagra ha.

### furface — 2012-12-21T20:55:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-a-tasty-way-to-prepare-oysters.5238/post-68153>

Hey, however we get them down...as long as we get them down. # Thread: This might actually be working!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-might-actually-be-working.5242/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-21T21:06:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-might-actually-be-working.5242/post-68154>

I just received more proof of how this whole LeptinRx, Epi-Paleo, CT shebang thing actually does work!

Not only have I lost 30 lbs since I began on Dec 3rd, have far more energy than I've had even when I was 30 years younger, but for the first time since I was diagnosed with having type 2 diabetes over 10 years ago, my Hemoglobin A1c (Blood Glucose) level is within NORMAL RANGE!!

The "normal" range is between 86.5 - 111.7 and on Nov. 6th, mine was 142.3!! But yesterday it was down to 111.7 !! Right on the maximum border, but still, it's not OVER!

I feel great!

### caroline — 2012-12-21T22:28:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-might-actually-be-working.5242/post-68156>

Nope - Â  Â it't not over .......it's just the beginning!! Â fantastic results! Â I am still proud of you no matter how stubborn you are { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### furface — 2012-12-21T23:31:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-might-actually-be-working.5242/post-68160>

Thanks so very much, Caroline, and Dr. Kruse. So very much appreciated.

This is just the start, and I feel so much momentum. I am not going to sabotage myself, or let anyone else knock me down.

I am proud of myself, but this is only the beginning. No resting on any laurels!

### caroline — 2012-12-22T01:17:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-might-actually-be-working.5242/post-68163>

>
> This is just the start, and I feel so much momentum. I am not going to sabotage myself, or let anyone else knock me down.

That is what I meant when I said ....I might have an ice cream cone - or I might not. Â if I want one ....I can have one...but I am not going to let anything \[or anyone\] sabotage my efforts. Â  Â It is my call and mine alone.......no guilt or shame involved! Â  Â  Just remember.......don't shoot the messenger LOL

### furface — 2012-12-22T01:47:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-might-actually-be-working.5242/post-68164>

Hey no worries about that! You are so right, it is each individual's own call, and decision. It's up to each individual whether or not they are successful...or if they fail.

What I find to be the best part of all this, is the snowball effect. Once you start to feel good, even just a bit, it carries onto other parts of your life, and you can't wait to do more.

Even though the bottom of my feet have been killing me for a couple of days from all the walking I've had to do for my Christmas shopping, and it prevented me from having a longer CT session last night than I would have liked, I was determined NOT to let it stop me tonight, and it didn't!

Between the good news on my bloodwork, the congrats and support from you, and Dr. Kruse, and the support, encouragement, congrats, and inspiration of someone very special to me, I managed to stand and sing through endless Christmas Carols for one hour and three minutes!

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-22T09:21:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-might-actually-be-working.5242/post-68170>

Dude, you are kicking ass. You are so right about the snowball effect of feeling good. That became my main motivation. You're working it, but in about six months you are not going to believe it. Power on, furface.

### furface — 2012-12-22T11:00:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-might-actually-be-working.5242/post-68173>

> **Dali Dula said**
> Dude, you are kicking ass. You are so right about the snowball effect of feeling good. That became my main motivation. You're working it, but in about six months you are not going to believe it. Power on, furface.

Thanks, Dali, so very much appreciated! You're so right about it being such great motivation. It makes it so much easier as well to stick with it.

You keep powering on yourself, Dali!

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-22T11:29:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-might-actually-be-working.5242/post-68175>

Thanks so much for posting your progress! You are doing awesome.

For me, too, the motivation is feeling good. When I eat & live like this, I just feel better. I just....smile more, laugh more, have a better outlook.

Oh, and the sleep is awesome. Not waking up in the middle of the night for several hours, tossing and turning, but rather sleeping the entire night, 8 straight hours....wow. These days, the only time I wake up in the middle of the night is because my arm is completely asleep, because I've been in such a restful position for 4-5 hours, not moving at all. So I awake, shake out my arm, turn over, and back for 4 more hours.

The longer you keep at this, the better you will feel, the better your labs will look, and the better you will look. I'm convinced that this is the pot of gold and am extremely frustrated at the same time when we read so much conflicting advice out there, as this to me is the truth.

### diane — 2012-12-22T12:13:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-might-actually-be-working.5242/post-68181>

That's amazing furface! I love your energy and spirit - and your willingness to jump in, ask questions and then just do it. Keep it up!

### furface — 2012-12-22T14:45:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-might-actually-be-working.5242/post-68193>

Thanks so much everyone for all your words of congrats, and encouragement! It means so much! Continue on as well with each of your excellent journeys!

@ Primal Pam: You are bang on about the sleep as well. I was diagnosed with severe sleep apnea in September, and I was put on a CPAP machine, which has worked wonders. That, and combined with this Leptin Rx has made sleeping a dream! As you said, no waking, or getting up in the middle of the night. No tossing and turning, and getting 8 or more hours of straight sleep each night feels amazing! Also I can't agree more about your statement that there is just so much conflicting information out there. At times I think there's TOO MUCH, and just deciding what to believe is a major cause of stress etc.

@Diane: LOL, thanks, Diane. I'm usually a happy-go-lucky person as it is, but at times stress can really pay a toll on one's mood, and spirit. Hey if you don't ask questions, you'll never learn.

### janagram — 2012-12-22T14:48:01+0200

You are an inspiration!

### furface — 2012-12-22T15:23:31+0200

Thanks, but all of you are an inspiration to everyone!

# Thread: Just made my own super simple "Mounds" bar.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-made-my-own-super-simple-mounds-bar.5243/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-21T23:37:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-made-my-own-super-simple-mounds-bar.5243/post-68161>

I bought some coconut manna yesterday, and I was trying to figure out what to put it on, so I thought, why not make a Dark Chocolate/Coconut Manna sandwich.

So I broke off two squares of a Lindt 99 % Dark Chocolate bar, two squares of a Lindt 90% Dark Chocolate bar, and spread some Nutiva Coconut manna in between them, and made a "Mounds" sandwich...but a lot healthier. Very enjoyable. Had it for "desert" after my oysters & Bubbie's pickle lunch.

### vkiernan — 2012-12-22T08:27:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-made-my-own-super-simple-mounds-bar.5243/post-68166>

How did you like the Bubbies pickles?Â  You can also melt the chocolate, manna, co and a little stevia and make bark.Â  Really yummy.Â  I actually like it a LOT w/out the chocolate and a little hazelnut flavored stevia.Â  Got to be careful with that stuff though iykwim! { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### furface — 2012-12-22T11:03:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-made-my-own-super-simple-mounds-bar.5243/post-68174>

The pickles are great! I wish they'd be a little crunchier in the middle, but it's definitely not a deal breaker. Just the right size as well lol.

I might try that bark recipe...sounds intriguing. (But I'd keep the chocolate since Dark Chocolate is so healthy for you haha.)

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-22T11:46:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-made-my-own-super-simple-mounds-bar.5243/post-68178>

That sounds so delish!

My "lunch" for about the past year that I bring with me to work every day is 2 squares of very dark chocolate and about 2 tbsp. of CO manna plus one Brazil Nut. Sometimes I use Coconut Cream, which can be found at Whole Foods in little boxes. I'll take a couple of tbsp. of that instead of the manna, as sometimes the manna is harder to find.

In any event, it's about 1000 times better than a Mounds bar. These days, whenever I think...well, I haven't had any candy in a while, let me try a mini-bar, it tastes bad to me. Like, I can taste the chemicals or something. So for me, it's become not about substituting, but about being the actual food that I eat.

Recently, on a business trip, I brought my little containers with my chocolate squares and CO manne in ziploc baggies. At lunchtime, it's what I pulled out. People looked at me so funny, but I was like, I just don't care anymore. I didn't leave the table bloated, which is the way I always feel at those big meetings.

### furface — 2012-12-22T14:37:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-made-my-own-super-simple-mounds-bar.5243/post-68191>

That is so smart, Pam! Very creative as well. Who gives a f\*\*k what other people think?! As long as you're healthy, happy, and full of energy, that's all that counts.

You are so right about it being so much healthier than a Mounds bar. I just used that comparison because of the main idea of the ingredients. But as you said, without all the chemicals.

It's funny that you mentioned that the mini-bar tasted bad to you...about a week before I began my Leptin Rx, and eating Epi-Paleo, I ordered some McDonald's french fries...which I used to LOVE, but I couldn't even finish them. They just didn't taste good.

### janagram — 2012-12-22T15:32:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-made-my-own-super-simple-mounds-bar.5243/post-68200>

funny thing, furface...McDonald's used to fry the fries in lard...that was their secret! Even Julia Child loved them! But social pressure led them to use some awful vegetable oil...so...they really aren't the same.

### furface — 2012-12-22T15:46:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-made-my-own-super-simple-mounds-bar.5243/post-68202>

> **janagram said**
> funny thing, furface...McDonald's used to fry the fries in lard...that was their secret! Even Julia Child loved them! But social pressure led them to use some awful vegetable oil...so...they really aren't the same.

You're right...they aren't the same. Same with KFC, when I first tried it, it was actually very tasty. The coating was even a bit "salty", but not overly. Now all you taste is grease! Of course I haven't had any for nearly 6 months, and don't plan on ever having any again. Same with the fries!

### caroline — 2012-12-22T16:31:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-made-my-own-super-simple-mounds-bar.5243/post-68205>

you are a changed man .....love it

### furface — 2012-12-22T17:00:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-made-my-own-super-simple-mounds-bar.5243/post-68208>

> **caroline said**
> you are a changed man .....love it

LOL, thanks!

It's funny, even walking by McDonald's, and just getting a whiff of their food used to be so enticing, now it's rather nauseating. It's like you can almost taste the grease through your nostrils...if that makes any sense.

If I want a burger, I'll have a grass-fed one at home along with some bacon as part of my BAB...which is exactly what I did this morning. Just wish the GFGB wasn't extra-lean, but that's all they had at the butcher shop. Oh well, can't have everything. haha

# Thread: Does anyone have a quick, and easy recipe for homemade clam chowder Epi-Paleo/LeptinRx style?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-quick-and-easy-recipe-for-homemade-clam-chowder-epi-paleo-leptinrx-style.5246/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-22T17:03:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-quick-and-easy-recipe-for-homemade-clam-chowder-epi-paleo-leptinrx-style.5246/post-68209>

I'd like to make a homemade clam chowder Epi-Paleo/Leptin Rx version that is simple, and quick to make, and would appreciate it if anyone has any recipes please.

I tried searching on Google, but they all included potatoes...which I never liked in any of my soups...except stew.

I also like my chowder...and stews nice and thick...so thick that a spoon will stand up in it if possible.

Thanks in advance.

### Chrysanthemama — 2012-12-22T19:02:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-quick-and-easy-recipe-for-homemade-clam-chowder-epi-paleo-leptinrx-style.5246/post-68214>

When I make my chowders, I add lots of bacon, and to replace the potatoes I use turnips. I make a veggie chowder that I add clams to, and it has every veggie I have in the fridge, so I often use lots of carrots, brussels sprouts, broccoli, turnips, onions, garlic, and cauliflower.Â  I bake the bacon in the oven prior to starting the chowder, then I will sautee the veggies in the bacon grease. Once the veggies are sauteed, I use pastured cream and vegetable broth for the soup base. I add the clams and the bacon at the end, so the bacon is still slightly crisp. Super delicious

### furface — 2012-12-22T20:33:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-quick-and-easy-recipe-for-homemade-clam-chowder-epi-paleo-leptinrx-style.5246/post-68220>

> **KristiB said**
> When I make my chowders, I add lots of bacon, and to replace the potatoes I use turnips. I make a veggie chowder that I add clams to, and it has every veggie I have in the fridge, so I often use lots of carrots, brussels sprouts, broccoli, turnips, onions, garlic, and cauliflower.Â  I bake the bacon in the oven prior to starting the chowder, then I will sautee the veggies in the bacon grease. Once the veggies are sauteed, I use pastured cream and vegetable broth for the soup base. I add the clams and the bacon at the end, so the bacon is still slightly crisp. Super delicious

Thanks, Kristi, that sounds DELICIOUS! I'll have to give that a try!

### SueC — 2012-12-23T11:54:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-quick-and-easy-recipe-for-homemade-clam-chowder-epi-paleo-leptinrx-style.5246/post-68244>

I do something similar, but I use coconut milk because I can't do dairy. Also, I had a pinch of nutmeg. Yummy!

# Thread: Can HCG Cause Problems with HPA?

**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 1

### Claudia — 2012-12-22T21:58:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-hcg-cause-problems-with-hpa.5247/post-68224>

Can Hcg cause problems with hypopituitarism and/or hypothyroid? I've been on two rounds (one in spring of 2011 and one in Spring of 2012). Before I tried hcg, I couldn't lose a pound. I was trying to get off the last 10 lbs of baby weight for 3.5 years and got sick of trying many other ways, including strict Paleo diets and ketogenic. So, I tried hcg and lost the 10 lbs. Then, it crept back on several months later. I did another round to get the same 10 off again. This fall it came back on again.Â 

Before Â hcg, I could not lose weight, but I wasn't gaining. Since doing two rounds, I gain easily now. Just wondering if it caused more problems.Â 

Could it cause a problem with the hypothalamus, since it directly affects it during protocol?

Thanks.

### Thor — 2012-12-23T13:37:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sooooo-i-am-wondering-something-else-about-guys.5248/post-68249>

.

From a guy -Â  it would seem like more pressure on ladies...Â  just look at the magazine rack at any store.Â  Cover after cover is hot skinny young flawless women.Â  When is the last time you saw a magazine like Vogue have a 600 lbsÂ  model on it?Â  Nope.

I just saw this week a 4 yr baby girl with earings, lip stick, eyeshadow, belly ring, ect... decked out to the nines

Â  Wow!Â  Things have really changed since I was a kid.Â  How far will people push the envelope?Â Â  Sure - keeping yourself up and looking tidy is great but there are extremes on all sides.

It's all about choice - do you want to learn more and do better?Â  I ask myself - who am I? I AM A CHAMPION !!!Â  You have to have HUNGER! To get more in your lifeÂ =Â You have to be hungry. Hungry to do more, hungry to seek out answers, hungry to look for higher ground...Â 

You don't get in life what you want - you get in life what you are.Â  You must program yourself for success.Â  NEVER lose your hunger.

Do not go where the path may lead on. Instead go where there is no path and leave a trail.

When you have a vision of yourself, beyond your circumstances and mental conditioning, that is a blessing and donâ€™t lose it.Â  Surround yourself with like-minded people who will challenge you to be your best. Everyone has greatness within themselves, and some may already be feeling it in their heart.Â  When you do step out to experience new things, show up in the spirit of expectation. You have it in you to do something youâ€™ve never done before, to die to your old self and give birth to who you can become.

Have an aim in life and don't forget to pull the trigger.Â  If you're not living on the edge you're taking up too much space!

I"m on fire and my ass won't stop now!Â  Look out world here I come..........Â Â  RAWR!Â Â Â 

Â Â Â Â  What's in my coffee this morning lol phew

# Thread: Constipation, Bloating and orangey/red stools!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constipation-bloating-and-orangey-red-stools.5249/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-12-23T07:38:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constipation-bloating-and-orangey-red-stools.5249/post-68233>

Hi Guys,

I have had bloating, constipation and many other issues for a while now but I have developed a new symptom recently. Orangey/red stool. Now the strange thing is it doesn't look like blood, it shouldn't be fast transit time as I typically go once every 6 days and IÂ don'tÂ think it is a bile salt deficiency as I have taken super enzymes, etc before and they didn't help.

I'm 21, male and eat strict epi-paleo... help please!

Thanks, Sean.

### nonchalant — 2012-12-23T11:49:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constipation-bloating-and-orangey-red-stools.5249/post-68242>

Hi Seanb4, welcome!

Hmmm, I haven't heard of this.Â  How long have you been off carbs?Â  Could it be a die-off of some sort?

If you have been constipated for a while, you might see some fairly intense reactions from your gut.

There are some 'occult blood' fecal tests I believe.Â  You might want to take one of those, just to be sure.

### [email redacted] — 2012-12-23T14:47:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constipation-bloating-and-orangey-red-stools.5249/post-68252>

Had constipation and bloating for ages and tried all kinds of diets that should produce die-off and almost non is produced.

Been eating low carb and paleo for 2 years now.

### nonchalant — 2012-12-23T16:18:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constipation-bloating-and-orangey-red-stools.5249/post-68256>

> **[email redacted] said**
> Had constipation and bloating for ages and tried all kinds of diets that should produce die-off and almost non is produced.
>
> Been eating low carb and paleo for 2 years now.

\----------------------------------------------

I would try eating raw meats.Â  You need to get your gut bacteria to improve.Â  Raw seafood and consider 'high meat'.Â  I think it would be the fastest way to change your gut population for the better.

### caroline — 2012-12-23T17:21:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constipation-bloating-and-orangey-red-stools.5249/post-68258>

Try Inger's Mackeral head smoothie. Â .... like nonchalant said Â - you need to improve your gut flora. Â I just blend a small frozen fish \[about 6 -8in\] with some water and lemon juice and corriander. Â I let the fish sit in the counter for a bit to unthaw a little and then blend for your life until it is smooth. Â Then drink! Â it is not bad at all. Â It is great brain food and will do wonders for your gut. Â good luck

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-08T23:17:32+0200

I assume raw meat can only be from grass fed right? I am having a hard time financially and I know for example JanSz says he just buys the regular supermarket meat (and seafood).

I buy seafood from the Asian store, so is it OK to defrost a frozen fish or mussels and eat it like that? For the other meats I tend to just buy bone and offal cuts. I only just re-introduced non-seafood meats because I was having problems like Sean (only not for 2 years) so I was trying to figure out what was going on.

I don't live near the coast, so all my seafood is frozen. I most often will defrost fish or scallops and put them in lime juice in the fridge, wait a day or two, and eat them as seviche. It tastes like eating cooked seafood, but my gut really likes it. My high meat is from grass fed lamb, yes. But the lamb hearts are inexpensive for me, unlike lamb chops, other grass fed cuts, or even grocery-store hamburger. The MHS has to be the cheapest thing, and actually the fastest-acting. You just have to be determined, and have a blender.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-09T16:20:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constipation-bloating-and-orangey-red-stools.5249/post-70075>

I have a very poor quality blender, can I use that? I think it might be a good idea to try the MHS. I can probably find super cheap mackeral at the Asian store. I don't want to break the blender and I know my house mate has trouble with it even for making simple soup/puree...so I will have to test. (Provided my friend got another one so that if it breaks she won't be out of a blender totally).

I had salmon from a can and raw onions in olive oil. I also had coconut oil and seaweed. I did enjoy this meal, I must say. I just really hope all the bad things happening to me stop happening/getting worse. If it stops right now and either stays or starts to reverse then I'm OK with that. If it means I had some road bumps in the Fall which caused downfall in the winter, I'm OK with that, but I'm not OK with it if it's something only to get worse before even beginning to get better. We shall see.

The double edged sword is that I had the return of my cycle yesterday after 6 months. This is good because it suggests normalization (or attempt) at normalization of my hormones, but it is bad b/c of how the PMS was basically over a month especially affecting my weight and mood. I think mood was hit hardest, second weight. My clothes are tight. I can't afford nor do I want to buy new clothes. It took me so long to improve body composition and in an instant/flash it's as if I did nothing...that's hard to contend with. So if having my cycle regular means all this? No thanks I don't want it. I still have bloating problems and rough tongue and darkness under eyes in ADDITION to all the new problems...so is it worth it? I think I don't have constipation though for the last few weeks. Maybe that's a improvement.

I AM taking vitex now (for over a week 3x/day) and I plan to actually stick with it this time (in addition to EPCOT etc.) I took some vitex in July. It helped (I speculate) then I stopped b/c I figured since I got my period then and that is when I started with the seafood I thought I was 'cured' and didin't need any additional support outside of diet. So I will report how next month is (PLEASE LORD LET IT BE BETTER) and also continuing of this month.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-09T16:24:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constipation-bloating-and-orangey-red-stools.5249/post-70077>

O, I suppose one more thing more on the positive side...I am not having as bad of body odour as before. It got so bad like REALLY nasty. I don't/refuse to wear deoderant so obviously it was kind of difficult to manage. Instead I just washed myself with baking soda quite regularly. Now I think it is WAYYY less. It is not 100% gone, but it requires way more effort to even get like a quarter as before. Please let this be a good sign.

I can convince myself that my darkness under my eyes is less too. I am not sure so I will just have to check up on it as the month progresses.

### MamaGrok — 2013-01-09T20:18:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constipation-bloating-and-orangey-red-stools.5249/post-70109>

Sean, have ever tried ox bile? It has absolutely ended my cramps & runs after every meal, and finally made my no-carb work successful at ending the chronic bloating.

My next step is to figure out why the heck I need it, but one thing at a time.

I had orangey/red stools with the diarrhea, frequently.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-10T20:08:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constipation-bloating-and-orangey-red-stools.5249/post-70230>

Hi MamaG,

I have gotten some ox bile for myself too now. I tried a digestive aide that contained ox bile in late November but I was not very diligent with it and also all of my other down fall symptoms began happening then too.

In your experience is one capsule enough? For example 500mg. I started with the digestive aid for last night's dinner one tablet that had 100mg of ox bile + other enzymes that I can't recall right now. For lunch I took 5 of those to equal one 500mg of ox bile. For dinner I took a 'only' ox bile of 500 mg capsule.

I will monitor how I feel/cramps and bloating.

Did taking them ever make you feel thirsty or dry in the mouth? I noticed that I can actually drink a glass of water with ox bile more easily instead of it just 'sitting' or 'filling' up. I can drink a whole glass now and it feel more like 'saturating' like how water SHOULD feel when drinking.

Did you notice immediate effects for bloating? Or how long?

Maybe if you keep taking it (and your NDT) that it will eventually stimulate your own body to do these things for itself so maybe in the end knowing why you need these remedies will be irrelevant. That would be nice huh?

For me I really think I also benefit from zinc+ b6+potassium. I don't know if taking ox bile for a while will help to not need this, but other wise I will have water retention quite badly.

I've been eating onions, seaweed etc. very strict for the past 5 days now and no coffee. So hopefully my gut is happy. I also am on day 3 of my cycle after it didn't come for 6 months. Was it because of a week of taking Vitex? Could Vitex really work that fast? Or was I 'due' anyway? I will never know, but I WON'T make the mistake of stopping it (at least right away). In July I took a Vitex tincture b/c I had missing menses for 3 months. It came back in July. I thought I was 'fine' after that and with all the seafood I thought nah I don't need any damn supplement if my diet is so tight and good. So I will stick with Vitex for at least 3 months to see how things go.

Wish me luck.

I also hope things continue to go well for you. I hope your gut shapens up and smartens up for you! (And your AB's too that they 'get lost').

p.s. When I was buying my ox bile I felt really bad for this older lady. I couldn't tell her age properly but she looked like death. Her voice was so scratchy and like a man. She was super thin and I don't know if she had some kind of cancer or something....(but had hair). Her hands were all bruised from probably IV etc. I felt so bad for her.

### MamaGrok — 2013-01-17T10:53:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constipation-bloating-and-orangey-red-stools.5249/post-71216>

> **Zorica Vuletic said**
> In your experience is one capsule enough? For example 500mg....
>
> Did taking them ever make you feel thirsty or dry in the mouth? I noticed that I can actually drink a glass of water with ox bile more easily instead of it just 'sitting' or 'filling' up. I can drink a whole glass now and it feel more like 'saturating' like how water SHOULD feel when drinking.
> Did you notice immediate effects for bloating? Or how long?
> Maybe if you keep taking it (and your NDT) that it will eventually stimulate your own body to do these things for itself so maybe in the end knowing why you need these remedies will be irrelevant. That would be nice huh?
> For me I really think I also benefit from zinc+ b6+potassium. I don't know if taking ox bile for a while will help to not need this, but other wise I will have water retention quite badly.

I think I found that 500mg was too much for me but that somewhere between 250-350 is right for my 65-75gm of protein breakfast, and that half that is good for dinner. It takes experimenting. I go by whether I get cramps and/or runs a few hours after meals (cramps & runs usually mean too little; runs alone usually means I took too much) and whether my gall bladder feels sore. When I first started taking it, I noticed a HUGE decrease in gall bladder swelling & soreness, but it would still be somewhat sore again just before bed & when I woke up. Taking the small dose at dinner has almost eliminated that.

I've also started up on HCl again. When I was ponticating a year ago about how to tell you've lost the need for extra HCl, I think I was actually mistaken, and it was the need for ox bile that I was seeing, not too muchHCl. I'm on a lot with no ill effects, so I think it's also helping.

B6 & potassium have done nothign for my water retention (only ox bile & no carbing it have), but I will try the zinc! IDK anything about how to known if you've gotten too much zinc, or no longer need to supplement, though.

### ashryn — 2013-01-18T22:44:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constipation-bloating-and-orangey-red-stools.5249/post-71526>

I usually have nothing much to contribute to these threads, but I i just had a thought about how to tell if you have too much zinc.. You can do a zinc challenge.. It involves drinking a little zinc in solution.. If you dont have enough zinc you will not really taste it.. Heres a thing from a website... Of course theynwant to sell you Zn supps, so their answer it to,take it regardless.. But basically a strong response means you have enough..

Adults: Hold 10 mL of Zinc Test in your mouth for 10 seconds. Your taste response will fall into one of four categories:

No specific taste or other sensation.
No immediate taste but after a few seconds a "dry mineral," "furry" or "sweet" taste develops.
An immediate and definite taste (not unpleasant) that intensifies with time.
A strong unpleasant taste is noted immediately.

A category 1 or 2 taste response may indicate Zinc Deficiency. Take Zinc Fix to repair your Zinc levels. Test every day until a category 3 or 4 response is indicated.
If a category 3 or 4 response is indicated take Zinc Maintain and/or Super Multi to optimise Zinc levels.

http://ethicalnutrients.com.au/content/products/zinc-test

### furface — 2012-12-24T15:34:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-such-a-thing-as-doing-too-much-ct.5256/post-68312>

Just curious as to whether or not a person...at any stage, or fitness/health/body composition level can do too much Cold Thermogenesis?

Not that I want to stop or anything, but I don't want to be overdoing it, if that's at all possible. Not that I'm anywhere near that (if it's ever a concern) stage yet, but just for future reference.

Otherwise, as a general rule, how much CT should a person partake in each week...or is there no limit?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-24T18:38:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-such-a-thing-as-doing-too-much-ct.5256/post-68331>

### villjamur_stevenson — 2013-01-03T10:47:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-such-a-thing-as-doing-too-much-ct.5256/post-69129>

>
> Just curious as to whether or not a person...at any stage, or fitness/health/body composition level can do too much Cold Thermogenesis?
>
> Not that I want to stop or anything, but I don't want to be overdoing it, if that's at all possible. Not that I'm anywhere near that (if it's ever a concern) stage yet, but just for future reference.

> Otherwise, as a general rule, how much CT should a person partake in each week...or is there no limit?

If that helps at all....

# Thread: How to Say ......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-say.5259/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2012-12-25T02:55:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-say.5259/post-68351>

This is a reminder from an extraordinary young man that I had the honour to know. Â He was an amazing son, father, husband, friend and doctor who passed away all too young from Cancer. Â He was 38 years old and he is so missed by so many. Â His life was so full of joy and passion Â - he surely was a starfish ... The Christmas RX Â .. that is how this young man lived every minute of every day. Â I miss you G

How to Say ...

The way to say "I LoveYou" to someone

is to say "I Love You" to that person

this has come to my attention

recently

"I love you" means "I love you"

and merits

being said

to the person

for whom you feel that love

various gestures and clipped phrases

do not

actually

say "I love you"

as lovely as a home cooked casserole

or cheque for some needed money

or gift certificate for an indulgence

is

and is loving, nurturing, caring

It is not the same as saying

"I Love You"

it is not

"Love ya" or "you're my girl"

or you're the best wife, mother,daughter

or some Hallmark equivalent

is

nice and perhaps true

But it is not the same as saying

"I Love You"

Do not mistake a gesture for the

declaration of love

nor heavy sentiment for it's

clear articulation

do not misjudge the brevity

of our existence

in missing the opportunity to say

"I Love You"

nor misjudge the simplicity of the

clear statement

with empty blathering, over -repititionÂ 

to meaninglessness

do not wait until your voice has dried

and your sunken eyes

mournfully cry "I Love You"

do not wait until your deathbed

or someone else's

do not give expression to love

in the heat of passion

nor as an act of contrition

like any real gift, give expression

freely, under no duress

with no sense of obligation

or awkward burden

tell all those you love

that you love them

not just your spouse, your lover

your beloved

tell them now or certainly soon

say to each person that you truly love

where your mutual love

is a bond beyond

the nature of an ordinary relationship

"I Love You"

for the only way to do this

I know

the only way to say "I Love You" to someone

is to say "I Love You"

### diane — 2012-12-25T13:12:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-say.5259/post-68377>

Amazing Caroline! Thanks for sharing!

# Thread: Half the Facts You Know Are Probably Wrong
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/half-the-facts-you-know-are-probably-wrong.5260/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2012-12-25T08:36:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/half-the-facts-you-know-are-probably-wrong.5260/post-68357>

Â I thought this article was a good reminder to challenge everything - especially our own beliefs.

"Dinosaurs were cold-blooded. Increased K-12 spending and lower pupil/teacher ratios boost public school student outcomes. Most of the DNA in the human genome is junk. Saccharin causes cancer and a high fiber diet prevents it. Stars cannot be bigger than 150 solar masses."

"In the past half-century, _all of the foregoing facts have turned out to be wrong. In the modern world facts change all of the time, according to Samuel Arbesman, author of the new book _The Half-Life of Facts: Why Everything We Know Has an Expiration Date (Current)."

The author hits medical "facts" and research pretty good.

"Facts are being manufactured all of the time, and, as Arbesman shows, many of them turn out to be wrong. Checking each one is how the scientific process is supposed to work; experimental results need to be replicated by other researchers. So how many of the findings in 845,175 articles published in 2009 and recorded in PubMed, the free online medical database, were actually replicated? Not all that many. In 2011, a disquieting study in Nature reported that a team of researchers over 10 years was able to reproduce the results of only six out of 53 landmark papers in preclinical cancer research._"

And the quote that really hit home with me:

"Another reason that personal knowledge decays is that people cling to selected â€œfactsâ€￾ as a way to justify their beliefs about how the world works. Arbesman notes, â€œWe persist in only adding facts to our personal store of knowledge that jibe with what we already know, rather than assimilate new facts irrespective of how they fit into our worldview.â€￾_ All too true; confirmation bias is everywhere."

Â 

http://reason.com/archives/2012/12/24/half-the-facts-you-know-are-probably-wro

### fitness@home — 2012-12-25T08:59:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/half-the-facts-you-know-are-probably-wrong.5260/post-68358>

Interesting article Huck. Thanks for posting!

### Huck — 2012-12-25T09:15:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/half-the-facts-you-know-are-probably-wrong.5260/post-68360>

TedX talk with the author.

\[video=youtube\_share;GaxYnvd7YAM\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GaxYnvd7YAM\[/video\]

### kathylu — 2012-12-25T11:52:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/half-the-facts-you-know-are-probably-wrong.5260/post-68372>

Huck, great TED talk! Confirmation bias is alive and well and affecting us on a daily basis. I worked in a medical research lab for a number of years and another fact that people don't realize is how specific many techniques are to a particular individual. Labs may have a good "cloner", a good "sequencer", one person who does all the ELISA or Western blots, such that results are not easily replicated from lab to lab (unless you bring their people in). Just another wrinkle to contend with.

# Thread: DIY ice vest 2.0
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-ice-vest-2-0.5263/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AndrÃ©s — 2012-12-26T13:45:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-ice-vest-2-0.5263/post-68432>

Although not as comfortable as the professional Ice Pack Vest since it is quite rigid at first, I think my approach a little more in line with the idea of getting our skin colder: I am trying to _apoptosise some belly fat { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

. I share some details of my customized ice vest, just in case it gives someone any useful idea.

An image of the Maxcold Ice Substitute, that is, reusable ice cubes for coolers. The box says that it stays cold twice as longer than gel packs but I don't have any idea if it is true. Each one is 1 pound. I used four of these in the [version 1.0 of my ice-vest](http:///forum-2/cold-thermogenesis/"/forum-2/cold-thermogenesis/effective-efficient-ct/#p30867"), using packing tape to join them in pairs and to make the suspenders joining them. The main problem was that with only four I had some area on my small back and in my upper torso unused. So I added two more. The one to cover my upper torso is flappable.

I put on the ice-vest first.

Second, I put a simple (and old) T-shirt so the next compression garment isn't stuck with the cubes (I found out the hard way

).

Third, the compression garment (not very manly... but quite chip on the street market

).

And, at last, a warmer_ vest so it doesn't loose cold to the air. I bought it at the street market too this summer and it certainly makes a difference in the duration of the ice.

I wear it for one and a half hours. When I take if off the ice is mostly melted and my skin cherry-pink

.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-26T14:48:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-ice-vest-2-0.5263/post-68438>

This actually looks like a great alternative to the manufactured ones.Â  I like that all the pieces are customized you fit your particular body.Â  Thanks for the great tips!

I think folks could actually wear this to work in the morning.Â  I'm thinking a long (or short) sleeve t-shirt with a North Face vest as the top garment.Â  It may be a bit bulky but would look a whole lot better than just the ice around the body.

### hellojtm — 2012-12-26T17:40:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-ice-vest-2-0.5263/post-68450>

Hey Andres,

Thanks for sharing. Â I went with the cold fat burner vest, hoping it works out. Â I'm not going for really long sessions but it's great to know you can use that DIY version for 90 minutes. Â It means I could make something at home that would last 45 minutes pretty easily.

best,

jesse

### hellojtm — 2012-12-31T19:40:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-ice-vest-2-0.5263/post-68871>

So far I have had mixed results with the cool fat burner. Â It's very tight and uncomfortable until the gel pads get quite thawed. Â So the initial 20-30 minutes are with minimal contact to my body as the stiff gel pads only touch on a couple of areas. Â  This leaves me with laying back against a large pillow to try to maximize surface contact. Â That's no better than sitting in the tub for me. Â The point of the device was to make me a mobile thermogenerator but for the first 1/2 of the session I'm tied to the couch. Â When it does thaw somewhat I can start attaching the velcro straps, but when you cross over the gel paks you get a really unbalanced cold effect as the left side (which has the velcro on the outside) is close to the chest by the right side (with it's velcro on the inside) is held away from the chest. Â This resulted in a skin temp of 50F on the left and 67F on the right. Â  Not ideal. Â  A bit frustrating after spending good money and I'll likely return it (they have, seemingly, a great return policy).

I only post this here, not as a dig against the company that I have no qualms with, but to encourage folks that may be on the fence about trying to design their own DIY solution that it may be comparable to what's available commercially. Â I'll crosspost this to the thread discussing cool fat burner too.

### endless — 2013-01-01T02:32:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-ice-vest-2-0.5263/post-68889>

Post it in the CFB thread...Eric, the company owner, checks that thread. I agree that the first while seems a waste while the cooler packs are solid and stiff. I think that if you are unhappy with the fit though that he will replace it for you. Seems like this initial batch were homemade by different seamstresses...maybe yours had a strap attached in the wrong place?

Thanks Endless,

I had already x-posted it over into that thread. And overall I'm feeling happier with the tool when faced with the alternative of designing a better one

take care!
jesse
