### freesia — 2013-01-03T15:34:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing-discussion-with-stephen-sinatra-video.5306/post-69189>

> **Raiken3712 said**
>
> It would be nice if someone sold footwear like Vibrams that work for earthing. Vibram shoes still have the same issue in that regard.

Pretty sure you'd be onto a winner there

You can get, from lessemf.com, some earthing straps that fit over regular synthetic sports shoes....probably too bulky for vibrams thoug

I mentioned earthing to my husband again, and sure enough - any excuse for a trip to the electronics store ha! - we're well and truly earthed now!
Yesterday he modified plugs to stay plugged in, earthed but switched off, beside our bed and we each have a one and a half metre spiral cord attached to an earthing band to sleep with. I feel tingling in my feet when my band is on, he feels no sensation but maybe a little more refreshed this morning. I'm intrigued to see how this works out longer term.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-03T19:42:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing-discussion-with-stephen-sinatra-video.5306/post-69227>

I spent a few hours tying to find leather soled shoes and boots on the internet yesterday. I actually have a bunch of moccasins that a friend made for us in Canada, so I was all set to wear those...but then I saw she had lined the leather with a fuzzy acrylic material ARGGGGG. Despite this, I put on two pairs of socks and wore them to do barn chores in -16 degrees. My feet didn't get cold at all, so if I can find some plain leather slippers I'll just convert them to outdoor shoes. As long as it stays this cold, there is no moisture to worry about and it feels nice to feel the snow underfoot.

I was also looking at Frye boots and cowboy boots. Here's my new cultural theory of the day: ....maybe the western stereotypical taciturn, low key, unfussed cowboy reflects the fact that they are grounded in their leather boots!! Apart from people who can afford custom made Italian super expensive shoes, no group other than cowboys and cowgirls has maintained an attachment to leather soled shoes that i can think of. Amazing variety and artistry.

There are also some beautiful cobblers who make old fashioned footwear in England. I've drooled over hand made riding boots from the 18th century....now it may be a medical necessity!

Off to google moccasins now and see about grounding our bed.

### Dali Dula — 2013-01-04T09:21:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing-discussion-with-stephen-sinatra-video.5306/post-69278>

When I CT in the pond I spend time standing with water up to my chin and my feet firmly settled into the muck on the bottom. The muck and I have become friends. Once you get past the ick factor the pond muck becomes incredibly comfortable and I'm sure the grounding is good. The muck loves the cold, it grows big and fluffy as the temps drop in the fall.

# Thread: Responding to Dr. Kruse's reply to my thread about having a problem at times eating 50 gr of protein
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/responding-to-dr-kruses-reply-to-my-thread-about-having-a-problem-at-times-eating-50-gr-of-protein.5307/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2013-01-01T14:05:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/responding-to-dr-kruses-reply-to-my-thread-about-having-a-problem-at-times-eating-50-gr-of-protein.5307/post-68916>

For some reason Firefox keeps voiding my javascript on the Leptin Rx forum, and won't allow me to reply after starting a thread. So I'm replying in a new thread. My apologies. I've checked my settings, and it still won't help.

> Dr. Kruse said:
>
> Here is something for you all to read regarding proteinâ€¦â€¦..and why Rosedale needs to be questioned.
>
> The Role Of Dietary Protein In Optimizing Muscle Mass, Function And Health Outcomes In Older Individuals
>
> Wolfe RR. The role of dietary protein in optimizing muscle mass, function and health outcomes in older individuals. Br J Nutr 2012;108 Suppl 2:S88-93. http://journals.cambridge.org/â€¦..id=8724365
>
> The determination of whether increased dietary protein can positively affect health outcomes is hindered by the absence of prospective, randomized trials directly addressing this issue in which all pertinent variables are controlled. Consequently, we can only address the question deductively by considering the support for the rationale underlying the notion of a beneficial effect of increased dietary protein intake.
>
> With regard to health outcomes, we have focused on older individuals. Muscle mass and function are progressively lost with aging, so that by the age of 60 many individuals have reached a threshold where function begins to be affected. An association between reduced muscle mass and strength and unfavourable health outcomes is more likely to be revealed in individuals who have significant decrements in muscle mass and strength.
>
> In this article support for the rationale underlying the notion of a beneficial effect of increased dietary protein intake is considered. Dietary protein intake, and the resulting increased availability of plasma amino acids, stimulates muscle protein synthesis. If all other variables are controlled, increased muscle protein synthesis leads to improved muscle mass, strength and function over time. Increased muscle mass, strength and function are related to improved health outcomes in older individuals.
>
> Since adverse effects of reasonable increases in protein intake above the recommended dietary allowance (RDA) of 0.8 g protein/kg/day have not been reported, it is reasonable to conclude that the optimal protein intake for an older individual is greater than the RDA.

This is very interesting, and I agree. I have no problem with the amount of protein required, or suggested by Dr. Kruse in the Leptin Rx BAB, no problem whatsoever. I LOVE protein!! My issue is actually physically being able to eat that much at times. Some days I have no problem eating 50, 60, even 70 grams for my BAB, but other days, I feel as if I'm going to throw up after 35 grams or so. I just can't seem to ingest it on those days. I don't care how beneficial it may be, or even how necessary it is that I do it, on some days I just can't seem to physically manage to eat a minimum of 50 grams of protein for my BAB. I want to, but I just can't without forcing the food down my throat...which causes me to feel like crap throughout the day.

And no, I am not ready to eat raw meat to make it "easier", so please don't go there...AGAIN!

Maybe I need to add more fat to my BAB, but then that still doesn't help me meet the minimum requirement of 50 gr of protein on those days.

Oh well, I'll keep doing my best to cram it down my throat.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-01T14:14:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/responding-to-dr-kruses-reply-to-my-thread-about-having-a-problem-at-times-eating-50-gr-of-protein.5307/post-68919>

Some times our ability to adapt up to the protein amounts also takes time.......but it can happen. Just keep falling forward in 2013.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-01-01T16:18:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-keep-cold-all-the-time.5310/post-68930>

Hi you guys,
in order to become cold adapted, can you basically live like a warm adapted human (except for lights and diet) but with cold showers/baths?
Or is it important to start living and sleeping in a colder and colder environment?
Should one always avoid warm situations or warm showers, say? Would that undo cold adaptation?
What about saunas? It would be so much easier to get your skin good and cold painlessly if you heated yourself up first. Is that a no-no?
Are cold showers not as effective as cold baths? Do I have to add ice? It seems like such an inconvenience.
Thanks all!

### freesia — 2013-01-01T22:31:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-keep-cold-all-the-time.5310/post-68952>

> **Mela said**
>
> Hi you guys,
> in order to become cold adapted, can you basically live like a warm adapted human (except for lights and diet) but with cold showers/baths?
> Or is it important to start living and sleeping in a colder and colder environment?
> Should one always avoid warm situations or warm showers, say? Would that undo cold adaptation?
> What about saunas? It would be so much easier to get your skin good and cold painlessly if you heated yourself up first. Is that a no-no?
> Are cold showers not as effective as cold baths? Do I have to add ice? It seems like such an inconvenience.
> Thanks all!

Hi Mela,
My interpretation of how I'm "cold adapted" is that now, immediately my skin is stimulated by cold (or even when I'm anticipating cold) my body warms up in response to that stimulus. Cold adaptation like Dr Kruse lays out in his CT blog series comes about by regularly getting your skin temperature to between 50 and 55 F. You'll need ice to achieve that indoors I think (unless your tap water gets down around 50F or less?).
Regular exposure to cold will mean after a while it will take less to make you feel warm. (I still take warm showers but anything much above lukewarm feels stifling.) I slept with less blankets on than I ever have over winter, didn't need to wear gloves or a winter coat...or a hat even once.

I got cold adapted with face plunges, then showers, then baths...adding more and more ice until my water was at 46 F. I'm taking a 45 minute bath 3 times per week at the moment, but over winter when it was easier to get the water that cold, I was bathing every day.

I don't know where you're at healthwise, but I was pretty bad. Housebound. Any inconvenience for me personally, is outweighed x 1000 by the fact I'm doing so well. Plus I had phenomenal support....from my family, and from the trailblazers here.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-02T05:01:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-keep-cold-all-the-time.5310/post-68990>

Ann, that was a very helpful reply. I had some of those questions too. I'm just looking to check a few things out before I go do another face plunge. I did it earlier today and to my surprise found it very difficult even in tap cold water that is really quite warm. I've been doing some (warmish) cold baths off and on since last April and do find that I don't need the blankets I used to and got through much of winter with what had previously been summer blankets. But I've never tried the face plunges or the ice before. So I'm going back to the beginning of Jack's advice and starting with the face plunges.

### freesia — 2013-01-02T05:34:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-keep-cold-all-the-time.5310/post-68991>

Aussie nana...I think the "trick" is just to (as Dr Kruse says) keep falling forward. The last thing you want to do is try and match someone elses rate of progress and turn yourself off CT in the process. Cold adaptation is too special a prize to rush for and risk breakage.
If it feels cold to you, that's where you need to be. If recovery is rough don't aim for more the following day just aim to do the same temperature (whether it's 70F or 50F) and hope for an easier recovery. That's still moving forward.
You're in summertime now like me? That's a GREAT time to start I think.....although if you went through winter with summer blankets it sounds like you've already made some decent progress. Yay!

There's something I didn't mention to the original poster and that is that I think you need to go into cold adaptation with a certain amount of faith. So long as you're consistent with the cold water, and you decrease the temperature, it's a naturally evolving process. You manage it to the best of your ability day by day. If I went somewhere heated I didn't think too much about it...I just removed a layer or two until I was comfortable. It meant, from a few weeks into CT, all winter I was wearing a bit less ...sometimes a lot less (!) than people around me.
Get used to wearing lots of layers of lightweight natural fibre for maximum convenience and comfort.

...By the time you're out the other end of winter......be prepared to want to wear your birthday suit and a smile and not much else! LOL.

Anne.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-03T00:05:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-keep-cold-all-the-time.5310/post-69090>

Anne is spot on.........

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-07T02:31:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-keep-cold-all-the-time.5310/post-69710>

I'm not sure if others have this experience but when its really hot here (100+F) and everyone is complaining I find myself tolerating the heat better than most people. Instead of turning the air con on as soon as it hits 28 (82F) I don't until I'm sitting and its over 32 (90F). I'm able to keep working in the heat without even thinking of it much as long as I'm not in the sun and at risk of burning. Has anyone else had this experience?

Also I went to buy meat from a wholesale shop that is kept at freezer temperatures. Everyone else had to wear thermal jackets. I went in and spent 20 minutes in there and was really cold after about 5 minutes, but at 10 minutes I warmed up and was fine till it came time to leave. Then when everyone was complaining about the 100F heat outside I was nicely cool and managed the transition.

This is rather fun.

### kathylu — 2013-01-07T03:06:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-keep-cold-all-the-time.5310/post-69714>

Same for me. I tolerated the summer heat last summer better than I ever have before. People are saying that 2012 was the hottest year on record in the US. I didn't particularly notice!

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T06:43:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-keep-cold-all-the-time.5310/post-69721>

>
> I'm not sure if others have this experience but when its really hot here (100+F) and everyone is complaining I find myself tolerating the heat better than most people. Instead of turning the air con on as soon as it hits 28 (82F) I don't until I'm sitting and its over 32 (90F). I'm able to keep working in the heat without even thinking of it much as long as I'm not in the sun and at risk of burning. Has anyone else had this experience?
> Also I went to buy meat from a wholesale shop that is kept at freezer temperatures. Everyone else had to wear thermal jackets. I went in and spent 20 minutes in there and was really cold after about 5 minutes, but at 10 minutes I warmed up and was fine till it came time to leave. Then when everyone was complaining about the 100F heat outside I was nicely cool and managed the transition.
> This is rather fun.

Ooh! Where is that place? maybe they'll give me a part time job so i can go hang out in their freezer!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-07T12:47:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-keep-cold-all-the-time.5310/post-69746>

When you use CT you gain the ability.......to tolerate heat well because you become able to release free heat easy.

### freesia — 2013-01-07T15:32:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-keep-cold-all-the-time.5310/post-69768>

Makes perfect sense. Thermoadaptive fitness?

We've had some extreme temps here over Christmas and New Year. An uncharacteristic high followed by a sharp cold change - lotsa big electrical storms....which is big fun while camping with kids in a tent by the way!

...Usually I'd be completely wiped out by weather antics like that but my new found (and much cherished!) energy hasn't flagged at all. So empowering.

# Thread: CT Damaging Toes?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-01-01T16:25:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-68931>

Have any of you found CT seems to damage your toes? After maybe 2 months of doing CT off an on with cold showers, or swims in the lake (with and without water shoes), I have permanently sore toes and toe nails. There are some with black spots looking in colour like a dark bruise (these have been there over a month). There are dead skin looking scales over some areas, especially the dark parts (I do have toenail fungus too, I think. Is it spreading?). Recently, my toes have started being swollen all the time, and the worst one, a baby toe, is sore with small bumps under the skin.
My feet are often hard to keep warm. They can very quickly become white and hard just walking around on my cold floor in the morning. At these times and during CT, my feet and toes are generally white, which I guess Jack warned against.
Am I damaging my feet? Anyone have anything like this? How do I adapt them or protect them?

### nonchalant — 2013-01-01T16:53:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-68936>

Hi Mela, I haven't heard of this issue with CT. I used to keep my toes above water when taking baths.
What temperatures are you using for CT? Since I have been going below 50F, I've noticed white splotches on the soles of my feet that feel numb for a while after my baths. I may start keeping my entire foot above water. I wonder if wool socks would help?

I also wonder if your toes are showing symptoms of detox. I got a rash on my hand (with small blisters) about a month after starting CT.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-01T17:01:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-68937>

I would be very careful Meta. There was a thread on Reynaud's syndrome a little while ago...you might want to read that. Two quotes from Dr. K that are in that thread.

"Well I think if you wear the socks and gloves and keep your hands and toes out you might not have any issues at all but you could test it out in your sink first. I would strongly consider getting an idea of what your Essential fatty acids are in your cell membranes because with Raynaud's people have some of the worse ratio's I have seen. This is why they have Raynaud's, along with a very permeable gut."

"Raynaud's in my opinion is a lack of bio-energenics in the vascular smooth muscles causing a lack of extracellular adenosine which cause reactive vasoconstriction to any stimulus. Anything tied to the MG ATPase function improves it. Mg, avoidance of calcium, D-Ribose, Phosphate, L-carnitine, DHA and Iodine help reverse it."

Hope this helps....

### Inger — 2013-01-02T02:14:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-68966>

Meta, please protect your feets!
That is why we should always listen to our bodies. I do only a minute or two in close to freezing water, because I feel my toes cannot take more they start to hurt! and there is no way I can hold them out of water. I use crogs too now, because I realized my toes got a bit swollen too and I want to have beautiful toes always! Now they are beautiful again! You need to not let your toes freeze too much! Baby steps. Use sock or shoes or whatever you come up with, if you are going to stay longer in! Jack says in hes CT protocol to do cold baths.. keep the feets out.. AND use socks too!!! That is for a reason. Maybe go back and read the CT protocol and you will see with different eyes. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

If you want more CT Meta, just go more often but short! I think that is way better.

And do CT where your feets and hands are kept warm. To keep your home cool is great! Just put wool socks on. I realize if I let my hands freeze too much they get not so pretty. Kind of swollen. Means to me, they are not ready for so much cold.

To help your toes heal, do some warm epsom salt foot bath, use a nice oil like coconut oil to keep them soft and moist, do some pedicure. I am sure your toes will look pretty soon!

### Inger — 2013-01-02T02:34:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-68970>

That is how my feet look when they are healthy. Not swollen or read. If they do I know I need to change something..

### caroline — 2013-01-02T02:43:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-68971>

Once again Inger - your instincts are right on and so powerful. Beautiful feet! It is lovely for a woman to have beautiful feet and hands that are well taken care of......
so feminine and sensual I think. I wonder what the guys have to say about this????

### maggie — 2013-01-02T11:15:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-69002>

Mela, I got dark, bruised looking spots when I first started CT'ing and was using gel packs to spot CT. Those spots were tissue damage and they hurt and took a long time to heal. Occasionally I still make that mistake, but I'm much more careful now. Feet out of the water, in wool socks and wool hat makes CT easier to take. Takes a bit of maneuvering to get those socks on once I'm in the tub, but I think some people put them on wet feet.

Are you CT'ing in a tub or in a lake/ocean? If you're "wild" CT'ing, maybe neoprene booties would help.

Best of luck to you.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-01-02T13:09:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-69020>

I keep my feet and hands out also. I put socks on feet, mittens on hands and a hat to cover my head. All of that ultimately seems to help my toes not turn white.

I wonder if part of what is going on with you Mele maybe a detox symptom. I can't explain it as it was just a thought.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2013-01-03T10:15:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-69124>

> **Mela said**
>
> Have any of you found CT seems to damage your toes? After maybe 2 months of doing CT off an on with cold showers, or swims in the lake (with and without water shoes), I have permanently sore toes and toe nails. There are some with black spots looking in colour like a dark bruise (these have been there over a month). There are dead skin looking scales over some areas, especially the dark parts (I do have toenail fungus too, I think. Is it spreading?). Recently, my toes have started being swollen all the time, and the worst one, a baby toe, is sore with small bumps under the skin.
> My feet are often hard to keep warm. They can very quickly become white and hard just walking around on my cold floor in the morning. At these times and during CT, my feet and toes are generally white, which I guess Jack warned against.
> Am I damaging my feet? Anyone have anything like this? How do I adapt them or protect them?

Hi Mela,

From my reading about Raynaud's, I would be very concerned about the black spots showing up.
I would try to keep your feet warm while doing the CTs from now on.

Do your fingers or toes ever turn white or go numb during or after the CT?

### lauren_r — 2013-01-01T23:27:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bacon.5315/post-68955>

I was wondering if everyone eats Free Range Bacon?
Free range bacon is pretty expensive in Australia, more than any other meat/seafood source. I do only eat grass fed, free range etc, however
the amount of bacon i would like to include for protein in my diet makes it a strain to include a lot. Due to conditions of pig farming, i think the stress/inflammation
in the meat from traditional supplies is prob not worth it...thoughts???

Also what are thoughts on non free range lard? SHould it be avoided due to stress/inflammation in meat from horrible conditions?
thanks

### furface — 2013-01-02T00:54:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bacon.5315/post-68957>

I eat regular, conventional bacon, but I check the label and try and find one with no added sugar. Maple Leaf reduced salt bacon has no added sugar. I think if we worry about every piddly ass detail in every single thing we eat or do, the stress of worrying will be more hazardous than any miniscule little damage whatever "problems" what we were worried about would, or even could cause.

I recently did try some double smoked free range bacon from Chilliwack B.C. but for $12.99 lb. I should have gotten the whole pig!! It was good, but not $12.99 lb. worth good!

As far as the lard goes, I found a local butcher shop that sells Grass-Fed beef tallow for $5.99 for a 2.5" x 5" x 1.25" slab.

### caroline — 2013-01-02T01:54:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bacon.5315/post-68964>

Lauren .... I don't know if you have Costco there? I got some great bacon at the Costco in Sydney - but it was quite awhile ago.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-06T00:20:48+0200

### Thor — 2013-01-06T01:32:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bacon.5315/post-69553>

.
I'm confused how you would say Nitrites are safe??? Nitrites have been clearly shown not safe - they cause cancer and disrupt hormones.

Russell L. Blaylock, M.D has shown chemicals can damage a critical part of the brain known to control hormones, leading to endocrine problems. They are called excitotoxins and include chemicals like; Artificial colors (FD&C colors) Sodium nitrite and nitrate, BHT (butylated hydroxytohicne) Saccharin, MSG (monosodium glutamate), Aspartame Caffeine, Propylene glycol, Sulfites (especially sodium bisulfite) Sulfur dioxide, BVO (Brominated Vegetable Oil)

sodium nitrite reacts with the stomach acids to produce nitrosamines - one of the deadliest known carcinogens.

The USDA actually tried to ban sodium nitrite in the 1970's, but was stopped by the meat processing industry - however the dangers of Nitrites were discovered in 1956.

Pubmed alone has several thousand research papers pointing to the dangers. But it's safe?

Pancreatic cancer isn't the only negative side effect of consuming processed meats such as hot dogs. Leukemia also skyrockets by 700% following the consumption of hot dogs and bacon. (Preston-Martin, S. et al. "N-nitroso compounds and childhood brain tumors: A case-control study." Cancer Res. 1982; 42:5240-5.)

Effect of processed and red meat on endogenous nitrosation and DNA damage

http://carcin.oxfordjournals.org/content/30/8/1402.full

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bacon.5315/post-69572>

The science and data are weak.........Blaylock is right about MSG and some others but he is an alarmist on others.......for the revenue stream in my view.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-06T12:51:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bacon.5315/post-69597>

yesterday in IPMG i wrote this about two of the chemicals you mentioned above: Butylated hydroxyanisole (BHA) is an antioxidant consisting of a mixture of two isomeric organic compounds, 2-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyanisole and 3-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyanisole.The primary use for BHA is as an antioxidant and preservative in food, food packaging, animal feed, cosmetics, rubber, and petroleum products and some meds like statins! The US National Institutes of Health report that BHA is reasonably anticipated to be a human carcinogen based on evidence of carcinogenicity in experimental animals. In particular, when administered in high doses as part of their diet, BHA causes papillomas and squamous cell carcinomas of the forestomach in rats and Syrian golden hamster. In Cali it is a listed carcinogen for humans but no where else. It is also OK in mice studies. Butylated hydroxytoluene (BHT), also known as butylhydroxytoluene, is a fat-soluble, organic compound, chemically a derivative of phenol, that is useful for its antioxidant properties. It has amazing properties against herpes virus so it is not all bad! BHT is used in fluids/soft solids to prevent oxidation. I use BHT for many people with certain viral conditions. It is not a bad drug. BHA is more controversial but based upon what I have read not too bad compared to most other neolithic agents. I would avoid BHA but I have zero issues with BHT.

### Jonathin — 2013-01-06T17:18:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bacon.5315/post-69637>

Pork tastes great no doubt. My wife just salt cured jowls from the half a hog we got from a friend. What a pig eats does matter. Truly pastured pork is practically non-existent as the productivity is low unless the producer has the perfect land situation to provide plenty of calories such as an oak woodlot. Most pigs are fed plenty of industrially produced agricultural commodities. Anything that is larger than a small well-managed farm is most likely an ecological nightmare. Compare that to something like grass-finished beef and you are talking about a world of difference in multiple ways.

### Thor — 2013-01-06T21:24:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bacon.5315/post-69672>

.
Of course Steve Jobs would contract some disease that end in death. Vegan diet is extremely dangerous.

Not so funny that vegan food is often more loaded with dangerous chemicals - Tofu Vegan Hot Dogs have more nitrites than a normal meat filled Ball Park Brand Hot Dog. Most Vegans eat a ton of soy based foods - tofu to edamame. Nearly all baby formulas have soy based protien as an ingredient. Baby boys who ingest soy will end up chemically castrated before their first birthday. Over time those boys will have small under-developed testicles and a smaller than normal penis - sometimes referred to as "micro-penis" yikes! The average penis will be under 2 inches in a short period of evolution. Sperm counts will continue to fall. and so on.........

Here's some proof: http://www.fws.gov/chesapeakebay/pdf/endocrine.pdf
Now scientists are connecting the dots with evidence of increasing abnormalities among humans, particularly large increases in numbers of genital deformities among newborn boys. For example, up to 10 percent of boys are now born with undescended testicles, which can cause problems up to and including surgical castration. Now up to 2 percent of boys in the United States are born with hypospadias, in which the urethra exits the penis improperly, such as at the base rather than the tip. Translates to peeing out the base of your penis.

Keep chowin' down on that vegen food! It's not as healthy as those tree-huggin' fruit loops like to think.

# Thread: Hello from Western Australia

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-02T04:53:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-western-australia.5318/post-68987>

Hi all
I'm a 62 year old woman currently living in Western Australia where we've just had a heat wave with temperatures from 37 to 42 C (98-105 F). I've had continuous health issues all my life that the doctors would disregard and tell me there was nothing wrong with me other than a mildly elevated lab that would change from time to time. As I've grown older the diagnoses changed from nothing to various autoimmune diagnoses which changed with the years. I've been searching for health for years and I hope I've found what I need here.

I'm currently eating a shellfish breakfast and paleo the rest of the time. I'm sugar free, grain free, legume and solanum free (except for a blip over Christmas). Now back on the wagon and eat no processed foods.

I've been reading and reading and reading Jack's blog and its making more and more sense, so I'm doing more and more. Now off to check the criteria for CT.

# Thread: How fast does cold damage

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-fast-does-cold-damage.5319/post-69037>

I've been getting in my 33-39 degree pool..and I'm in there for less than 5 minutes...and my hands have a weird sensation for 1-2 days ...am I damaging them? Is frostbite in above freezing water quick? How long does frostbite take outside in the cold...I guess the effects are magnified in water though...perhaps I need to do something to protect my hands and feet?

Anyone have anything that works really well to isolate the hands and feet in the pool. So they stay warm?

### kathylu — 2013-01-02T15:28:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-fast-does-cold-damage.5319/post-69039>

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-02T16:46:15+0200

# Thread: Epsom Salt in cold water can it work?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epsom-salt-in-cold-water-can-it-work.5320/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2013-01-02T15:57:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epsom-salt-in-cold-water-can-it-work.5320/post-69042>

So I've put it in cold water before..and was wondering...was that pointless? Will it get into my skin in cold water? Can I stir it to dissolve it...or would I have to actually make it warm for it to dissolve. I've heard people with thoughts that taking warm baths might slow down adaption to the cold so I don't want to take a separate warm bath for Epsom salt. If it doesn't work I'll just buy some more Magnesium Citrate supplement I was using.

Thanks

### nonchalant — 2013-01-02T16:17:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epsom-salt-in-cold-water-can-it-work.5320/post-69045>

I often use epsom salts in my cold bath, and I think they work fine. They dissolve easily in the cold tap water.
I use them to help detox bromine. I also use epsom salts, along with sea salt, in a bowl on the counter for my right hand, to keep the rash down. Again, I dissolve the salts in tap water, and dip my hand several times per day. Works like a charm.

### maggie — 2013-01-02T16:26:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epsom-salt-in-cold-water-can-it-work.5320/post-69046>

I dissolve 2 cups of epsom salts in a small pan of hot water and throw that into my cold bath. It's not enough to warm the tub water. Since Nonchalent's getting results from soaking her hand, I'm hoping that I'll be absorbing the mag this way.

# Thread: Does anyone have a foolproof way to......

Does anyone have a full proof way to maximise a consult with JK???? Honestly - I write everything down - I read as much as I can and still something happens! My mind turns to Salami ... and I seem to always overlook important questions. I am a pretty efficient person - but all that seems to fly out the window.....WTH....
Than God for recordings - but still ... there has to be a better and more efficient way to do this.... any ideas? what has worked for you? It would be so much more efficient if he could just read our mind .....would save a ton of time....surely he would be up to the task......

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69075>

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-02T23:18:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69084>

my advice.........turn off that little voice in your head that is always thinking as we talk to one another.......and just focus on listening. Record it. The educational consults are not meant for you to ask a ton of questions.......they are really about teaching you how to understand how your biology takes electrons in food and uses them to make energy......and how your particular labs help decipher what is going on in your Ferrari engines. I think too many people try to do too much during the consult instead of the intended purpose. You will see after you employ this technique your follow up consults will lead to deeper meaning and understanding. For members......anything that comes up later can be asked in Ask Jack........and I usually will answer it unless it is way off base for the forum.

### caroline — 2013-01-03T04:43:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69101>

Actually - I am pretty proud of myself. I was thinking about this all afternoon and I realized something .....when I go to see my Doc - I have a limited amount of time - 15 minutes or 30 minutes if I book a double. I would never dream of trying to ask a million questions ... I would just focus on a couple of things and then listen and learn. So Dr. K. - I figured that out on my own. It is much more important to just listen and learn.
Thanks for putting that so clearly. We definitely do better when we know better.

### caroline — 2013-01-03T05:23:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69106>

Dr. Kruse ..... in your response above ....you said something pretty special ... "we are talking to one another". I guess we aren't used to that attitude from our Doctors. It never seems like a two way street. You are educating us in so many ways. I used to watch a remarkable Kidney Specialist interacting with his patients in the hospital. He wasn't my husband's Doc but I saw him in action so many times. He always listened intently to his patients and always wanted their opinions. He spent at least as much time listening as he did talking. He would think nothing of cruising into the ward at 11:00pm because he had thought of something and he always joked that he only saw half the amount of patients on his day off as he did during his regular clinic. A very special guy.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-03T06:55:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69111>

I got a good advice from Adriana before I got my consult. She told me that I should only listen and not talk too much myself. I tried, but since my papers were lost in the 'either' somewhere between Germany and Tennessee, Dr. Kruse had none of my medical history or lab results. So I tried to make up by giving him as much information as possible. Since he is very intuitive he knew most of it anyway and had it figured out often before I mentioned the result. But I guess I wasted a lot of time by affirming something he already knew. He gave me the prescription of which supplements to take in that consult which is the same he gave in his December webinar. Since the webinar I understand why. Since I trust Dr. Kruses advice, I do not question it, only try to understand the implications. Guess I am like his son, just saying, tell me how you did it and I do the same. It all makes so much sense. The understanding for me comes mostly later, when I can observe the results on me.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-03T10:41:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69126>

Question everything including me..............just sayin'

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69196>

However, the end result has been I have opened up emotionally to parts of myself I have tried to hide from and have done a complete 180Â° turnaround from where I was prior to the consult.

and yes, once I find a new doc here and can figure get new labs I'll be booking another one. Hopefully next time I won't have to make Jack chase me all over the internets and I will be able to at least be coherent and not trying to hide under my desk.

sorry about all that Jack. I still feel like I owe you a big one.

# Thread: Just wondering.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-02T20:27:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69068>

These last three days I've started having massive adrenaline runs at the drop of a hat - well the sound of a voice more like. To start with I was feeling a bit despondent that after all the work I've been doing I had a new set of symptoms. Then it came to me that given that I've had massive fatigue, CFS/fibromyalgia + multiple symptoms all my life I was wondering if the adrenal rush is a step forward - an improvement. Perhaps I'm actually producing adrenaline but my body doesn't know how to manage it properly yet.

I've been paleo eating 90-95% of the time for five years. Weight loss 20kg during this time, mostly in three periods of time and maintenance during the times of autoimmune flares. I knew with the AI and exercise intolerance that things weren't right so I finally decided to take Jack's stuff seriously. I dropped out eggs, solanums (potatoes, tomatoes etc) and added mussels and offal as breakfast foods (instead of bacon and eggs). Then I couldn't buy offal as the shops didn't have them so I just ate the mussels (New Zealand green lipped mussels with a little bit of home made bacon (salt and smoke cured, no nitrites). Other meats are largely grass fed. I went back to having cold water baths, though they are currently about 80 degrees which is all I can manage at the moment or on a couple of days trekking out to the beach for a swim. For the last four years I've been rolling out of bed at dawn and taking the dog for a walk and not wearing sun glasses as I walk into the rising sun. I go to bed about an hour after dusk in summer and within three hours in winter. Then over Christmas I had prawns in my diet as well instead of other meats at a number of meals. I have meditated regularly for years and am good at managing all the psycho-social-spiritual issues that come up.

Other things I've done is to stop taking 5HTP in November for depression as I didn't feel like I needed it any more - no depression after 60 years - that's massive for me. Then stopped taking the thyroid meds in December and I haven't got cold or had the other myriad of symptoms I used to have. Then stopped taking dozens of supplement pills a day as Jack says supplements should be for the short term not forever. PreChristmas I was waking feeling a deep and huge total body experience joy peaking through my normal morning feeligns.

Then the adrenaline starts running and I have anxiety about things I have no need to be anxious about. So... is this things getting worse due to a four day eating blip over Christmas (2 servings of Christmas pudding and too many chocolates and sweets, but no real pigging out)? Or is it an indicator that post Christmas I was eating more shell fish and crustaceans and gone back to the cold baths and things are improving and I only have high cortisol now, not the total adrenal fatigue with depressed cortisol?

Any thoughts anyone?

### nonchalant — 2013-01-02T22:17:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69074>

Hi Nana, welcome!
My first thought is that face dunking into ice water might ease your anxiety fairly quickly.
Perhaps some of the supplements are still needed? Magnesium or something else? I know I have been dropping supplements, but now my sleep is messed up. The holidays have also disrupted my normal CT and sun watching/light avoiding patterns. I need to get things back to normal and see if things magically even out, or if I need to add back in some supplements. Or add some better nutrition, as Jack would say.

How long were you taking the 5-HTP? Serotonin needs to be balanced with dopamine.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-02T23:38:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69087>

Thanks for the welcome Nonchalant. I was taking the 5 HTP for about three years. It was the only thing that got rid of my residual depression. After I had worked through my life issues from every angle I was still depressed - though not deeply so - till I started the 5HTP. That worked a treat. And I suspected I was somewhat dopamine deficient but wasn't sure what to do about that.

I started the face dunking yesterday and might have overdone it as I had a spot on the tip of my nose this morning. I dunked very briefly - 1-5 seconds was all I could manage, about 8 times I think, till I could do it no more.

I took some notes from Jack's adrenal fatigue posts and will add a few supplements back. So will go look see what I have in my cupboard. I know I have some green tea extract there. Also go put some more water to make ice in the freezer.
"Adrenal fatigue Rx for cortisol elevation: Control cytokine response by controlling fast acting eicosanoid hormone system through Leptin Rx, CT protocol, supplements or foods with curcumin, quercetin, resveratrol, pycnogenol, green tea extract."

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-03T02:40:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69097>

Any idea of how long "pretty quickly" is when it comes to easing anxiety? Two face dunks done and a third one coming up. Plus I had a teaspoon of tumeric (curcumin) and a green tea capsule. If no-one knows about how long, I'll let you know how long it takes me.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-04T01:17:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69258>

Day 3 of face dunking. Adrenaline and anxiety now only around one issue but it still woke me up around 3.30am and I've been awake since. I wasn't dreaming of anything relating to what makes me anxious and there is no reason to be anxious about the topic which sets me off so I think the 3.30am wake up was physiological, not psychological.

### Inger — 2013-01-04T01:59:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69259>

Nana,
I have suffered quite a lot from anxiety too.. since childhood. I guess I somehow have it from my mother as she still have anxiety issues. I am thinking my bulemia was a way to try to deal with that anxiety too. It "kind of" went away after binging and purging but sure it did not lasted for long. It was very stupid, indeed.

What helps anxiety disappear.. to me?

To eat raw. Yes, it is not something I understand why, but it works. It really works. I have tested it on and off many many times. When I eat cooked I get anxious much easier. Raw makes my nerves strong as steel..huh. Or almost. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

It also makes it easier for me to face the stressors and deal with them in a good way.
To eat crap like puddings and such is really bad.. at least for me. So I never do that. IDK why but my body does not like it at all.. or my psyche. Cigarettes does it to me too. If I smoke just a half cigarette I can feel it for days after. I get a low. Nope. Not worth it at all. I always tell myself I need to feel happy and to feel love inside all the time, and enjoy my life every minute! Because you never know when you die and I want to die happy! It makes the fear for dying go away quite a lot too.. because I figure, when I die it will be the state of mind I am in that continues..

No idea if it is true but I like to think like that.

Then.. coffee adds anxiety if I already have it. So to quit coffee might help! Coffee might be absolutely no issue for me if I feel well and happy and stressless.. but the more stressed out I am the worse I feel from coffee..

Then.. if I do too much CT I might get a weird feeling of anxiety around my heart. It can last for many days. I think it is detox of some sort.. I am not sure though. But I keep my sessions short and do not overdo it. But it is only from submerging the whole body in close to freezing water, icepacks or facedunking never get me this feeling. Might be only a physical thing as it feels like my abdomen and heart area get frozen somehow.. feels very much like anxiety though and I do not like it.

To let go. That is the mental work that have helped me. To let go of anything I was clinging to. It really helps anxiety too.
To always do my best, to do the right things even if they are painful, to face my fears. Just go through them. If you feel anxious, just ask yourself, why? Dig deep. It is actually fun stuff to do that work, very interesting. I am by far not done yet..
I have figured.. fear and anxiety are somehow very close. And what have I to fear, if I do the right things? It might be hard to know what the right things are at times, but then I read, and search...and search. And then I find the answers. It is lots of work but it is very interesting! So it does not feel like work at all.

When I use both the mental and the physical way I don't suffer from anxiety any more. There is just a beautiful peace inside.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-04T11:03:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69294>

getting in the cold ocean is the best panacea for anxiety

### Inger — 2013-01-05T16:26:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69484>

It always helps me to go for a dip in the river too.

Totally. Maybe it is the grounding? Together with the cold..

I thought about you Aussie nana today at work and I wanted to add that lightly cooked low fat fish/mussels with seaweeds never give me anxiety. Cooked salmon is not that good though. Don't ask me why.

So I always eat salmon raw.

# Thread: Clearly, the scales are broken...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 237
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ashryn — 2013-01-03T02:49:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69098>

I've been using the Internet since 1994 and have never kept a blog.. I lurk on fora but rarely post.. I'm outing myself as a lurker here, and have been inspired to actually participate in an attempt to change things (if you always do what you've always done you'll always get what you've always gotten). So here's the back story..

I have always thought I was fat, turns out i was a healthy looking kid, full of muscle, energy and coordination but paralysed with self-consciousness issues because my mum was constantly dieting and telling me what was "flattering" (small child brain: you normally look fat but that dress hides it well) ...and etc. Pretty sure this is a common story so no need to expand further.

After I had a child and stopped breast feeding I began to actually get fat, (and now that I think about it, the experience coincides with staying up all night to talk on irc with people who were interesting...ooh circadian snap!)

I trained as a naturopath and knew all the nutrition things and the chemistry things and the herbs that would help, but they didn't - much. After I realised I didn't want to be responsible for anyone else's health and stopped practicing, I gradually forgot all the good things I knew and got fatter till eventually I went to fat classes to learn how to not be fat. I lost some..

Then I discovered substances that not only made me not hungry, but also made me want to dance a whole lot and feel confident enough to do so.. I lost almost all the weight I needed to.. (Rockstar on the outside, blubbering mess inside was me. Moreover, looking good is kinda irrelevant when your lifestyle is so damn selfish that you destroy all of your good relationships... So I saved my relationships and started to put on weight.)

When I was almost back to my starting weight, I decided it was time to get serious again and started doing all the cw things that, of course, made no difference. So I started to do some proper research..
\-I found out about leptin and heavy lifting, about cheat days and low carb.. It worked for a bit then stopped.
\-I tried subbing meals with shakes to lower my total calories down combined with medication (pretty much slow release speed) to give me energy to exercise.. Worked a bit then stopped.
\-Cut out sugar.. Worked really well, then stopped.
\-Tried adding more exercise.. No effect... Figured I must be destined to just be fat.

I'm glad I tried all those things because it made it much easier to accept the crazy ideas I found here.

I had already realised my body was no good at getting at the stored fat and using it for energy, so regardless of how low I reduced calories, I just couldnâ€™t shift it. Reading the science of why was actually quite liberating, and gave me enough optimism to try just one thing and see how it went.. I found JK's site in mid November, read everything for 2 weeks and began to make some changes in December.

Things I learned in the last 18 months;
Â· about cardio â€“ it is a complete waste of time, and actually makes you weaker and less fit. Exercise should be done no more than 3 times a week for 20 minute sessions and should involve lifting heavy things and not cardio
Â· about sugar â€“ its an addictive poison and breaks your hormones among other things which stops you from being able to utilise stored energy (also causes low immunity and hellish period pains)
Â· about calorie counting â€“ complete bullshit and doesnâ€™t work.

Things I recently learned about from the paleo people:
Â· about saturated fat â€“ your body needs it to function, and if you donâ€™t eat it, you will store every carb you eat because your body thinks thereâ€™s a famine
Â· about vitamin D â€“ thereâ€™s a deficiency epidemic because of sunscreens and sunglasses and deficiency causes depression and low energy sore muscles (and some suggest, skin cancer)
Â· about carbs - you donâ€™t actually need them for energy and your body and brain function better with protein and sat-fat as its main source of energy
Â· about sleep â€“ how you utilise energy and heal is all tied into circadian rhythms. You release melatonin 4 hours after eating when it is dark. Any insulin spike stops the production of melatonin (=insomnia) Melatonin makes you release a bunch of other hormones that make you burn excess fat, stay asleep all night, and heal damaged cells. If you break the melatonin cycle, the hormones donâ€™t happen and metabolism gets progressively brokener.
\- About cold - Cold heals metabolic disorders like insulin resistance and diabetes because it forces you to use other mechanisms than the sugar burning ones. Freezing fat cells causes them to suicide.
(wow, this post is getting epic)
\- leptin reset â€“ retrain your body to burn fat preferentially over carbs.

I began the thing a bit half arsed, with just the giant breakfast and no snacking and mostly paleo eating, but after losing a kilo per day in the first week, I added more elements until by 21st December I was following these 10 rules strictly:

1\. Eat 50grams protein for breakfast every day within 30 minutes of waking
2\. Eat dinner early so that 3-4 hours elapses between eating and sleeping
3\. Never snack, never count calories, and donâ€™t do any cardio.
4\. Eat less than 25 g carbs per day (it goes faster if you can do less than 10g/day because you go Keto, which is awesome for making you good at using stored fat)
5\. Look at no artificial light after the sun goes down each day (I have sexy blue blockers and f.lux) get to bed before midnight and 8 hrs sleep
6\. Lunch and dinner should be strict paleo (seasonal with no grains or legumes at all (and ever again))
7\. Supplement with lots of magnesium, krill oil and vitamin d
8\. Make sure you look at natural sunlight during the day, and get some on your skin
9\. Eat liberal amounts of coconut oil because it cannot be stored as fat and the body can use it for energy immediately
10\. Expose yourself to cold whenever possible

Results after 1 month: (sample size of 1)
Â· In 4 weeks, I dropped a couple of dress sizes, and can see my ribs and 6-pack muscles (yer, not buff yet, but I can see them)
Â· I often sleep all the way through the night
Â· I have energy all day with no dumb slump at 2pm
Â· Skin is tighter, less wrinkles and less flabby skin.
Â· I eat breakfast at 5am and am not hungry for 12 hours without lightheadedness, cravings, tiredness or any of the other cranky stuff that comes with being hungry
Â· I donâ€™t feel deprived by not eating deliciousness when it is around, I am not particularly interested in it any more
Â· I no longer have sore muscles all the time
Â· Less period pain no PMS (although I want a few months to test this to make sure it wasnâ€™t a freak month) and shorter duration
Â· Increased motivation / less depression
Â· Frozen shoulder has mostly sorted itself out
Â· When I get pedicures now, they donâ€™t have to razor off the hard skin on my feet or cut out ingrowing toenails (Used to do this weekly)
Â· No pimples

Things I discovered:
Â· Icepacks on your belly arenâ€™t that uncomfortable, and they make you fall asleep fast afterwards, and they actually do make fat go away without leaving loose skin. (Iâ€™m considering rigging up something to ice my canteen lady arms!)
Â· There is only so much bacon a person can eat in one sitting,
Â· 50 grams of protein is an awful lot of meat.
Â· Bulletproof coffee is focking awesome (see below for recipe)
Â· Once you get past sugar addiction, it isnâ€™t actually that delicious.
Â· You can lose dress sizes and not have your scales change much at all
Â· You can lose weight doing no exercise and sleeping more and eating giant amounts of fat per day (seriously giant amounts â€“ 30% protein, 70%fat most days)
Â· Coconut oil is awesome for removing makeup and moisturising and sunscreen
Â· You donâ€™t need fibre in your diet
Â· I can actually cook.
Â· I donâ€™t burn as easily as I thought I did.
Â· You can make awesome pizza bases with coconut flour
Â· Homemade chocolate is really good
Â· Cold Showers are quite niceâ€¦ once youâ€™re in!

Not so fun things I had to deal with:
Â· Detox the first week (itchy skin, trouble sleeping, skin breakout, scaly legs, nausea) I think this might have been easier for me because of giving up sugar 6 months ago.
Â· Some cooking disasters and wasted food
Â· One day of incredible sleepiness
Â· Everyone predicting that I would get sick, constipated, destroy my liver/kidneys, have no energy.
Â· Bad hangover after the first Wednesday night dinner with my parents (because I ate ice-cream I think)
These things have all gone away now, but I havenâ€™t tested the ice-cream thing to see if I get a hangover. People have stopped telling me Iâ€™m going to â€˜die from my eating disorderâ€™ because I look healthy and they can see itâ€™s working.

Caveman things I plan/hope to do in the nearish future:
Â· Go camping more
Â· Learn to like seafood
Â· Swim in the ocean daily
Â· Graduate from cold showers to ice baths (10 degC water)
Â· Lift heavy things sometimes

What a giant essay... I hope to track things I'm learning and things that work and don't work here just to stop myself getting it arse about in my head and make sure I stay on track. After all, it was reading other people's experiences that helped me put together the pieces, so here's my first step at paying it forward.

### ashryn — 2013-01-03T03:03:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69099>

PS I realise that much of what I wrote about the protocol is incomplete and simplistic, and I am slowly fitting more of the science into my understanding as I read more, but seriously, the first post was long enough, if anyone is reading it for a complete understanding, allow me to point you towards the blog index...

### Charlotte — 2013-01-03T05:59:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69108>

Hi Ashryn, WOW, I am thoroughly impressed. You are a meticulous observer. And WELCOME, happy to have a new contributor and such a knowlegable as that.
I did experience a lot of the same. What also stuck was, that I used to have a huge build-up of skin on and around my feet, which needed extensive pedicure to keep in check, with itchy soles and open wounds (scratching) which made me miserable at times as it made walking a chore. Now, My feet just don't have that skin anymore and I haven't even had pedicure in a very long time. I also had some rheumatoid like pain in my left shoulder which is also completely gone. And lots of other little corrections that sorted themselves out.
So I am looking forward to hearing a lot more from you.

### ashryn — 2013-01-03T07:25:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69112>

Hi Charlotte, thanks for the warm welcome { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

it's a bit sad that I don't \*need\* pedicures each week now.. Bt I figure ill just swap them for something more enjoyable.. Like a massage.

And talk about opening the floodgates.. From silence to my whole life in a couple of hours... I feel an urgent need to write more things.
Here goes;
When I was studying naturopathy, I learned an adage in homoeopathy that has been on my mind a lot whilst reading people's journals... It is about the direction of healing, they say it proceeds from above downward, from within outward, from more important organs to less important organs and in reverse order of their appearance. It seems to me that this is the case with many many people in here, with old symptoms and illnesses cropping up as they walk their paths. (Luckily I've been reasonably healthy thus far, and did some hardcore healing with homoeopathy way back then so I hope I'll get away with only seeing a brief resurgence of eczema and hay fever... I hope. I have always had tight muscles and joint pain and nothing has really sorted that for any length of time but I am hoping the no grains and ct will get that gone too. )

So, this week I have had some weird things coming back that I haven't had for years, hip pain that my parents used to call 'growing pains' (sorted that with spot icing) and cracks under my little toes (no idea what to do about that, a small voice in my head is telling me that's a candida thing, but I might just give it a few days before I go all mad rush to add more treatments!) as a child they got painted with mercurochrome, but I'm pretty sure that stuff doesn't exist or work on humans any more.

What I really wanted to post about now is the amazingness of cold! How did I never work this out for myself? I've been sitting around reading this website for several hours a day with a giant bag of ice strapped to me... (DH and DD thought I was mad, until they saw the results.. They are both a bit miffed that the freezer is so full of ice that they cant fit any bread in... and then they cant have bread (snigger) thees plenty of meat in the house though.) anyway... I got a cool fat burner vest today and DH asked me about how it works, then said he'd like to give it a try.. Eh heh heh... I'll bring him with me on the journey eventually! Oh.. It might have something to do with the oxytocin therapy that ensues after a cold bath...

It is going to be a challenge to ever manage to get water here below 10C... Even at its coldest the ocean doesn't go below 20C. Also there's a drought here.. Not a chance that I can waste that much water filling the bath every day or spending the money on ice... I have convinced the family that we NEED a pool stat. Until then ice bags and cold baths it will be.

Other odd things I have noticed this week:
Food tastes too salty
I can walk much faster than recently
I don't get tongue tied so much when I try to discuss the science of this stuff with people

Things I need to find solutions to:
I am struggling big time with the BAB- it was always hard but doable, and I pushed through because it is clearly the most important part of the reset.. But this week I physically can't put that much food in me. I am going to have to go looking for recipes that include super high protein foods in small quantities.
I bought fish, but after two abortive efforts at cooking the stuff, (probably shouldn't have forced myself to eat it, but I was working on the premise that I'd get used to it if I ate it) I am having trouble trying it again... Maybe next week.

Argh I have neighbours that sound like they are beating each other to death with their children.. I can't concentrate and have to go eavesdrop. Probably I'll be back later with more burning things to write about..

### Charlotte — 2013-01-03T08:43:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69118>

I love when the flood gates open as that is a fountain of information also important to me. So keep pouring it out.
I have never been a big breakfast eater myself, but I ordered me some good protein from this website here. A little tedious, as I have to have it shipped to my son in NJ and he then needs to forward to me. So once it is here I just make me a shake with that, raw eggs and oil. I can drink a lot and it is a good way to get fluids into my body. Also I eat lots of sashimi in the morning. It is so good when it is fresh and with all the fat it goes down very easy. I find it easier to eat BAB when most of it is raw. The cooked stuff often is too dry for my taste.

Okay, CT. I have a suggestion. I bought me a bunch of hot water bottles at the supermarket for real cheap. Filled them with water and put them in the ice chest. I just take them out and use them. Since we have really cold weather now in Germany, I do not need to cool the water down, but to spot ice, they are perfect. If you fill you tub with water and throw the bottles in it gets pretty cold also. I will have to do this when the weather gets warmer here. This way you do not need to buy ice and after you are done just stick them back into the freezer.

Now I must run, as my cake is waiting to be made. Your neighbours sound like a real challenge. When children are involved it gives me the creeps. Thanks that there is a law now in Germany that you cannot beat up children. I become a fury when I see that happen and I go to any length to stop that. Old memories of my own childhood.

### ashryn — 2013-01-03T10:01:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69122>

Argh.. The raw thing. I am sure I will get the eventually, but right now, not a chance.. I will keep it in mind that you need less quantity when it's raw... But.. Ugh. Perhaps when I'm closer to optimal eh?

So.. The food thing. The weight thing..
I started tracking with paleo track because it has all the cool stuff like how many micronutrients in the foods you track, and also as a way to calculate 50g protein, so I really like it, except that I suspect the quality of food between the states and Australia is quite different, also there's foods I don't know the American term for, which is a bit of a pain, not to mention trying to constantly convert measurements between imperial and metric.. All that was ok, but the biggest thing that was doing my head in is knowing the number of calories I am consuming per day.. I am not counting intentionally, but it counts them for me, and I see that I'm up to 2500 or more and freak out. So I have stopped that for a while.. Though I did also learn that I am regularly not getting enough vitamin a or k - not even half of the RDI... So I have been adding in some herbs and baby spinach when it grows in my garden... I also have some spring onions ready to add, so that should sort out those two. Next multivitamin I buy will hopefully include them too.

I'm going to see my doctor next week to convince her to give me all the tests that exist.. My plan is to tell her I'm doing a radical change to my diet and I want a baseline to start from so that I can compare in 6 months and make sure everything is ok.. Pretty sure she will play ball, it isn't her money after all..

The first couple of weeks I was weighing myself daily and that was happy times for the first week at least, then the scales stopped moving. Each day I'd get up, look at the changes going on in my BC and be really excited to see how many more kilos I had lost.. But my scales are clearly broken because they haven't moved even though I've had to dig out old smaller clothes because my others were hanging on me. So my first journalling goal is to open up the "don't be ridiculous" suitcase of clothes in my garage and wear things from it

Ok, so I should record the food here too I suppose... (Though it seems all kinds of obsessive and I want to avoid being so focused on food all the time, I guess it could be valuable to track potential issues, so I'll try)
B: 3 chicken thighs crumbed with coconut flour, turmeric, sesame seed, almond and hazelnut meal fried in co, Bulletproof coffee, enough supplements to make me rattle washed down with water and lemon juice.
L: nothing
D: avocado and tomato salad with lemon juice avocado oil and black pepper and two GF lamb chops
Supplements: milk thistle, women's multivitamin, vitamin d, magnesium, wild salmon oil, glucosamine

Gawd.. That's really enough from me for one day..

### diane — 2013-01-03T12:33:46+0200

Ashryn - that is amazing! All of it! Thank you so much for sharing. And glad you were able to get it all out LOL. I think you're like a lot of us who don't necessarily get all of the scientific stuff - but see the results so know it's working! I can't wait to see your journey!

but WELCOME! Your story is absolutely breathtaking ... except for the fact I HATE you now (just kidding!).

You've done a stellar job of explaining the protocol. I hope you don't mind but I want to email your first post to my sister (and now that I think about, my husband) who is still caught up in the "fat will kill you" syndrome and I can hear her saying to herself when I describe what I'm doing "OMG she's going to die!" LOL.

I'm SO glad you quit lurking and decided to join the rest of us in sharing the journey. WELCOME WELCOME WELCOME!
