### ashryn — 2013-01-03T21:49:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69246>

Ha ha ha Thor!
Diane, I get the science.. But i dont have the words to explain it (what? no words... surely this cant be true i hear you say) and i think translating the science into something practical makes it a whole lot easier to follow and explain to people in a non-evangelical way... the science reminds me of things I learned back in hippie school eg the vitamin d and sunglasses thing- I knew it years ago, and lived by it then gradually forgot about it as I moved away from being a hippie... Also I recently read a brilliant book called the brain that changes itself ( normandoidge.com/normandoidge.com/MAIN.html ) that turns out to be quite old but when I read it I thought it was new.. It started me thinking about the science again, and neuroscience... Actually coursera has a neuroscience 101 course starting this month that I am enrolled in... So like most other things in my life, my science is a bit dusty, but I'm going to give it a bit of a rub and see if I can't start talking the science talk

Darleen, thanks for the hate

I must confess it was your journal I read first all the way through that made me out my lurking self, so thanks.. It might be that I just wanted to talk to someone else that has a rock star connection... For the last 5 years I have been the state editor of a music website called fasterlouder.com.au I just recently gave it up and I think I'm having withdrawals finally... Though I can play several instruments badly, I love to write music reviews and take photos of bands rocking out, and I love finding new local bands before the radio stations do.. Can I ask what was the name of your band? And did you do originals that are recorded somewhere that I can listen to?

DH is in a band and fills my house with original music that is awesome, and I'd love to dust off my vocal chords or an instrument and join him, but... Too shy. Maybe another goal for optimal eh?

Of course you can send my first post to your sister if you think it will help... I suspect it won't since people can only come to this stuff on their own and when they are ready... My approach after spending many years trying to convince people the truth of things is just to be the example.. Answer questions when they ask, but not too fully, and I always caveat with 'this is my experiment... Lets see how it works for me before you try it..' Of course all my people are stubborn and more likely to try something if I tell them not to \*evil grin\*

How I lost weight so fast? Dunno.. I wasn't all that strict the first week, so I suspect it was water weight that I didn't know I had. Add to that the low to no fat diet I have eaten all my life which means I am unable to use carbs as energy, and it makes a good case for getting into keto and staying there because then my body just has to find another source of energy. I must admit the lack of vegetables is making my brain shriek at me that this isn't healthy but I figure I'll just go with it for now and make sure my vitamins and minerals come from supplements until I get reset.

The only dietary omission I struggle with is chocolate.. I used to eat it daily, but it isn't so hard now.. After Xmas our pantry is pretty much made of chocolate, but I haven't gone there. A couple of times after my bab I've thought a piece might be nice, then I think some more and just don't go there.. I do make bulletproof mocha about once a week, (Espresso, two tbsp butter, 1/4cup CO with a teaspoon of raw cacao powder) but after reading the travails of many people on this site who can't lose weight, it seems to me that the common thread in their stories is eating cacao, so I don't go there often.

One other thing about the protocol that I love more than anything else is the stricture on no exercise... w00t! I hate exercise for exercise's sake. Don't get me wrong, I love being active when there is a reason, but it has to be a fun/life reason, not a get thin reason.

Today's food so far totals 2900 calories.. (Damn you paleotrack) that's just the BAB! The only thing that makes this slightly ok is that I won't eat much else today but still..
2 lamb chops (scooped the marrow out of the bones too.. Eww but apparently it's good for me so whatevs)
1 chicken thigh same as yesterday
2 eggs fried in CO
Assorted herbs and things
\=51g protein, 302g fat, 4g carbs... That's 7% protein, 93%fat and 1% carb.. How this isn't going to kill me or make me put on weight I have no idea..

### ashryn — 2013-01-04T21:31:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69381>

Yesterday's lunch was 2 lamb chops, and dinner was some Greek yoghurt with 5grapes a passionfruit and a sliced peach
Went to play board games with friends last nit and drank wine and didn't get to sleep till midnight, but still woke at 5.30am tried to go back to sleep but got hungry!

Today's BAB included 250g steak, 2 strips of bacon, 2 eggs and a handful of mushrooms all cooked in coconut oil with turmeric. Pus a bulletproof coffee. No idea what the numbers are I'll stick it into paleotrack later, it is definitely 50g protein though.

I recently increased my dose of magnesium and have been doing 4 or more hours of CT in multiple sessions and I appear to be getting a bit of detox from my lungs this morning.

I am heading to the beach for a long hard soak in not so cold water shortly.
Sunburn from my idiotic nude sunbathing effort yesterday seems worse this morning despite slathering on CO all day yesterday. I'm going to try tea bags if the beach doesn't soothe it. (Imagine the look on DH's face when I carry not only ice upstairs to my cold bath but a box of tea bags as well!)

### endless — 2013-01-04T21:59:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69384>

4 HOURS of CT??? Wow. I am impressed...I feel accomplished if I manage to fit in 30 minutes!

### ashryn — 2013-01-05T07:29:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69401>

Endless: Ahh yeah but it isn't hardcore CT like others around the place.. Just spot icing and baths with tap temp water, which feels warm to me (summer here)
When we got to the beach today, everyone was standing on the shore and the shark warning siren was going off so we watched for a while hoping they would chase it away.. Eventually they turned off the siren and we went in (but not too far out, DD would never forgive me if I got eaten by a shark) but then they turned the siren on again and we were advised to go no more than ankle deep. Being as how ankle CT is sub-optimal, we decided to drive down the road to the kids and nana beach (no surf, protected by a reef, no rips or blue holes) and after parking in the wrong car park and picking our way like mountain goats through the rocks for 15 mins we spent a pleasant couple of hours soaking, but not getting cold unfortunately.

My sunburn damaged skin is quite painful, especially on the bit of my belly where the stretch marks from pregnancy are worst, which I guess makes sense.. The skin is very thin and papery there so it would damage more easily. I couldn't work out how to keep enough cold tea bags balanced there, so I just chucked 20 bags into the bath and had a bath in cold tea.. CTea \*nyuk nyuk\* No idea if it helped.. Slathered myself in CO afterwards and a couple of times since.. Not sure if its my skin or my clothes absorbing it so fast..

Speaking of skin, my skin seems very dry in general the last couple of days, especially on my hands.. I used to say I had old lady hands, but have been quite happy with how they have been firming up lately, but now the skin seems super thin - I probably wouldn't have noticed except that I got a small scratch on one hand somehow and even though it is shallow, the skin is all inflamed and very red.. I've been keeping it soft with CO but it seems to soak in really fast.

What with coating myself and my food and 'oil pulling' with coconut oil every day, I am really getting through it fast. I did some research to find someone closer to Australia that makes it because of the high carbon cost of transporting it to here from Fiji and I found a company called niugini organics that supports coconut growers in Papua new guinea, i bought some in bulk a couple weeks ago and am onto the second last jar already!

Weighed myself today, no scale movement still, disheartening even though I know they aren't a reliable guide. I took no measurements when I started so all I have to go by are my clothes.. I know I KNOW it doesn't matter.. But it does to me. Today I ate 2 sausages for lunch and some more lamb chops for dinner with stir fried veg and butter.. I shouldn't have eaten the sausages because I really wasn't hungry and wasn't real hungry at 5 when I aim to eat dinner.. So I ate dinner anyway since its a circadian thing and the vagus nerve must be stretched, but I will go back to not bothering with lunch from now on.

I have taken to freezing my wild salmon oil to hopefully prevent it from going rancid.. And I took an extra multivitamin at dinner cos I'm concerned that chronic vit K and A deficiency might be contributing to my skin's current condition.. Prolly need more vit c as well - guess I'm off to the shops tomorrow to get those things sorted..

I am wondering about light cycles in summer because the sky starts to get light here at 4.30 am and we're lucky if we have full dark by 8.30pm at night.. I like the idea of watching the sun rise and set to get the natural light in my brain, but how does that square with needing 4 hours of dark for melatonin secretion, and getting a long enough sleep? (When I'm working I have to get up at 5am, so I wake up with the sun anyway)

I am also wondering how to increase the intensity of my CT (economically) I have the cool fat burner on over a damp t-shirt and 6 kilos of ice packs strapped to my belly right now, and though I feel numb, and it takes about an hour for the iced areas to go back to normal, I don't really feel that shivery cold or have goosebumps... Am I getting cold enough? How can I ger MOAR COLD!?

Ahh that's enough..I have to find some motivation to do the computer work that's waiting for me upstairs.. But it is just so nice to read everyone's stories in the lounge room on my iPad.. Feeling super lazy and inexplicably sad this evening.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-05T09:19:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69404>

Hi Ashryn, so sorry to hear you are sad today. When I read your last post, I thought, wow it would be nice to be on your part of the planet now. I just cannot get warm today. Even CT was a meager event this morning. I just could not tolerate the cold, so after 5 minutes I got out. Today is my first day after over 6 weeks of 500 kacal on hCG and all last week I have been looking forward to my first BAB. Well, was not hungry at all and all I ate was a bowl of bone broth which has been cooking since thursday with some bone marrow and meat. Well, come to think of it. I was so busy yesterday that I forgot to eat. When I remembered, it was already past 5 in the evening, and I did not want to eat so late, so I did not eat anything all day. As a result I could not go to sleep until after 3 in the morning and I woke up early nevertheless, so maybe that is why I am so cold today.
Then when cooking (I am expecting visit today and tomorrow) I took three bites out of a raw milk cheese and 15 minutes later I had a big pressure on my liver. Guess, must get used to the fat slowly. How you can eat so much is beyond me. 2900 kcal just for breakfast, wow. I realize it is easy when you are eating a lot of fat but still. I have to work up to that.
I got up early this morning and went to the quail farm to get eggs and looking at the little quails. How nic,

### diane — 2013-01-05T13:11:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69444>

Hi Ashryn. Here (in Canada) I usually get really dry skin in the winter - and this is pretty much the first time that I haven't. The naturopath had me adding a lot of Omega 3 and fish oil for my dry skin, and it didn't do a thing for me. I figured that something wasn't processing right which was accounting for the dry skin.

Try adding seafood - since I've increased my seafood I haven't had dry skin. I usually eat beef for breakfast then try to make lunch and dinner seafood meals. I may eat other meat for dinner a couple of times a week, but mainly seafood.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-05T14:07:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69450>

OOOH goody! A fellow rock enthusiast. You are lucky to have so much music still in your life. My DH cannot carry a tune in a bucket nor can he manage to move with the beat, LOL. when he used to dance I swore he looked like Ichabod Crane. And he calls ME "grace," LOL.

Our band was named The Legal Limit. Guitar, keyboards, bass, drums, flute/sax and vocals. This is one of those things that really chopped me off at the knees ... the guitarist (a\*\*wipe misogynistic pr\*\*\* that he was and probably still is) broke the band up on purpose just to get rid of me. He figured he could get everyone back together and off they'd go w/o me. Too bad for him the others saw right through what he was doing. It was a real shame because we were just getting some regional notice and could have done well.

No we hadn't gotten to doing our own material yet. But I was starting to dip my toes into songwriting which is probably why he did what he did. He had evidently written some songs and entered a contest or two and had gotten nowhere. Did I already call him an a\*\*wipe? Let me do it again. I let that rejection ruin my love of music for a very long time. It just hurt to even go there. It was like getting a divorce times 5. Have I mentioned I do not handle rejection very well?

Eventually I moved past it. Music may never rule my life like it once did ... I think I have upwards of 400 lp's and at least that many CD's and then there's the stuff I've bought on iTunes and now Spotify. Did I mention I was also a DJ for a local radio station for several years? Yeah, that too. I had "Music is life is music" engraved on my first iPod.

Anyway, returning you now to YOUR journey ...

Hi Diane,
Based on what I now understand about omega 3s (see giant treatise in other thread where I asked if fish oil sups were always bad and Dr K said go and read the blog and work it out yourself) I have been adding too much to my diet, so it's interesting your naturopath had you omit for dry skin.. I suspect it was more to balance ratios than to add more o3. The other thing I learned about that is that your skin needs more o6 for the inflammatory-->healing response in the case of tissue trauma.. I was whining about a scratch on my hand yesterday, and I'm going to taks a guess that I didn't haven the right ratio of o6 for my body to deal with it effectively, hence the extra pain and swelling and slow time to form a scab..
Huh. I love it when something I learn can be directly related to me.. Reminds me that I AM the centre of the universe after all \*grin\*

I hate seafood! But I am trying to acquire a taste for it.. Any and all recipes that trick me into eating seafood without realising it would be gratefully accepted

### ashryn — 2013-01-06T06:33:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69564>

Episode four: In which ashryn whines a whole lot about fish, not being able to cook, or get cold...

I am sitting in the lounge with the air on as low as it will go, belly ice on and ice vest on too. Unfortunately it's stupidly hot so all that ice does is keep me comfortable.. Sigh. Stepped up the process of having a pool installed (ie. hassled DH to pester the dude who is supposed to give us a quote) at this rate I'll have one just in time for the cold weather.

Today I got up late because the sky was overcast which was awesome, then we went to the nana beach again because DH was freaked about the sharks at our normal surfing beach (he said there were 5 of them about 3 metres long, but I figure.. The ocean is where sharks live, right?)

Anyway.. The clouds made it feel quite cold which was a nice change. We stayed for a couple of hours. I am really looking forward to the chance to immerse myself in actual cold water someday.

BAB was leftover meats and BPC pretty uninspiring but it had to be eaten and now it is. Not hungry for lunch, and dinner happened around 5pm... I ATE FISH! Ok, so it was a couple of little tins of tuna in chilli and oil with a handful of spinach and cheese.. Not too bad. I got a bit sick of it after about the first tin (95g) but it didn't make me feel ill, so maybe we can call that progress.. I have a tin of smoked oysters in the pantry but so far haven't been able to bring myself to try them.

I do realise that fresh is better, but fresh requires prep and knowledge, neither of which I have yet, and the smell of cooking fish makes me lose my appetite completely so I'll wait till I am a bit used to it first. I have a tin of salmon in the pantry too - how does one eat that? What's good to mix it with?

I went on a shopping expedition to find DHEA and k2 and a better multivitamin preferably with A and iodine, but not much luck there.. I'm not sure we can buy DHEA in Australia, and the health food lady said you can't get vitamin K here either. When she asked me why I wanted it I forgot what it was for.. So I said I was deficient in it. She was really asking because she didn't know it was used for anything other than to prevent phenylketonuria in newborns (I think) when I didn't give her a good answer she went and dug up her nutrition book and looked up what it was for hahah.

So anyway, I bought dried herbs, kale, spinach, spring onions and broccoli hopefully they will do the vit k trick. Will have to check with paleotrack later to make sure I remembered the right foods to buy. I also found some wakeme, but it tastes very fishy and salty.. Anyone got any tips for hiding that stuff in food?

Maybe I'll just chow down on some fresh seaweed at the beach (seriously.. I could probably do that, except are there any poisonous seaweeds?)

I had my first go at deciphering a blog post today and putting it into a context I could understand. I put it in the nutrition forum http://www.jackkruse.com/forum-2/optimal-nutrition/are-supplemental-fish-oils-always-bad/ hopefully I understood it right. It made me realise how much of the blogs I really haven't taken in, and I might have to start at the beginning and summarise them all (dust off my anatomy and physiology and my organic chemistry and see if I can make it make enough sense that I can explain it back to someone.)

Still haven't found the motivation to go work on the computer upstairs yet. Will need to get onto that tonight. I also need to come up with a plan for my BAB tomorrow since I have finished all the leftovers. Perhaps I'll have another go at fish. It's whiting, how do I cook it? Last time I just fried it in butter but it was too salty and fishy.. And I think I cooked it too long. Any fish cooking experts reading this? (Not you Inger, I'm not brave enough for raw yet!

) I will be one day, I promise.

Also.. How the hell do I prepare kale? Man, I'm such a retard in the kitchen.

Hrm. Not a totally successful day in all.. Seems like the beach was the only thing that went well. Maybe I'll suggest we go again when DH gets home from band practice.

### PrimalPam — 2013-01-06T11:42:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69587>

From one seafood-hater to another, all I can say is, I hear you. I did find a recipe for coconut shrimp (coconut flour, shredded coconut and egg) that sounds good, as a way to hide the taste of the shrimp! I also made a lemon-butter sauce that I froze & haven't used yet that sounds great. Although Dr. K. says not to eat sat. fat (i.e. butter) with seafood....sigh. Next time, I'll make it with Ghee.

I have to force-feed myself my seafood. I buy frozen shrimp, and then I let them thaw in the fridge during the day. When I come home, either I eat them or throw them out. Since the frugal side of me can't stand wasting food, I. Eat. It. I still don't love it, but down the hatch it goes. If I don't thaw it out, it will just stay in the freezer forever.

As to preparing kale, the only way I like it is sauteed in olive oil & garlic. Actually kind of spinach-y this way. Just wash the entire bunch, put it in a pan, sautee till it wilts. It will reduce to about 1/3 the physical size, and it's pretty good this way. Or you can sneak it in salads.

Re: the dry skin. Being epiPaleo, I've had significant improvement. Something else I've done is, instead of lotions and chapsticks, I make my own, which is so much better. Beeswax pellets, coconut oil, and cocoa butter, melted together and formed into little bars (I actually poured into muffin cups). I found the recipe on a coconut oil site; if you want, I can dig it up. Way more moisturizing and no harmful ingredients.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-06T11:59:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69590>

How can you NOT like fish???? We're having mussels and shrimp tonight or as Vickie says, muscles and shrimp.

Canned (hopefully wild caught) salmon ... try this is it so good. Take however much thick sliced bacon you like (I use 3 slices), slice it up into tiny bits and sautee until crisp. You can add in a bit of minced onion if you like. When ready, drain the can of salmon and toss it in (keep heat low so moisture/fat spatters don't hit you) and stir until warm. Pile on your breakfast plate and chow down. EVERYthing is better with bacon.

As to the band, actually I did drag bass player off to Seattle with me (which at the time was a hotbed of rock music) to try and put another band together. Didn't work out. He chose to sit on his ass all day long, do nothing to help locate potential band mates while I, busy little bee that I was, found a temp job during the day and spent my evenings trying to figure out where all the musicians were hiding. I finally gave up because of that and the fact I missed DH and came back home. Oh well.

Oh yeah, and I'm with your hubs on the sharks and the beach thing. Of course, the ocean kinda scares me anyway it is just so overwhelming. I prefer the mountains. Nice and stable and big and strong ... always at your back (or front or side depending on how they and you are oriented).

don't know how you turn off auto-correct on the iPad. I don't use for surfing or email. That's what my computer is for. And I only have the wi-fi version anyway. I don't run around much so saw no need for the 4G version. Mine only gets used at home.

### ashryn — 2013-01-06T21:29:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69674>

Episode 5: in which fish plays a starring role...

Interesting... Fish has lotsa protein but few calories... Can someone remind me what fat to protein ratio we're aiming for? I never thought i'd say this, but i'm not sure how to get more fat... (There's no way i can drink it out of the pan, so don't suggest it!) current ratio is 70% protein to 30%fat...
I ate fish for bab this morning.. I didn't love it, but i ate it. I actually might have overdone the coating, but i was trying to hide the taste.
Defrosted two whiting fillets in the fridge last night, then dipped them in egg and a coating of almond, hazelnut meal, garlic and sesame seeds with some italian mixed herbs and black pepper. Cooked them in some unsalted butter because it had to be used up. I'll get some lard (bacon fat) next time i brave the grocery store and use that in future. Served it with a cup of baby spinach, some cheese and lemon juice with a couple of chunks of polish sausage to make up the protein quotient. Apparently all that was just over 1000 calories but 75g protein so it will be interesting to see if it takes me through to dinner.

Interestingly, eating fish and the green things gets the problem vitamins up, but leaves me low in calcium iron and zinc.. Getting some bacon in me at dinner should sort those out i guess.

Without salmon oil supps my omega 6-3 ratio is 3:1 not too bad i think. Will keep a close eye on my skin and that stupid cut on my hand to see how it is healing.. Added coenzymeQ10, vitamin A and bioC to the daily pharmacy i scarf down, but it looks like i wont need vit A every day if i keep eating the green stuff. I planted the spring onion ends in the garden so i have a steady supply of them in future.

Had some detox stuff this morning, nothing too serious, but a good sign i think, and my rings are falling off my fingers again which makes me happy and sad at the same time. (Actually it makes me want to jump on the scales.. Just to see.. But i shall endeavour to resist!)

CT is cold today, i can feel the fat cells suiciding as i type! It is probably due to the overcast sky today, i am able to get the room cold for a change.

This is my last week of holidays which is quite sad, but it means i need to make that appointment with my doc for the blood tests.. I don't know why i'm procrastinating.. Surely it will be interesting to know... But what if they find something scary? I know.. If they do, then it's better to know early so i can fix it.. But still i procrastinate. JUST DO IT WOMAN!

In other news, my nude sunbathing disaster of a few days ago appears to have resulted in sunburned butt too because today it's peeling! It didn't hurt, so it didn't get the coconut oil love that my belly got, and i don't make a habit of checking out my naked arse in the mirror these days, so i didn't notice, but hahaha! So not telling DH. Belly isn't peeling yet, though it looked like it was going to for a while, the coconut love might have saved it.

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T00:30:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69704>

\_\_Summary of the post leptin reset eating template:\_\_

\*\*I doubt i am reset yet, but in order to be ready when i am, i thought i would summarise the plan for myself so i have it straight in my head. Explaining something back to a person seems to be the best way for me to understand things, so here goes.\*\*

The reset takes 6-8 weeks for most people, and is designed to bypass the leptin system that is currently broken and rewire the hypothalamus to deal with food in another way. It takes 6 weeks of training the hypothalamus to work with the vagus nerve signals and to figure out what to do with the food and cravings. Essentially, the LRx teaches the brain to associate the time of the day with the timing of meals, and to work out the best way to apportion the nutrients to the body. Because it hasn't used this method since we were tiny, it doesn't make the mistakes our leptin receptor pathway does by storing everything. We are initially trained to link light and activity cycles and meal times as foetuses so the ability is innate in our biology, which means that even if you or your mother broke your leptin system, it can be reset as an adult.

\*\*\*Look for the following signs that you are reset:\*\*\*

\*Weight loss, or change in body comp- clothes fit differently
\*Sweat differently- more or less depending on your issues.
\*Increased energy and improved recovery from exercise
\*No hunger or cravings
\*Waking up refreshed

When you see these signs you may add some high intensity interval training if you wish..

NOTES ABOUT WHY:
The way the mother wakes, eats and sleeps during pregnancy trains the hypocretin neurones prior to the leptin receptor developing, and so ultradian rhythms control things like "hormonal release, heart rate, thermoregulation, urination, bowel activity, nostril dilation and appetite."
The body rhythmically releases peptides that stimulate or suppress appetite in time with the cycle of the mother's body. If the mother doesn't sleep well during pregnancy, the foetus has to work out what the time is based on the hormones being released by the mother. (Many of the hormone issues we struggle with as adults are directly tied to what kind of signals were sent from mum during gestation. In particular, these hormones dictate how our leptin receptor will function.) After birth, we have no need of these circuits since we can work out day and night and feeding times for ourselves, so the pathways go dormant, but they don't disappear.

Since the leptin receptor in the hypothalamus is more efficient at interpreting circadian cycles and electrons that arrive at meal times, babies quickly start using this pathway instead. It all starts when the baby gets leptin from colostrum in mothers milk. The receptor is trained by feeding and sleep cycles by repetition (fire/wire)

This all works very well unless the receptor has to deal with inflammation, and then it tries to downregulate by paying less attention to the leptin coming from our food.. This makes us not efficient fat burners but good fat storers (which leads to further inflammation and a whole vicious cycle - leptin resistance)

POST RESET RULES:
Once the leptin receptor is retrained, we keep it functioning properly by eating a low inflammatory diet (paleo template) high in fat moderate protein and low carbs. Some people can go as high as 30% carbs, but YMMV.

\*Breakfast still has to be eaten within 30 minutes of rising and should include 50% of the carbs you will eat that day, plus 25g protein and 25-30g of fat.
\*THOU SHALT NEVER MISS BREAKFAST (bf starts the circadian rhythm for the secretion of gastric acid which we will need later in the day to deal with body comp.)
\*Don't work out before bf because it raises cortisol and breaks the circadian cycle.
\*Be active between 9am and 4pm, look at the sun, walk around and do stuff. Sitting down is bad.
\*if you are hungry at lunch, you can eat 25% of your carbs for that day, skip it if you want.
\*if you want to work out you should do it between 1pm and 5pm... In the sun.
\*Dinner should be 45mins after your afternoon workout (if you did one) and 4hours before you will sleep. Done by 8pm at the latest. (Eating within 45 minutes of a workout triples the amount of protein you make so it gets you 'ripped' faster. If you do it in cold, not only do you build muscle, but you burn fat at an astounding rate.)
\*Dinner should have 25-75g protein, any carbs left over and the rest fats. The best fats for dinner are coconut oil, ghee, pastured butter, and bacon fat because they kill the appetite.
\*Sleep by 11pm at the latest, earlier in autumn and winter. Get 7-8 hours a night and try to wake without the alarm.

WHAT ELSE?
\*If active drop all supplements.. You shouldnt need them with proper eating. If not active, keep CQQ and vitamin d.
\*You can do intermittent fasting now if you want so long as you never miss breakfast.

\*High protein is now eaten at dinner because thats when we're ready to use it. The BAB was just for retraining our brains.
\*Carb intake and choice should follow the seasons.. More carb in summer, less in winter.
\*Sleep is imperative, because thats when we get rid of excess fat and calories. We do this using T3, leptin and uncoupling proteins.
\*Not enough sleep leads to reduced DHEA levels and reduced Growth Hormone release which causes high cortisol (and carb cravings)

Of course some of this has now been updated by the epi paleo template, augmented by cold thermogenisis, but its good to start from somewhere....

Taken from blog post here http://www.jackkruse.com/so-you-completed-the-leptin-rx-what-is-next/

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T06:49:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69723>

Insulin inhibits lipolysis and stimulates lipogenesis. If you have a lot of insulin floating around, you can't burn fat efficiently because insulin blocks hormone sensitive lipase (no cleavage of fatty acid esters can occur). Insulin increases lipogenesis by increasing the concentration of malonyl-CoA, a substrate required for fatty acid synthesis. Too much insulin signals a "fed" state to the brain and fat storage occurs easily. Doesn't matter if the insulin is "spiked" by protein or carbs, the result is the sameâ€¦you store calories as fat.

Stolen from kathylu...

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T09:29:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69727>

Episode 5-1 redux in which ashryn crows about having a good day with fish and CT...

Grr.. Eating more fish tips my omga 6-3 ratio the other way.. Now i am not getting enough o6... Where do we get them from?
Not much on the list that i actually eat... Sunflower seeds maybe i could add to something pine nuts, chicken, sesame seeds, hrm..

I was right about not enough fat in the fish breakfast, I was hungry for lunch today, but I ate a little tin of tuna with lime and chilli and ginger with the oil it came in, and that sorted me till dinner. Also, I LIKED IT! Tomorrow's breakfast is going to be leftover burgers I made with spinach and an avocado but i'll take out the fish and try to have another go at cooking it well.

Heading to the shops to get more tuna tins, scallops and bacon fat plus bacon to wrap the scallops in. I'll get \*fish adapted\* by hook or by crook! (See what i did there?)

I wrote this somewhere else, but I shall record it here too because i'm very pleased with how things worked out today.. Not only did morning CT get me cold, but i wrote a summary of the post reset plan and feel like I understood it... Then, I managed to sync up exercise in the sun with CT and the right timing for exercise (swimming was exercise today because the current was strong and the water over my head 90% of the time) as well as eating in the 45 minute window after exercise that maximises the muscle build and the fat burn!
It may even be possible for me to do this all week too!

In other news, after looking closely at the reset signs, I think I might be nearly there. I'd just like to be able to sleep the whole night through without getting up to pee at 2am. I have been sleeping through a couple of times in a week, but i really really wake feeling refreshed when I don't wake up compared to waking 'refreshed enough' also, when I sleep all the way through I remember my dreams which is exciting for me since I rarely do that. It is hard to break myself of the habit of drinking water just before bed.

I'm not sure what the change in sweating pattern is supposed to be... Sweating is for cooling us down, so we should sweat when it is hot and when we exercise enough to make us hot... Now, I walked to my parents house last week and usually by half way there i'm tired and my feets hurt. Normally DD walks much faster than me but this time I walked faster than either of them.. But it didnt seem like I was working harder than usual, and I wasnt tired half way. When i got there mum said my face was pink, and I felt hot, but I cooled down quickly and didn't notice that I was sweating at all. Its a change.. But I don't know if its the change i'm looking for.

Arg, thats enough.. God i ramble on.

I haven't had cravings for anything except chocolate for well over six months, since i quit sugar, but i have noticed that once ive eaten, i'm done and am not interested in more food even if it is sweets of some kind. I think that counts as appetite has gone? I am still hungry within 5 mins or waking up. And was hungry at lunch today, as i said before.. So not sure about that one...

### ashryn — 2013-01-08T09:55:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69842>

Well, I wasnt going to bother to update today, since not much has changed... Nothing on the scales, yes.. I caved again. Stress about going back to work next week and wanting to have a number to give them when they ask what i have been doing and react in a horrified way to the no carbs revelation.. Ahh well. I am getting impatient for something else to change... I could use some dramatic improvement to keep me motivated to do even better.

Got a haircut today because it has been growing really fast due to the vitamin D I've been getting. The hairdresser was a competitive bodybuilder, so I asked how she eats.. She said high protein low carb, and mentioned a 'protein muffin' that she makes.. I asked what is in it (always looking for ways to get the BAB in) and it was full of carbs! Banana, apple juice, oats... And when I said, I didn't need her to write down the recipe because that is more carbs than I eat in a week and more grains than I've eaten in 6weeks.. She was horrified. Lecturing ensued.

\*sigh\* when I am looking healthy and svelt, i'll get into these conversations with random strangers for sure.. But right now, when I am not finished yet, I don't think i'm a great advertisement for the protocols.

I can't remember who said it on another page, but people do get REALLY emotionally attached to their carbs; it is EXACTLY like an addiction response.

Got most of the way towards making a new CT vest with belly strap, but the icepack shop wasnt open, so thats on hold. ( hopefully it wont be another never to be completed craft project)

In good CT news, dude is coming round tomorrow to valuate the house so we can remortgage for the pool.. Yay!

In good circadian rhythm news, I slept all the way through the night last night and BOUNCED out of bed this morning. Not sure if it was all the swimmingIi did yesterday, the not drinking water before bed, inverting the colours on the ipad so it is white text on black (btw, your avatars look very funny as negative images) or the tap temp cold bath just before sleeping, or if it is a reset sign.. Whatever, I'll take it, and any more where it came from

Went again to the beach this arvo, it wasnt as nice as it has been, but still, I got some cold on.. I even munched on a bit of seaweed, but was too chicken to swallow it.. Maybe when i've done some more research on it.. (Anyone know if you can just harvest seaweed straight out of the ocean as it floats by? Or do you have to cut it off the plant?

I ate tuna and salmon today, bought some lard to cook my next whiting in, couldn't find any scallops though. I liked the tuna, didnt like the salmon.. Then I poured lemon juice on it and managed to finish it.. This fish eating thing makes me get hungry at lunch though, which confuses me. Can someone point me at the blog that will explain how to eat fish and reduce hunger?

Well.. For someone that had not much to say, i said a lot..

### nonchalant — 2013-01-08T12:32:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69862>

Ashryn, about your 03/06 ratio, you don't have to have your daily ratios balance. If you are like me, your body stored lots and lots of oxidized 06's from the various vegetable oils you ate for years trying to follow CW. I think it takes a year or two to really change your body's stored ratios. Your sunburn is probably because of these excess low-quality 06's. Since it is summer for you, I think you should eat all the 03's you can. Then go for more quality 06's in the autumn and winter.

Also, about the seafood being less filling, Dr. K has mentioned that the body knows the value of the DHA O3's in seafood, and will shuttle these important nutrients to the brain and elsewhere as needed. Unless you have been eating seafood for a while, the body will not waste it on mere fuel. I find seafood quite satiating now, but it wasn't earlier.

I plan to make sure the pool is shaded, that should keep it cool. But I think i'm just going to have to accept the fact that I live in a hot place, and take cold (or cool) wherever I can get it.

I have to admit that the ocean is still a cold shock when I get in.. I know that as soon as i have dunked my head it will be fine, not cold at all, but I persist in walking in trying to acclimatise.. Such a baby, I know :/
My dad was telling me that our ocean has a strong cold current running in close to the shore (closer than usual) which is why we've had so many shark attacks lately. Normally they stay west and south of Rottnest because thats where the current usually runs. Of course, my dad often believes anything someone tells him if they are persuasive enough, so.. Maybe grain of salt that one

Your poor doc

maybe he wants a chance to read up on one thing first before you hit him with another! The thing we have to remember is that GPs are like triage.. They are for sick people, and their job is to stop sick people dying by directing the to the appropriate specialist. They don't study health, they study disease.. Specialists on the other hand \*should\* know all about their field and how people came to be in the state they're in so they can recommend appropriate changes... But if they did that too well, they would have no patients!

CT last night after the beach was great.. I shivered and all! Not so much this morning though, but i still did a couple of hours after the house valuer came through. Then later I went for a spot of nudity on the balcony, and the hell with the aeroplanes

I did have an alarming thought though.. What if today was the day google was taking satellite pics of my house for google earth? And now that i have written that, i remember a site... Streetviewfun.com and this http://googlesightseeing.com/2006/11/top-10-naked-people-on-google-earth/
There really is no privacy at all, is there?

Slept right through the night again with no wakings.. Funny, I didn't think sleep was much of an issue for me, but sleeping the whole 8 hours really makes a difference to how I wake up. I love it! Now if i can just get me some awesome dreams like the one Darleen had the other day (seriously, a baby she immediately loved as her own being raised by the whole epiP village? Freaking AWESOME DREAM)

The last dream I remember DH was driving us to the beach along a route we always take, and some dude in a white ute was being a tool behind us.. Instead of getting stressed and angry as he usually does, DH changed lanes to take a different route. Ute guy sped past as soon as we got out of the way, through a red light, realised, jammed on his breaks, lost control and smashed at full speed into a wall. (He was seriously and graphically dead BTW) DH and I were nonplussed..
Me: "argh shit, do we have to hang around and be a witness or something?"
DH: "naah, he's already dead"

Go you dream talkers go!

So I'll be walking to my folks house tonight (it will be interesting to test the new fast-walking-without-getting-tired theory now after another week of healthful eating and CT) Dinner will be late unfortunately, but I have decided to wear my sexy blue blockers tonight and who cares what they think (actually, DH & DD both say they're cute on me, so I'll take that, even if the rest of my people call me a hipster)

### Linz — 2013-01-10T05:51:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70130>

Ashryn, with your question on storing O6s, we don't store O6 and not O3. Most modern diets have a ratio that is far too high in 06 mainly due to using vegetable oils and grain-fed meat, and a lot of it is oxidised before consumption.
From one (or more) of the blogs on CT I think we actually store very different ratios in different tissues as they are required so long as we have a ratio close to 4:1 in our diet. My understanding is that there is a natural seasonal change in these ratios to help cold tolerance and the ratio can be improved rapidly with CT. When you start ct with poor ratios there are some unpleasant detox symptoms if you look at the early CT posts. Face pain with dunking and skin problems were some of the common ones and I think Reynaud-like symptoms. The O6 is not bad, we just have too much and when the right ratios are restored one of the obvious signs is greatly reduced sun burn.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70384>

I know this is going to sound sooo Southern California nutball but no journey is ever a straight line. You might be detoxing, you might be getting a bit of low-carb flu, maybe you aren't getting enough food or fat.

Like I keep telling myself .. change takes time. This isn't going to happen overnight so patience, grasshopper.

### diane — 2013-01-12T12:59:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70400>

Yes, what Darleen said!

To me the journey is about learning what works for you, what doesn't, what changes and what stays the same. I have learned to value the learning rather than get impatient with the results. Because sometimes you miss the little things that are happening - that add up to big ones over time!

### PrimalPam — 2013-01-13T13:05:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70510>

Tuna is so full of mercury, and if you are not able to clear it properly, you could have a metal problem. I was Paleo for 2 years, then epi-Paleo for many months, and had a Genova full nutritional profile done. Almost everything looked awesome, except mercury was so off the charts it was scary and B vitamins were extremely low. My doctor (paleo, gets it) put me on a concoction of B vitamins, as B's can help clear mercury. So I'm wondering what your B vitamin situation is? Are you on supplemental B?

I'd ditch the tuna, as it's such a large fish it eats so many smaller fish and gets so much higher mercury concentration. I know, it was one of the only fish I liked, and I've had to give it up too. It's funny, but I ditched it the first time I read Dr. K. calling canned tuna "the spam of the sea". I'm afraid even the tuna steaks or sushi just has too much mercury, which in my case contributed to my super-excessive levels.

### ashryn — 2013-01-15T08:14:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70837>

Hi Pam, i have lotsa b vitamins, both supplemental and from all the meat i eat, i doubt its that. If i am ever going to develop a tastefor fish, i have to start somewhere, and tuna is about the only thing i can manage, so i was erring on the side of something is better than nothing.

Argh, i don't know.. Maybe this is going to be just like every other thing i've tried.. They work like magic for the first couple of weeks, then bam. Plateau and no change. Maybe its cortisol from work stress compared to being on holidays. I need to learn how to not let myself get stressed at work.. It is really a nice job with supportive team and decent managers doing meaningful work.. I create the stress myself, i just don't know how to not stress..

Ahem.. Anyway, heres an update..
BAB has been going fine, 50 or more o protein within 30 of waking, plus 1/4cup coconut oil for good measure.
Still getting hungry at lunch now.. Going to try adding extra-more coconut oil to brekky tomorrow and see what happens. It is really convenient to not be hungry at lunch time, so i'd like to get that back. Dinners have pretty much been the same as BAB only with some spinach and avocado or some similar veg. Usually dinner happens about 5pm and usually that has me sorted till breakfast, though last night i had to work late on the computer editing photos, and so couldn't use the blue blockers or all my photos would have had their colours skewed. I noticed that my stomach started to growl around 11pm... Which is about when i always used to go get a snack to get me through another hour of work, so thats interesting. luckily that photo editing job was a once off thang..

The fish saga has stalled for the moment. I WILL get back on the fish horse sometime soon.

Cold showers only, at least, no hot water.. I'm not sure the water could fit anyone's definition of cold.. I've drunk colder cups of coffee!
Not so good with the ice belly the last few days, after whatever day it was when i had my little spaz attack here and got too discouraged to bother writing. I have still been wearing the ice vest daily for as long as it stays ice anyway. I'm not sure if it is ToM impending, or not belly icing, or a combination, but today is a fat day and i'm trying not to dwell on it.

Got some new beta blockers, but they give me headaches, and dont seem to block as much blue light as my cheap ones, so i've switched back.
Sleep this week has been ok, but not as optimL as i'd like.. Too much light, wine and visiting. Back to doing all the right things tonight.

On the supplement front, i have added DHEA to my daily regimen, just 33 mg this morning to ease into it, haven't noticed any difference in energy levels, but i'll give it a week.. Uless anyone reckons i should jump straight to the 100mg dose immediately? I'm willing to experiment, hell, maybe it will shake things up and get them moving again.

Shock of my life, my daughter has joined me on the leptin reset journey this week.. She has gone from being the never-eats-breakfast-always tired-late-tv-watching-carb fiend to being up every morning scarfing down meat.. She has even had a go on eggs and mushrooms. Amazing-
She only has a couple of kilos to lose, so hopefully she will feel much better in herself and keep off the carb/sugars when the reset is done.
It is nice to have someone ask me what to do and just trust that i'll tell her the right things, but i'd really like it if she was interested in the whole package, and the reasons why this lifestyle is important.. She keeps calling it a diet, so i don't think she sees it as a way of life. But i have to be cacreful to not bombard her with too much, just have good succinct answers ready when she needs them. My sister is doing the no carb keto thing too, now if i can just convince my mum and dad that grains are what is causing their bellies, i'll be happy.. But i'm not going to evangelise.. Just wait till they ask, and let them see the results on me.. Assuming i see any more results. DH is losing weight just by being near people on the paleo diet.. The bastard!

The pool is going ahead for sure, 6.5% deposit paid, expected to go into the ground in 3 weeks.. Then watch how easy my CT is!
Unfortunately my mad powers of persuasion didn't extend to convincing DH that we should drop several thousand on rollershutters for our bedroom windows, so there is still light leakage through the curtains. I bought a pile of material planning to make something i could block the light with but then i lost my craft mojo and couldnt work out how to make it effective. DH has been doing the manly thing and finding tradesmen to come and finish off all the bits of our house we didnt do before we moved in.. On the weekend we moved all of the timber we ecycled from the house we demolished from storage to the garage, and i noticed i could carry as much as the boys with no trouble at all. I also noticed that i can really see my biceps these days.. (Ahah! Thats why the scales haven't changed in weeks.. All the fat weight is being replaced with biceps!) ----listen woman, Hold on to that change.. Its a change in the right direction, its proof that you're not wasting time and energy here..
Yes, also, now that i think of it, the bathers i wore to the beach today are too big, ( i know this because once they were wet they were hanging off me, and the bit of boobie support they have was loose and thus unsupportive heheh) when they weren't too big last week. GOOD THING NUMBER 2!

I remember now, the way to keep myself positive is to LOOK for and notice the good stuff. Duh. Someone slap me next time i start whining ok?

Right, journal is up to date.. I'm off to summarise the DHEA blog now since i read, understood and acted on it, but can't explain why.. Except that its a cool hormone i want more of..

### ashryn — 2013-01-15T09:36:04+0200

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-15T13:35:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70874>

Clearly you are still on step one

because the day you stop worrying about how much you weigh and become more concerned with the markers that show you are on the way to optimal is the day you have finally gotten "it." don't feel bad about this because it's taken me about a year to get there. this program is NOT about losing weight (my little low-estrogen driven temper tantrum from this past weekend aside) it is about repairing the damage done to your health over however-many years you have been getting it "wrong."

The best advice I can toss your way is to stop worrying about your weight. Focus on how you are feeling. Get the labs done to check your baseline and then rinse and repeat to track your progress. (god I'm starting to sound like Mini-Jack here)

I hear you starting to voice doubts that this will work. Trust me. Seriously ... trust me on this. IT DOES. Ask Vickie, she'll say pretty much the same thing. I feel SO much better than I did a year ago. When doubts start to creep in I go read Jack's story again. I trust what he says because he has BEEN there.

And kudos on your daughter joining you. Leading by example is the best way to convince others.

### endless — 2013-01-15T22:41:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70974>

I would not worry about the mercury issue...eat some tuna when you can and don't stress about it. If you get to the point that you are more comfortable with fish you will probably naturally eat more fresh fish anyway.

I have to agree with Darleen as well....if you read the first pages of my journal you'll see that I was reluctant to start the reset because I knew from reading that some people GAINED weight before they lost it....it's hard to do something that might result in weight gain when you've spent your entire adult life trying to lose weight

I had to decide that it was ok with me if that happened because the end result I was after was not just about weight. So far, that has not happened to me, but if it does I will not freak out about it because I believe that in the long run this is what I need to do.

Sometimes we just have a shitty day and our outlook is crappy too....hope you feel more positive soon!

### ashryn — 2013-01-16T05:48:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71008>

Hrm.. Yes, i know all those things you helpful people are saying.. Thanks, Don't mind me, sometimes I need to 'say it out loud' (or in this case write things publicly) to counter the negative imp on repeat in my brain. Hence finding the good signs at the end of my post. I am not technically counting calories but I do need a tool to make sure I get the required amount of protein for BAB, I eat till I am full and don't opt for the 'low cal' alternative. I don't ever know how many I have consumed till the end of the day when I record it for the purposes of making sure I have enough vitamins and working out how much protein is in the meals I prepare so I know what is the minimum amount I have to eat etc.
...but i forgot that most of your food should be done in the morning. Thats why I was getting hungry when I changed to tuna for breakfast, it doesn't have enough fat to make sure I get half at breakfast.. Hence needing to eat lunch again.

I ate a stupid amount of fat off the lamb roast for breakfast this morning plus 1/4 cup of CO and have only just started to feel hungry 12 hours later..

Like furface, i am keen to continue to see fast results like the first couple of weeks, but being impatient doesn't mean i am going to give up. It just means I don't have exciting news to write in my journal

And it was a fat day because of ToM - i didn't even bother with the scales, they are broken! I just felt like a lump.. I really should be happy about this, since I used to feel like a lump but in so much pain that I was doped up to not feel anything. Even if its a fat day, it has been one with no need for painkillers, an amazing development that I give credit to CT for.

Woah, check out that the semi-defensive self justification... Think i shall put this one to bed and go have a little think about what's been going on with me the last couple of days.. Tomorrow i'll have interesting things to say..

### endless — 2013-01-17T01:43:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71153>

I use avocados liberally to make sure I am getting enough fat......but I also don't plug it into an online counting tool. I have a rough estimate of my most common breakfast foods, so I can get as close to 50 g protein as possible....but the other meal(s) have no pressure, so I don't record them at all.; I am sorta shooting for 50% of my daily intake in those second 1 or 2 meals, and that is all I have to know!

Hasta Manana!

### ashryn — 2013-01-17T07:09:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71184>

blockquote>**diane said**

Ashryn, you have a unique way of writing that makes everything you say interesting!

Cheers Diane, glad I'm entertaining someone!

Endless, I will probably use paleotrack less as I get better at judging protein amounts, but until then it helps me to know i'm on track.. Knowing me, if I wasn't counting and had a slowdown in changes I'd be stressing about it.. And you know, cortisol poisoning

A little bit, I want a record of what I was eating when I lost all my excess weight forever.. For posterity or something, or to prove something to someone one day if I ever need to..

Today's update:
Felt shitty this morning because of ToM cramping, woke at 3.50am in much agony. Decided to give CT a go and got an ice pack elastic strapped to my belly and went back to sleep. It worked... ish. But by the time I had to get up the ice had gone warm and melty and my ovaries were screaming again which left me shaking and unable to think straight. So I took ibuprofen and a tablespoon of disappointment and went to work.

The good news is that I didn't need any more painkillers today, and despite being insanely busy at work, i had focus, patience and insight with an ability to inspire people to join me on my dream conspiracy to upgrade the computer systems.. (Long story, boring)
I am doing some preventative CT as I write this in the hopes that this morning was the worst of it over, and if that's all I have to deal with, I can cope with that for a few months whilst the hormones sort themselves out.

I stopped eating salmon oil a couple of weeks ago, whereas I ate it for the full month previously.. So I took some this morning and will keep going for the month to see how we go.. Then stop for a month to test what happens..

I have been asked to head a huge project at work despite having little experience in it, and it makes me feel like people have recognised my competence and trust me to do a good job, this makes me feel very positive. I want to attribute it to my brain working better that I am not terrified ( ok, i am a bit, but it isn't paralysing me for a change) I don't feel the need to point out my uncertainties or shortcomings as much, and this, more than anything makes me feel GOOD!

PS feeling positive or happy about anything on day 2 of ToM is unheard of for me, so... Shh if you think i haven't been doing this long enough for my brain to be healing. Just shh.

And now a curious thing about which I would welcome speculation...
I have long suspected PCOS but never had it checked, tbh, I kind of liked an excuse to zonk myself out with codeine 6 nights of every month and sleep well - I sleep well most nights now, so it's a nonissue. Several times in the past I have wondered if I could feel lumps in my abdomen where my ovaries live, but haven't really taken myself seriously since I do have a tendency to be a bit of a hypochondriac, but now that my belly is shrinking, I can actually see a slight lump on one side, compared to the other side which is flatter. The lump is about fist sized, and stands out about a centimetre different to the other side. I'm not overly worried since it has probably been there hidden by fat for years, but I am curious to know if anyone has any ideas about this.. When you grow cysts on your ovaries, can you see them from outside? It doesn't feel any different to touch.

For the record, I fully expect that if this is PCOS, my CT and epi paleo will sort it, so I don't feel the need to get 'checked out' unless the ToM thing gets worse again..

More odd things...
I have some kind of infection in my belly button.. I noticed this morning the skin inside is very red and angry.. It may well have been like this a long time and Ive never noticed because I couldn't see it hahaha. Salt water? Tea tree oil? Leave it alone and let it sort itself out?

I walked shoeless to mum and dads for dinner last night.. Bless them, DH & DD took 5 whole minutes to ask me why I was carrying my shoes

I told them I was earthing and recharging my negative electrons. They said ok. The words nutjob and crazy only appeared twice during the rest of the walk as well! I think they might be very entertained by my newfound eccentricity. I know I am!

Now off to catch up on all your journalling too!

### Charlotte — 2013-01-17T07:20:16+0200

### ashryn — 2013-01-17T08:16:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71191>

Notes on melatonin

It is the third most important hormone after DHEA and oxytosin.
We make melatonin from tryptophan, and it is only released after 4 hours of dark, as long as there is no insulin floating around.
We need it to repair and renew our cells, reduce oxidation, take care of the heart, eyes and gut. It helps our body repair damage that would lead to diabetes, heart disease and cancer. Its also involved with immunity, hair growth libido, blood pressure, weight loss, bone maintenance, and lots of other things.

Tryptophan is the least common amino acid in our diet, and difficult to absorb because it needs to compete with other amino acids for absorbtion.

### ashryn — 2013-01-17T09:11:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71197>

Notes on grounding blog

Grounding is about making contact with the earth for the purposes of recharging your negative electrons. The earth has lots of negative, and bodies have progressively more positively charged electrons owing to insulating shoes/clothes and increasing Electro-magnetic frequencies generated by all our awesome tech.

You do it by sticking both feet sans shoes on the ground. The idea is that negatively charged electrons will move from a place of abundance (earth) to a place of scarcity (body) until they are balanced.
Sticking a conductive rod into the earth and attaching it to something conductive (body, ipad, kettle) works too.

This was proved in a 2005 study by Applewhite.

The charge lasts longer when its cold. You are more conductive when wet.
The ocean is a good option for grounding because there is cold, wet and the earth.

EMF
The power grid produces EMF of 60hz, way higher than the earth. Our buildings have powerlines running through them that produce these frequencies even when nothing is plugged into them. Much like running the blender interferes with the TV reception, these frequencies can cause interference in our body's electrical signals.

Reactions with electrons and EMF in ungrounded bodies creates "unnatural molecular perturbations" grounding shields us from this effect. This was also shown by the Applewhite study.

"An EMF has both an electric and a magnetic field, and the difference between the two and their effects on the body are determined by the bodies current energy supply of free electrons from food and their own ATP production in mitochondria."

A university study found that exposure to electrical energies for long periods increased the risk of stress, illness and degeneration as well as reducing oxygen uptake and energy levels. Insulated and synthetic clothing makes this worse

The SCN in the brain uses the earth's EMF to know what time it is so it can release the appropriate chemicals when we need them. For example, the mitochondria use these signals to create ATP. This creation stops in a positively charged environment, so the more positive charge we build up, the less energy we produce. EMF from our gadgets is so loud it interferes with the reception and our brains become deaf to the earth's signalling. If you are earthed, emf charges coming at you from anywhere else are negated.

The SCN gets signals from light and temp through the retina, which tell the pineal gland when to produce melatonin where there is enough ATP to do so.
The SCN also has the same kinds of cells found in birds, bees and bats that pick up the earth's magnetic frequency, so it stands to reason that we also pick up the earth's magnetic frequency with these cells.

If we can't pick up the earth's frequencies, and we can't tell night from day because of our lights, and our hormone signalling is out of whack because of these things, then we can't produce the right chemicals to make us function in an optimal way. The longer this goes on the harder it is to remain healthy.

### ashryn — 2013-01-19T08:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71574>

Ok, update time..
Feeling a bit crap today... I hijacked someones journal to chime in to ask about how much Mg we should be aiming at and got the "up to bowel tolerance" response.. I have found it. 1200 mg morning and night is too much. Wll drop down to 600 morning and 1200 night and see how I go...
(Damn I'm punny)

Couldn't finish BAB , but I almost made it.. I was about 6grams off, which I put in the fridge to eat later when I got hungry... It has been 12 hours, and no sign of hungry yet.. Honestly, this is most confusing. Is this a sign that I am reset, or just a response to the Mg overdose...

Apart from a few hours of bad on day 2, cramps have pretty much disappeared, which spins me out, DD just came to tell me that she has had the same pleasing results, after eating this way for just a week. She did spend 4 hours in a cool bath though. Most interesting.

Was really tired after staying up late with friends last night, despite sleeping 8 hours with no wake-ups, so I went for a nap around 2.30 and slept 3 hours.. In all a pretty wasted day, but I'm going to chalk it up to healing and go with that. DD and I had a good talk about stuff, and how well she is doing, and she said she thinks she will stay on the paleo thing after she has done the reset because she feels better and doesn't really miss the foods she though she would miss all that much. This makes me so happy! We have decided to both go to the doctor and make her agree to send us for all the recommended tests.. I just have to come up with good reasons to give her. I may have to trawl the labs threads to see what I can find to relate to my health..

I am on my second go of CT as I write this, it is quite addictive, and i LOVE the way my belly feels when it is all numb and frozen firm after CT. So what if it doesn't LOOK nice (yet), I just don't look, but I find myself rubbing my belly like a pregnant woman, (which probably looks odd) and imagining that firmness is what its always like... This is as good as positive self talk mumbo jumbo, right?

I have been quite entertained by the grounding craze this week, mostly because my crazy conspiracy theory uncle told me about it years ago (which made me skeptical, of course, contrary thing that I am) but the idea has always stayed with me, and as a consequence, i am amd have always been mostly barefoot whenever i am home... Taking off my shoes is the very first thing I do when I get home from anywhere, some days i don't even make it through the car drive home! As with all of my contradictions, I absolutely LOVE shoes, and cannot walk past a shoe shop without stopping, I have been called Imelda Marcos on occasion.. I just don't like to wear them if I don't have to

Anyway, I have no idea if my new tiled floor is earthed enough to do any good, but I have always chucked a pile of salt into my baths, and use salt to exfoliate the super-deep ingrown hairs I get so I reckon intuition might have got me some way towards good health, now I have to go do some more research to see what I can do to make it more effective..

And just a word of warning... I am about to begin a philosophy course on coursera, so my posts for the next couple of months are likely to be a bit on the thoughtful side..

### ashryn — 2013-01-20T06:25:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71775>

Oh dear.. My poor belly skin must hate me.. It has just recovered from sunburn, and now I've given it ice-burn. Not sure why it burned today and hasnt any other day... Could it be related to still having salt on my skin from this morning's swim?

I guess I'll have to ease up on the bellyicing for a couple of days.

After feeling vaguely unwell all day yesterday, and not at all hungry, I ate 1slice bacon for dinner (because I figured I should eat more than one time in 24 hours.. Then I wasn't hungry for breakfast either. I still ate it though, and just got hungry at 3pm.. I ate some hardboiled eggs because I didnt feel like eating any meat, I guess I'll call that dinner. Is this what the good Doc means when he says hunger is gone when you're reset? Could it be possible? Eep! What do I do now?

I read back over the post reset eating thing but I'm still not sure whether to start on that now. What will happen if I am not reset and start eating that way? Minus the carbs of course, because I still have weight to lose. Is there any post reset people reading this who have advice?

Maybe I should give it a couple more weeks before I decide? I really like the idea of only having 25 grams of protein for breakfast though.

BAB today was a steak and 200g mushrooms cooked in lard and seasoned with turmeric.
Dinner was 4 hardboiled eggs with pink salt and black pepper.
Earthed and CT at the beach for 2 hours, tried to go to the surf beach but everyone was out of the water again because of sharks. Damn they are getting annoying.

Spot iced and CFB vest for 2 hours whilst I caught up on forum posts, the washing and the EMF film

Last night I had itchy legs again so I did the cold bath with salt and slept like a baby.. A baby who sleeps well. Actually my skin is looking pretty crap this week.. I wonder if it is shedding the nasty layer to make way for lovely childlike skin.. One can only hope..

Perhaps I will keep on the LRx until optimal reset starts, do the 90 day thing and then reassess. Yes, that seems logical.

Then reading all of Dr. Kruses blogs on which I have not caught up yet, plus the forum and my own research, I am busy from early morning till late evening. But I would love to read what you have learned.

Neuroscience is very interesting. My friend, who visited me last night is a doctor of psychology and does a lot of Trauma work after Peter Levine and Manfred Spitzer. She is very much into neuroscience as this is a big part of her work and what she tells me is very very interesting. Actually we were discussing this morning, how epi-paleo could support her work with her clients as trauma seems to shut down the endocrinological system at some level. ### Charlotte — 2013-01-21T06:31:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71986>

I understand, Ashryn. I thought you were working in this field.

My job is not limited to 8 or 9 hrs per day but often double that but never less than 12 hrs. per day. So I have to be very selective what I do with the limited spare hrs. I have. So I prefer these days to stick with facts, which good science provides and stay away from what philosphers think about life. To me philosophy (and religion) are opinions and beliefs (I may be wrong, who knows, as I can only base any of my own opinions of what I know now). I have read a lot of philosophy and in the end realized (this is just for me and I do not claim that this necessarily goes for anybody else), that there is nothing there that has not been thought before and the one has this way of thinking and the other the opposite and they both have good arguments with which they back it. Somehow, this all seems to me in the realm of fiction. I make a distinction between the brain and the mind. I like to use the brain for practicalities, not to use my brain to entertain my mind. That is why I want to optimize my brain (there is the true intelligence, it knows what to do without me, actually much better without me, all I have to do is give it the best nutrition and conditions), but my mind I do not trust.

Now, I just want to think for myself and my daily life observations give me ample food for thought. Since I do not even take my own opinions seriously anymore I became a little wary of the others. There are 7 Billion people on this planet who have every day opinions about the world, life, the neighbour, wheather, you name it.

I feel the need to explain that more closely as I do not want you to misunderstand me. One of my best friends is a vegetarian and she has a lot of opinions about that. If she could back it up with hard science I would consider, but she cannot so I just feel she can do as she sees fit and I help to reduce the damage as much as I can. But I am not going into dialogue with her about that, it is futile. She does not care about the facts and I do not care about her opinion. But facts intrigue me.

I really hope that it does not sound harsh, sometimes I come across like that, and please do not take it personal, it is not meant in that way. See it as an attempt to explain where I am at right now and what goes on in my mind (which I do not take seriously

)

### ashryn — 2013-01-21T10:33:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-72015>

It does sound harsh.. But 'm not offended.

I do have to reply though because I feel more strongly about what you said than I expected... And because this is my journal!

It feels like you're scolding me for wanting to stretch my mind to consider ideas I have never considered before.. (Because even if someone else has thought about these things, I haven't thought them yet.) If I wasn't the kind of person to be fascinated by ideas, I would never have made it to this site. I would never have even started looking.

I disagree that philosophy and religion can be used interchangeably, philosophy is about embracing ideas, religion is about controlling and restricting ideas to a specific set. Sure, the 'great' philosophers had set ideas about things that they believed wholly, but studying them isn't about learning and following their dogma, it is about exploring their ideas and using them to understand (or try to) something about humans, human nature and our world.

I also don't make a distinction between my mind, brain or body, I think they are all intricately connected in a way we don't understand, and I doubt we ever will undstand if we don't think, ponder, ruminate and explore any and all ideas. If we force a distinction, how do we know we aren't missing something?

Here's a thought... Since Descartes, we have thought there was a distinction between mind and brain, and it led neuroscience to believe (dogmatically, despite evidence to the contrary) that certain parts of the brain were responsible for specific functions. Remove the part of the brain that sees, and the person will never see, even if they have functioning eyes... They now know this to be untrue, and that you can make new brain pathways. This has only been accepted in the industry for a relatively short time, though the thinkers, the explorers in the field had proven it over and over again.. But the BRAINS of the establishment refused to see the patterns, accept the evidence, or even consider the possibility that what they KNEW could be wrong.

This story is common.. Look at CW.. Look at the patterns Dr K makes... Look at the things he links up because he thinks and philosophises about things... Look at the things You know are true that other people KNOW are not.. Who is right? How do you know you have read the right science? How do you know they haven't missed something?

I'm not suggesting you should go do philosophy if you aren't interested, (in fact i was just being encouraging because it sounded like you wished you could study some more) but perhaps you might consider that people thinking (and learning how to think) might have a contribution to make to our society, and not hate on it so vehemently.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-21T11:42:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-72036>

sorry Ashryn, it was never my intention to scold you, I knew you meant well and that is why I said not to take it personally because I was afraid that might happen, when I explain and I do not hate it at all, I am just not interested for my own reasons, but I like the idea of you studying it, if that is what you like doing and I could see that you were excited about it. But I guess I missed my point. There is a distinction between brain and mind as a computer hardware and a computer software are not the same. The software is the interpreter of what the hardware receives. And yes, they are a unit in some way, but my idea is why not let the hardware receive without the software interpreting everything.
Just looked up the german word for "Verstand" and it gives different translations. Maybe I used the wrong one and I should say rather intellect than mind, but mind was the first one that came up on leo.
So what I mean is for example: we see a flower and immediately we give it a name. Ah this is a rose, but could we not, instead, just enjoy the beauty of the color, shape, the smell, its leafs, the softness and leave it there? The message (color, shape, etc.) is received by the brain, but it is interpreted by the mind. I had the best moments in my life when my mind was quiet and I was just taking in what I saw without interpretation. True peacefulness.
So for me now, I rather want to improve this quality of receiving without interpretation.
I guess I made a bad job at explaining, I now hope that I could do it better.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-21T11:52:31+0200
