\[waves at Ashryn\] ... a fellow traveler!!! How cool! Nice to know there are more out there with a similar mindset to my own. Makes me feel less lonely. THANKS!

### ashryn — 2013-01-27T15:17:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-72853>

Argh.. It's been a while since I have updated, mainly because I don't have anything interesting to tell. Nothing much is going on with me getting healthy, still sleeping well, still eating BAB getting hungry 10 minutes after waking and around 4pm most days. Plenty of energy, and feeling a bit more capable in general, but still shitty at the lack of weight movement..

yes yes YES! I know what you're going to say, but it doesn't make any difference. And you can tease and bully me all you like for 'still being at the first stage' I am still shitty that I am doing the right things and am not skinny enough yet.

The EMF2 blog was a bit of a letdown I feel, because whilst I believe how important Schumann resonance is, and the benefit/urgency of ensuring electron balance, I felt that the blog would do more to convince people to stop reading into the topic because of the hyperactive tone and constant repetitive insistence that it was true, without a proper explanation. I don't think simply repeating e=mc2 over and over is an effective way to convince or communicate. I get that it was a source of inspiration, but I wish the mechanics had been better explained in more scientific terms.

Gah. I'm probably going to get flamed to death for saying it, but using science words does not make it science. Calling a horse's tail a leg doesn't mean it has five legs...

i understand the inspiration and the metaphor, and the need to pick something no one can argue with to use as a base for an argument, but I am far more convinced by reading the experiences of people exposed to artificial light or wifi, or night shift workers when it is combined with scientific studies that explain the role of melatonin ( for example) i am far more convinced when I read case studies jack has conducted to test his theories than metaphysical insistence that the end of the world is nigh.

And I am convinced.. Just not by the blog.. The first one had me making all kinds of changes, taking extra opportunities for grounding, reexamining my WoL to find more ways to shield myself, congratulating myself on the many things i am already doing right...

I love all the things I have learned since reading this site, and rediscovering the things I once knew and somehow forgot, I truly feel like Dr K has saved me from a life of increasing degeneration and early death, and has freely given me the tools I need to take back control of my life.. I want his ideas to change the world, I want him to be recognised for his genius, and I am afraid that the presentation of the ideas is going to get in the way of that. I would like to help if I could, after all, he has helped me, and I have been able to pass on enough of the ideas to help others whom I love.
I fervently appreciate the opportunities I have had thanks to this website.

I have redoubled my efforts to get 'fish adapted' and cooked some whiting in foil with ghee, garlic and lemon for dinner. It was edible, though I suspect someone else would have cooked it better.

I have been experimenting with my TENS machine and have found that I can set it to 8hz, which is close to the magic number.. It makes me feel energised, but is it replenishing electrons? No idea. The fact that it is brilliant for chronic pain management suggests to me that it is, since the painkilling effect lasts several hours after using it, and longer after each session. (I don't have any pain at the moment to test it on, but this is true based on past experience from a chronic back injury) DH has a 'sound and light machine' that pulses light in time with the frequency of beats it hears, and he has come up with a way to make it pulse in response to binaural beats in Schumann resonance. (You have to mix a binaural beat track into both channels to make the light pulse properly) we haven't tried it out yet, but maybe using it on the grass outside will increase the earthing effect... Who knows?

I am interested in TCS now too, especially since the existing tens unit i have can be used for that.. But I can't find any info on whether the frequency has to be precise, or if anything that pulses electricity into your brain will do to increase electrons. It sounds like that's what jack is saying... How do you tell if its a negative or positive charge?

### diane — 2013-01-27T15:59:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-72862>

Hi Ashryn! I don't think that anyone is trying to tease and bully you - they're just trying to help. But it's fine for you feel shitty about your weight - those are your feelings and you have to experience them. I'm not saying it's not frustrating, cause it is - and I'm not trying to convince you to not have them. Just saying I understand.

### ashryn — 2013-01-29T11:19:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73129>

Yesterday I ate a 'paleo pizza' made with a batter base made from coconut flour and eggs.. Probably too many carbs in it anyway, since I later ate a couple of biscuits from a Xmas present. Sadly, it wasn't even worth it. If they had been delicious, I wouldn't regret it.. Lesson learned I suppose.

Still, considering its the first sugary carb thing I have eaten since December 20 (ish- tbh I can't remember the date is started doing this properly) I don't think it will put me off the rails. I expect to be back in keto tomorrow if I'm not already.

I have been quite sleepy the last three days, needing a nap in the afternoon on Sunday and Monday, but I also got my front garden sorted yesterday with 28 bags of mulch and a bunch of plants and some additional reticulation, so even the naps didnt stop me for long. I noticed that I had no trouble at all picking up 50kg bags to load them into the trolley, into and out of the car and then out to the garden, I'm sure in the past that sort of thing would have left me tired and sore, but I had no ill effects from it. (The nap I had before the mulch work.)

Shortly I am going out to sit in the light of the setting sun to weed the lawn for a bit and get some grounding at the same time.

I ate lunch yesterday then wasn't hungry for dinner, and was still not hungry for breakfast this morning. I made bacon wrapped scallops as recommended by many of the fish lovers, but they were yucky, and I could only choke down 3 of the 4 I made. As a consequence I got hungry for lunch.

So... I have a bunch of frozen wild caught scallops still in the freezer.. What else can I do with them? Preferably something that hides their taste. - I retasted breakfast for hours today

For lunch I mixed up tuna, salmon and cream cheese, stuck it in a rammikin with broccoli and cauliflower mash on top and a sprinkling of cheese and black pepper and am calling it sea-shepherd's pie.. It's actually ok. I have another one heating in the oven for dinner as I write this. I'm not hungry at all, but I assume I should eat anyway.. Shouldn't I? It eats a bit messed up when I have days off because I want to use the time that DH & DD are out to experiment with fishy cooking.. So there is lunch when I probably don't need it and dinner so I don't get hungry later.

Next time I will use just cauliflower in the mash, and make sure i get a real (not frozen) cauli, but for some reason I was ultra-vague today and forgot to buy it twice, then my local grocery store didn't have any real ones.. I have a suspicion that they might be out of season because they source all their fresh produce locally. I am looking forward to trying a slow cooked shepherd's pie with the cauli mash.. Should be awesome.

I am actually struggling to come up with interesting things to cook because I'm just not really hungry.

Been a bit slack on the CT after the ice burning incident, partly because I am letting the burn heal, and partly because now the CFB seems to be hurting where it sits on my collarbones, and sort of burns on my back. Whilst I am happy that I have collarbones to hurt, I am wondering why CT is painful on my skin all of a sudden... Any ideas?

### ashryn — 2013-01-29T13:28:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73135>

Lawn is weeded dude.. Sitting on the grass in the sun gives me too much time to think, so now I have more musings i wish to record here..
About being pissed that I am not getting skinnier..
Everyone says its about getting health sorted first, but I feel like mine is sorted mostly. I mean, I had some hormonal issues clearly, since the painful periods, but they have gone away, and I sleep well and all night these days and don't get cravings for food or energy crashes, and I don't have any long term illnesses.. Except fatness. I am seeing changes in my strength and energy, stamina for walking or doing gardening, I even feel more confident in myself, so why am I not losing weight now?

Reading everyone's journals just makes me more depressed because everyone keeps finding more things wrong with them, and adding more and more supplements and gadgets to their routine, and I begin to wonder whether we are all trooping up the garden path.

Did anyone start off not so unwell and get the right results in the 6-8 weeks initially estimated? Apparently calories don't count when you produce the right amount of melatonin at the right time because it kicks off the chain that will ultimately result in burning all the excess off.. This makes sense to me, except that I'm not burning off any of the stored fat any more. How do I make it start again?

One of the blogs says that as you get reset you naturally eat less, so it seems to me that perhaps I should just not eat when I'm not hungry, and stop trying to eat till I am full at all meals. Is the no IF rule just to prevent craving/bingeing? That's generally not an issue for me anyway.

\*sigh\*

### NittDan — 2013-01-29T13:47:18+0200

### Charlotte — 2013-01-29T15:25:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73139>

Hi Ashryn, I was logged out since the change of the forum, so I could not post. I wish there was something I could say to ease your discomfort which I can understand clearly. For what my personal opinion is worth:
Like you, I am strong, do not have a lot of health issues (thats what I think, maybe Dr. Kruse is of a different opinion, my doctor, however, thinks I am doing great). I am just fat, not so much anymore though. I do not have the patience like Darleen to hang in there for year and maybe another and then maybe lose the weight I need and want to lose. So I have my own approach which is to lose the weight now and then worry about the other issues later. At least I do not have to suffer that way, as my weight is going in the right direction. Coming off my 2nd hCG round I noticed that I still have leptin resistance as well as insulin resistance, but it is still better than it used to be. So this is moving in the right direction also. And even though I know that this is not the approach that Dr. Kruse prefers, I do prefer it that way and it suits me. And oh, it feels so good to lose that weight. I got really confused for a while reading on this website and was trying to mix what I have been doing sucessfully with all the new insights I gained here just to realize that this did not work well except throw me off. So back to square one for me. hCG until I have lost all the weight, then leptin reset and then all else. One thing at a time. I guess, what I want to say is, when you have made up your mind and realized what works for you, stick with it and do not get discouraged. Watch what is happening and adjust. Maybe a good step is like you said, not eat when you are not hungry and stop when you are not hungry anymore.

### diane — 2013-01-29T19:55:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73180>

Ashryn, I think it's all about context and what works for you. Darleen's journey is her journey - Charlotte's is hers, neither is the same, nor is it better or worse in my view. They have each gotten to where they are through different paths and the one that's right for them. It's for you to find yours.

I'm starting to think that the body is like an instrument panel. Sometimes one light has to come on before the next one does, sometimes multiple come on at the same time. And sometimes turning one on turns others off, so you have to figure out which one gets them back. I could be wrong - that's just my view. It's all very complex - that I know for sure! But from what I've seen from people here, when start with the foundational things, certain things will change. They will be different from person to person. From there, you can see what changes and what doesn't, and then figure out where you need to go next. Which is my naturopath's approach, and one that really resonated with me. Again, just my observation and I could be wrong.

Where I've gotten in my journey is that when I just concentrate on losing weight - I know I screw things up for myself down the line, and it comes back plus more, and then makes it harder the next time. And that's not just through the SAD diets - thats' from paleo too. But that's my journey, and so I'm trying to turn on a bunch of panel lights, then work on the weight.

### ashryn — 2013-01-30T15:48:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73338>

Update time... Thank you all for being encouraging.. I will keep on powering on regardless of how impatient I get, because i DID lose some weight, and I have plenty of energy (mostly) and despite the suspicion that I have turned into a crazy bitch with my sunglasses at night, ice cuddling and barefoot walks on the street, I do feel better, sleep better, think better etc.
Diane: I agree it is about my personal context, but I would like to deal with it from the vantage of another dress size down!
Charlotte: I don't think HGC is for me.. Prior to finding this stuff, I had been 6 months on VLC of 700 calories per day, and whilst I know there is more to that protocol than shakes and soup meal replacements, I never want to go back to trying to deal with that again... And I do have some concern about what it will do to my long term health what with me having already broken my metabolism with other things in the past. It is good to know its there though and that many people have had great success with it.. I shall give that a go before i sign up for gastric banding surgery or something!

Gadgets:
I just read the MDA blog on earthing and all of the comments.. Interesting. Someone's idea that you could make an earthing sheet out of an electric blanket has me interested.. I won one at a quiz night once, but have never used it. I wonder if I still have it. Actually on second thoughts it wouldn't work because the wires in the blanket would be insulated.. I shall consider it some more before I decide whether to add a little bit more crazy to the mix

some of those MDA forum posters are total wankers, aren't they? Mark was all balanced and curious, and then you have all these other people (who are already following what everyone else considers to be a whack job lifestyle, lets remember) jumping in and insisting that the science isn't right... "I have a PDH in physics, and a masters in electrical engineering" oh, really.

i did have a play with my TENS unit at 8hz for a while, but didn't notice a difference... Probably needs tombe for longer, or more regularly, or to have controlled all my contexts  for it to me more precise as a test though.. By the way, these things (TENS-transcutaneous electrical neural stimulator) are magic for pain relief-no idea if it has anything to do with electrons or whatever, but musculoskeletal pain is sorted out really fast. (And yeah, apparently people DO use them as a BoB!)

I haven't gotten around to finding DH's sound and light machine that pulses Schumann frequency of light and sound into your head, but I will give it a shot when I find it.

Today:
the fish odyssey continues...
for breakfast, I ate 250g tuna with ginger, chilli and lime. 'twas ok even if it was in sunflower oil. Certainly it was a fast BAB. made sure to add a good dollop of CO to my coffee and lasted through lunch with just a little twinge around 2pm which vanished in a few minutes. Usually I'm starving by the time I get home at 4pm, but today i was good till 5.

Cooked lamb chops with fresh rosemary for DD and leftover breakfasts, and ate a couple of pieces of fish (it was frozen, in a packet with some kind of sauce of evil on it (soy, some kind of thickener guar gum... All artificial etc) it was edible though, even tasty, but next time I'll look for something less full of plastic coating. I had it with a big fat spinach salad covered in avocado oil and lemon with cheese and avocado and tomato. That was also tasty, and there is some left for tomorrow! I figure I have to start somewhere with fish, and the frozen prepackaged shit just might get me acclimatised to the fish concept, so I am going with that and assuming some is better than none, even if its nothing resembling 'optimal'

...speaking of optimal, I have had occasion several times this last week to use the word optimal in some very professional sounding reports. I have this little imagining that someone will read the word and know what we REALLY mean when we say optimal, and it will be like I have added an in-joke just for them...

For the first time since starting this thing (afaicr) I am feeling peckish now. Probably the fat free processed evilness of the fish sauce. Damn. I went out of my way to add extra fats to dinner too. \*edit\* actually, I think I felt this one night when I had to stay up late and do some colour sensitive photo editing\*

I speaking of crazy.. I had to open a new jar of CO whilst I was cooking, (does anyone else love the fact that if you spill it on you when you're pouring it out of the jar you just rub it into your skin?) and the oil had spilt and filled up the plastic that seals the jar for shipping. DH &DD stood around watching me try to work out a way to not coat the kitchen in CO. I ended up slathering it all over myself which normally would have been fine, except I was already well moisturised thankyou, and I was standing in a hot kitchen with the cooking.. I got so incredibly, uncomfortably whiningly hot and sticky I had to air condition myself under the vent as cold as it would go! Hilarity (for my family anyway) ensued. God help me when there is menopause..

next I did some CFB time without the belly icing because the ice burn is still scabby and tender (yah, more crazy-DH was asking if it has turned black yet.. Yes, gangrene belly... Maybe it will fall off!) and now my regime of madness is complete, except for going to bed soon.

Yesterday I spent several hours sitting on the grass, and went to bed at 9pm.. Slept all the way through till 5 but was really groggy on waking.. I could have slept another hour I think. I haven't felt that stumbly in the morning for ages. I wonder what that was about.

No parental dinner tonight because my dad has ruptured an Achilles' tendon and was waiting on the orthopaedic surgeon's pleasure. I convinced him that ice might prevent him from needing surgery, but since it is in a cast its a bit hard to ice the injury, all he can do is ice above the knee. It will be interesting to see what the surgeon has to say.

Oh my Science! some days I just rabbit on like a three year old on red cordial.. If you made it to the end, congratulations.. Or commiserations!

feeling a bit bloated in the tummy the last couple of days.. Need to go back to basics I think.. I ate some 85% chocolate over the same time I was feeling bloated for, and tomatoes in my salad. I have been eating chillies as well. All of those things I avoided completely at the beginning, so I shall do so again for the next week or so and see how I go.

Went to the beach this morning - no sharks! It was so breathtakingly lovely. I am going again tomorrow. Every time I forget how excellent the ocean makes me feel.

Cooked up some more pot pies... A batch with beef mince and bacon, and one with diced pOrk and bacon. Both have a couple of eggs beaten with milk to create a bit of a gravy with the fat. Cauliflower mash on top with cheese 15 mins in the oven and yum!

Did some kitchen chemistry last night to try to make magnesium oil with Epsom salts.. Mostly I just made a mess. And then is had some trouble staying asleep... Ate cauliflower and beef pie for breakfast, some hard boiled eggs for lunch and finished off the extra dark chocolate (not buying any more) ate a pork cauliflower pie for dinner. Going on the hunt for mag chloride tomorrow -the non health food ripoff version.

Thinking I should have a go at the autoimmune protocol for a while to see if that helps with weightless.. And maybe I should look up what I was eating when I first started compared to now to see what is different.

I enjoyed today's blog, and found the global coherence project information really interesting. That's going to be my next new interest..

oh, I went shopping today and bought not one but two bikinis.. (Because.. We will have a pool, and if I can't get my guts out in my own backyard, then where can I?

also, I bought some shirts and underwear all another size down... That was pretty liberating.

### kathylu — 2013-02-03T17:20:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73954>

Coconut Almond piecrust

3/4 cup sifted coconut flour
3/4 cup almond flour (or FINELY chopped almond meal)
3 eggs
1 tbsp honey (optional)
5 tbsp of melted butter or coconut oil
1/4 tsp salt

Mix well and roll out on parchment paper. The crust is fragile and the paper makes it easier to transfer the crust to a pie plate. However, if it breaks, just repair by patting with your fingers. Works well for sweet or savory fillings.

If the baking time is longer than 20 minutes, cover the edges with foil to keep them from browning too much.

### ashryn — 2013-02-04T15:43:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-74064>

NOTES ON THE CORTISOL BLOG

Cortisol is a glucocorticoid hormone that plays a role in homeostasis and stress responses.
It follows a circadian rhythm, but it is more complex than the melatonin one, and is more tied up with transition times. (Increase at transition from night to day to wake you up, decrease from day to might to prepare for rest perhaps?)

**Usually cortisol rises 30 minutes after waking**

\-It is controlled by the two pathways, hypothalamic/pituitary/adrenal, and the ANS/adrenal medulla. Sometimes the HPA stops controlling the synthesis of cortisol.
\-Light at night makes the SCN tell the ANS to increase cortisol production by the adrenals. The intensity of light is important to cortisol levels. White light increases cortisol in the morning, which can increase heart rate. Other studies found that bright light dramatically reduces cortisol in humans, but artificial light has blue/greens which wreck the melatonin/DHEA/cortisol pathway.

(So intense red lights may turn out to be useful in reducing cortisol whilst maintaining other hormone cycles.)

**If cortisol is low, it is not usually an isolated issue, so attention must be given to fixing the other hormones first.
**However this can take a long time.

Increasing sex steroid hormones can reestablish the diurnal cortisol response. This is because if those hormones are abundant, the substrate hormone pregnenolone doesn't get taken from the adrenals to be used elsewhere. This is also true of DHEA. This is called pregnenolone steal.

Thyroid hormones only weakly stimulate cortisol secretion, whilst high hgh, low TSH and high melatonin levels will keep cortisol low. Oral oestrogen also weakly inhibits production.
The pill, fludrocortisoneand high aldosterone contribute to adrenal fatigue

"When people have low cortisol production in the morning, and increasing cortisol levels at night, there are many ways to naturally boost the cortisol levels to balance the circadian cycle back to normal."

"Sunlight or bright artificial light are potent effectors of the cortisol pathways. The cortisol awakening response is highest at sunrise, due to release of cortisol from the hippocampus in the brain. It is lowest at night when light ends. "
Bright light lowers cortisol, if it is really bright, and darkness lowers it the longer you stay in dark.

Cortisol awakening response is increased in those who:

1\. waking up for a working day compared to non working weekend day.

2\. experiencing chronic mental stress and emotional worry

3\. overloaded with tasks

4\. suffering acute stress of any type.

5\. In those worn down by burnout (PTSD), some studies find an increased though other researchers find a decreased or normal response.

Artificial light at night raises cortisol because glucagon which is usually released in dark to maintain blood sugar levels whilst we sleep is confused by the light (or late night carbs)

If you eat carbs at night and it helps you sleep, your liver is doing it wrong. (Observe the light cycles and fix your liver to sort this out.)

**How to make it work for you..**

\-Increase your exposure to sunlight, especially in the morning.
\-Maximise darkness at night by sleeping with an eye mask at night and avoid artificial light after dusk.
\--Do not wear sunglasses.

Dietary saturated fat (LDL levels) is necessary for the production of cortisol as saturated fat cholesterol is the first building block for cortisol synthesis. Avoid most alcohol, vinegars/acids, caffeinated beverages, sugars and sweets, most soft drinks with fructose, most baked goods like bread, pastas and cereal grains.
Avoid all cereal fiber and all milk products, especially those with casein proteins to modulate cortisol levels.

caffeine (coffee, tea, cola) and alcohol should be avoided before bedtime as caffeine can increase cortisol, and considerably reduce night-time secretion of melatonin.

Excessive and prolonged stress of any kind, exhausts the adrenal glands. Evening or night-time stress is a very strong stimulator of cortisol secretion, and quickly depletes the adrenal supply of cortisol. This results in decreased cortisol levels for the next morning when it should be high.

Low cortisol can be supplemented bio identicals are always best. The higher the initial dose the faster the effect, but it doesn't lat long. Sower and lower works better. If long term supplementation is necessary, it's important to take into account the effects on the pituitary of long term suppression of the HPA pathway.

**Try light and sleep and food methods first.**

Otimal dose may vary following changes in the need for cortisol.
The minimal efficient dose in most women is usually 15 – 20 mg per day. In men, it is greater, about 30 mg per day, usually divided in at least two separate doses. One can be given in the morning and one at noon to follow the normal circadian rhythm of cortisol.

**contraindications:**

1\. If you do not need it, you should never take it.
2\. Cortisol supplementation should not be a first line treatment option if other hormone deficiencies exist. It rarely occurs in isolation, so fix the other hormones first.

Catabolic effects of cortisol include loss of muscle, bone, and cartilage, osteoporosis, skin atrophy, ecchymosis, petechia (spider veins), and immunosuppression.

SIGNS OF CORTISOL EXCESS

\-The most common finding is obesity (hypercortisolism) -truncal obesity.
\-mental and emotional signs include being overly emotional, excessive agitation, euphoric, insensitive to human suffering, a craving of stress and often creates their own stress to satisfy their physiologic needs, stressing others out to create an environment of stress but not stressing themselves out.
\-After several hours of elevated levels, we see significant reduction of thyroid and insulin levels in the adrenal gland.
\-Insomnia is commonly tied to very low DHEA levels and low melatonin levels. Women show pregnenolone and progesterone defects.

After several days of excess: pounding heart and or anxiety. This leads to swollen hands and feet, swollen face especially around the eyelids snd elevated basal blood pressure.
After several weeks: excessive weight gain, easy bruising of the skin and tiny skin hemorrhages.
After six months: atrophic skin, altered immunity, and severe osteoporosis develop.

"When someone is stressed and has an altered cortisol level, one should consider adding B6, Mg, and CoEnZ Q10, L-carnitine, and D-Ribose as adjuvants to therapy. I also like to see B12 levels that are high and hovering above 1000 pg/mL with a low homocysteine level. I also look for greying of the hair and increased wrinkling of the skin to see how a cortisol level maybe affecting other hormones in the cascade. Chronic fatigue is another symptom to pay attention to as well for hormone cascade abnormalities."

actually I am saying bloated, but I'm not sure if it really is bloating, it doesn't feel like when I have drunk too much water with food, my belly is just a bit rounder than it was. It is only uncomfortable in the sense that I don't like the way it looks.
I did try pickles but the ones I found were sweet and so I don't like them, and I believe kombucha is sweet too? Sauerkraut I could do, if I could find any, I've looked a few places, including those fruit and veg markets that have odd veg and products that cater to non-sad diets, but haven't seen any yet. (Aussie nana, are you reading this? Have you found sauerkraut anywhere?)

re liquid vitamin D... What does that taste like? Mine is in a clear capsule (DD calls them little balls of sunshine -she's so cute sometimes)

cooked something kinda cool that will work for BAB -meat and nut muffins. They're pretty good. I was avoiding nuts but I remembered that I made something like this back at the beginning when everything was going smoothly, so.. Why not. I stirred up a mixture of beef mince, and unsalted mixed nuts (no peanuts and Brazil's removed for selenium supplementation) some mustard, turmeric, a mixture of cheddar and mozzarella (because cheddar can be a bit strong at breakfast)a couple of home grown spring onions and 6 eggs. Chucked into giant muffin tins Cooked for a long time at low ish temp, and done. I just had one cold bab and it was good.. Easy

Ate tuna for BAB yesterday, and whiting fillets for dinner (ugh.. Can you get them without the skin on?)
drank red wine and umm oxytocin under the stars last night. Heh. Seems I say something out loud and prove myself a liar.

Had a good talk tp DH about circadian and cold.. And all the different things I am trying to align.. The biochemical leptin pathway to retrain myself to use fat not store it, the aincent pathway of cold to slow the metabolism and make me need less food, and the physical cold to make my extra fat cells suicide instead of just shrink and lay in wait for the next carbs, plus conversion of brown to white fat. It's the first time he has been interested enough to find out all about it. Then we talked some about body comp and happiness and what a difference happiness makes to how a person feels and looks. We made some guesses about why men are more likely to be resilient in the face of disconnection from the earth and bombardment with technological light at night, and also why they lose weight faster.. I think the designated abbreviation is SO... I am aware of how sexist and 1950s housewife that particular gross generalisation is, but based on other threads it might be true anyway.. Women seem to need to _learn and practice_ SO, men just do it whenever the opportunity arises, I can't imagine any forum threads popping up supporting men trying to get more oxytocin.. But perhaps I am wrong.

Hooeee. This weekend I must write a job application, help DH waterproof the wall outside so we can raise the ground level in preparation for the pool, and I am going to get to the beach **twice**. Whilst on my laptop, I shall purchase some d-ribose despite knowing nothing much about it, other than that lots of people find themselves with lotsa energy after taking it.. And I could do with that.

I am going to stop all supplements except DHEA, mg and D for a week to see what happens. I have blood tests on Monday morning. Pool gets installed on Tuesday, not sure how long it will be till I can swim in it, but we're getting close

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-10T07:13:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-75183>

> [ashryn said:](/goto/post?id=74955)
>
> Darleen, fermented grapes doesn't count, does it?
> Sauerkraut I could do, if I could find any, I've looked a few places, including those fruit and veg markets that have odd veg and products that cater to non-sad diets, but haven't seen any yet. (Aussie nana, are you reading this? Have you found sauerkraut anywhere?)

You can buy kim chee at Farmer Jacks in Girrawheen, which is similar, but the Korean (?) version, I think.

I make my own sauerkraut. I thinly slice red cabbage, as for coleslaw, stuff it in a jar, pounding it down with a wooden spoon. Sprinkle it with celtic sea salt, more pounding, add more cabbage, more salt, more pounding. Then I add a little filtered water so the cabbage is almost covered. Then I partially fill a small zip lock plastic bag and put it on the cabbage to keep out the air. Leave it on the bench for 24 hours, then into the frig for a week. Seems to work. And interestingly after 3 months in the frig its still crispy, though I ought to actually eat it, not just leave it there.

thinking my wbc count is up because I got the cold everyone around me has had, but CT prevented me from noticing or getting any symptoms ( now that I think about it, I have been a little more snotty than usual the last week.. But no feeling sick)

spent a couple of hours soaking and reading a book after work yesterday, and then again before bed, but not today because the pump isn't connected yet, and it is quite dirty with sand and grass.

Shops: Ordered some d-ribose and am expecting it to show up tomorrow.

Foods: been eating fish and tuna for one meal per day mostly, and ate seaweed too.

Hormones: seem to still be on the mend, though time is flying this year, I can't believe its totm again. No PMS, and very little pain so far, just a couple of cramps for an hour here or there.. Just when I think I can't stand it any more, the pain stops, and I forget about it. Soaking in the pool seems to help too.

More foods: I must make more of the nut and beef muffins... I swear I lost belly when I was eating them for BAB. will do the test next week and see. I think perhaps i am eating too much protein and need to swap some of the night portion for fat instead. I have pretty much been eating the same thing for dinner as for BAB, not realising about the insulin spike thing.

Sleep: slept badly last night.. Too much light at mum and dads, and ate dinner too late (but it was fish) the grocery delivery people woke me up at 2am and I was crampy and had to get up and stalk the house like a ghost for a while, which reminds me.. Going to bed early tonight.
Goodnight!

### ashryn — 2013-02-16T11:07:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-76093>

Just ate barramundi and garlic prawns.. Cooked from fresh by moi! They were quite yum.. Though I cooked too much and couldn't finish all the prawns. Thats two sea meals i have had today, and lets see... I can now happily eat barramundi, prawns and tuna, salmon if its fresh and someone else cooks it for me, whiting with a bit of fortitude, and snapper grilled in the sandwich press by the fish n chip shop. That's not too bad for a lifelong fish hater.

Started eating d ribose yesterday when it arrived.. I like it! Though, I have to find something else to put it in since I like my coffee unsweetened. Must get around to making that chocolate..

Been swimming lotsa, and did a couple of hours this morning pushing sand around the outside of the pool trying to make it slightly less deserty. All in bare feet with the hose to help sculpt the sand so I got lots of grounding today.

No more cramps or any kind of symptoms from Aunt Flo, which I'm happy about. And I am going to have to give some of the credit back to the no grains diet, since I haven't been belly icing for a few weeks, due to the bag I was using breaking and not finding another one that is suitable. (Yah, excuses excuses) these just so many things I should be doing for my health, I don't really have time for them all. S I have prioritised them

1\. Mind the light
2\. Eat BAB preferably fish
3\. Eat paleo
4\. Get my feet on the ground or use the TENS as much as possible.
5\. Get in the pool and try to get cold.

Current supplements: d ribose, magnesium, vitamin d, coQ10, milk thistle, multivitamin.
starting krill oil again on monday

### ashryn — 2013-02-17T12:36:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-76192>

Krista, I was doing that too, but I think if I could use it in place of sugar in something, I might be able to have a sweet thing sometimes without breaking things.. I have just made some chocolate.. Raw cacao powder, raw cacao butter, coconut oil, coconut flakes, cashews, maca powder (because it doesn't dissolve in coffee, and it smells like honeycomb) and ribose.. Tasted ok when melty, but it is too late for me to eat anything more today, so I'll have to report back on the taste tomorrow.

I spent hours today in the pool reading a book, and got sunburnt. \*sigh\* when will I learn? I have been to the beach for hours at a time recently with no sunscreen and haven't been burnt, so I thought all the vitamin d was doing its job. Apparently not on the parts of me that have only seen the sun once or twice in my life. Went and had a cold bath with 2kg epsom salts afterward hoping that my skin would suck it all up and it will speed the healing. Covered my skin in coconut oil and cocoa butter and Also ate some krill oil and coQ10 and ribose hoping to reduce inflammation... No idea if its a good idea or not, but I doubt it will hurt.

Early to bed tonight, and make certain I don't see any lights to make sure I get the maximum healing power out of my sleep.

Knowing better doesn't necessarily make me do better it seems. I wonder how much of a setback sunburn is.

Clearly I am a wuss. And the last two days have been HOT!

also eating less protein at dinner is going to take a bit of getting used to. I cooked up prawns for dinner tonight on a bed of cos lettuce with aioli and bacon.. Couldn't eat them all, but instead of forcing them in like I would if it was BAB, I left them. Now to remember that when I'm preparing the dinner so I don't waste it.

Thats two sea meals today! Woot!

I had a bit of an EMF spell Tuesday... I had a training course in a computer lab and I stupidly stuck myself in the back corner of the room. By 10.30 I was feeling SICK I nwent outside a couple of times just to discharge, and felt much better. I guess I have found my emf limit.. N more computer labs for me if I can avoid it. It seems plausible to me that there could be more to it than brain fag.

### ashryn — 2013-02-23T03:38:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-77205>

Thanks heidihen, I have been having coconut oil in coffee at dinner, (coffee doesn't keep me awake, and since I have borderline haemochromotosis, it stops me absorbing too much iron from my food - at least that's the way I see it)
perhaps I can just eat less protein at dinner and not repla e it with anything. I shall try that and see how I go.

I am feeling a bit thoughtful today.. Here's some of them..

The forums have all been a bit combative of late, and a lot of people seem to be doing the same addiction response that we noted in people who are told carbs are bad for them.. I think it is interesting that we get so defensive when challenged, particularly those of us who feel we are already doing everything perfectly _especially compared to those SADs_
probably the comparison is making things harder for us.. Probably everyone is doing as perfectly as they can right now, and needs to make peace with the fact that we will never do everything perfectly, but we can keep that as the goal and know every step we take in the right direction is worthwhile.

If I understand correctly, the EMF thing is such an issue for everyone now because you can't escape it. It bounces around in the ionosphere and hits even remote lovely schumann areas... But even that must be a matter of degree. There must be a way to do some studies to convince people that something is fiddling with our ability to find balance.

I saw a NASA article yesterday that said they had detected schumann resonance from space.. So perhaps nature has worked out a way to let some of the other Emf out too? [http://www.nasa.gov/mission\_pages/sunearth/news/lightning-waves.html](http://www.nasa.gov/mission_pages/sunearth/news/lightning-waves.html)

why can't we just put schumann resonance generators in our homes and pockets?

if epi genetics is speeding evolution, is it possible that we will mutate a way to thrive with frequencies above 60hz? Could quantum science help us speed that up?

can we last 10 more years until the singularity? When a computer is invented that will design its successor, all bets are off anyway. No one can predict the future of the species once we have synthetic intelligence, perhaps computer much smarter than we can hope to be will sort it out.. Or perhaps they will think that natural selection is the answer to our parasitic species.

Or there's the work of Randall koene at Boston university on substrate independent minds [http://www.carboncopies.org/substrate-independent-minds](http://www.carboncopies.org/substrate-independent-minds) ... Perhaps he will come up with a way we can upload ourselves and no longer be reliant on these weak and fragile bodies we have.

Perhaps we can combine our n=1s on a site like patientslikeme [http://www.patientslikeme.com/](http://www.patientslikeme.com/) to assist in data collection regarding all the kruze protocols. If everyone here added their data, and Dr K asked his patients to add their data, we would be able to crowd source the scientific backing of his claims..

### ashryn — 2013-02-24T04:34:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-77368>

Caroline, I suspect it is the addict response that we see in people when we tell them we don't eat carbs.. The nice thing about this community is that it is full of clever self-evaluators who don't just coast along but actively look at their thoughts and feelings to decide what their truth will be. Even if we snap under the strain of changing our assumptions, we knit ourselves back together pretty fast.. And I think most people are generous enough to overlook instances of bad manners knowing everyone has them.

So... Back to the more traditional journal entry today, because there is a bit of strangeness going on.

the last two nights I have woken mid-sleep with gastrointestinal disturbances. Diarrhoea both times and last night vomit as well, despite only having water and half a glass of red wine 6 hours earlier. Last night I also had uncontrollable shaking and a racing heart. It was all over within 10minutes and I was back to sleep for a few more hours, and feel perfectly fine today. What the hell?

I think I have been in ketosis for 3 days (can only tell by strong smelling urine, and knowing that I have had no carbs)
i am having hardwood flooring put into my library and there is a strong smell from the glue the tradie is using to direct stick them (could it be poisoning?)
Both nights I saw more artificial light than I usually do, but still through my glasses.
both episodes happened after 6 hours of sleep.
Friday I only ate fish, Saturday I ate steak. Friday I took extra coQ10 Saturday I didn't, plus took no mag on sat.
Friday no swimming, Saturday a couple of hours.
Thursday and Friday I did spot icing for the first time in a week, and Saturday my cold came from the pool.

Today the scales actually showed me 2kg down compared with Friday morning... Is this because of the evacuation events, or perhaps something gut wise has let go and this is another detox phase?

This morning I made a bacon and egg omelette for BAB, but could only eat half of it. So roughly 25g protein and whatever fat comes from 3 strips o bacon, and 100g ish cheese and 3 Brazil nuts. No coconut oil in my coffee this morning because didnt feel like it. I haven't eaten eggs for a week. I have been taking krill oil again this week, and have added d ribose and removed the glucosamine/chondroitin/msm I had been taking for joint and back pain. (Last time I stopped that, the pain came back, this time, not at all-yay!)

At the baby shower I was amazed that every piece of food on the table was carbs. There was not a single thing I could eat. It was ok though, the only thing that would have tempted me was the red velvet cake, and only because I have never tried it, but they didn't come handing it around, so I remained pure heheh. Came home and cooked a piece of steak for DD and I then went to the vintage markets...

Found an awesome pair of black and silver corduroy flares with a lace-up fly for $8 and a super funky 70s style shirt that **will fit me** some day. Also bought a cute 60s denim skirt that, on reflection, will NEVER fit me. Even if you took all of the flesh off and just left me with bones the skirt wouldn't fit. (My sister did point out before I bought it that it was a perfect tool to use to destroy my self-esteem..) But in the dark I had no clue about just how much too small it is for me.. BUT IT'S SO CUTE! It occurs to me that I could take it to a tailor and ask them to make one in my size.. Then give this one to my 8year old niece.. Because I think it's a child's skirt  also found a hippie wrap skirt that I will wear to the camping music festival next weekend, and a funky steam punk pocket watch for DH.

oh yeah, DH's band is playing at the nannup music festival next weekend.. It's a hippie camping thang in the forest 3 hours drive down south ...looking forward to getting my nature on!

people kept taking photos of me at the baby shower which I hate.. But every time I complained they showed me the photo and there was me not looking like me, but like some other happy chick with my hairstyle and clothes and a dimpled grin.. I say again.. What the hell? I always look terrible in photos. However.. Pink hair doesn't really go with a red t-shirt. Should probably remember that :/

Speaking of happy, I hadn't noticed it, but DH has commented a few times in the last couple of weeks that I seem much happier and positive in general, and thinking about it, I haven't had one of those real bad mood days for a while, or one of those pissed off times where I radiate anger but don't speak about it.. This is a nice thing. Funny how you don't notice being happy.

### ashryn — 2013-02-25T11:44:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-77540>

Caroline I have a denim skirt that I bought years ago for a couple of dollars, and I love it.. Just the right length, colour and shape.. But it's getting old, and I have been trying to replace it but don't like any of the new ones that you can buy at the moment. The vintage one i got is probably a mini, but it has a nice circular cut (?) if it sat on my hips instead of my waist it would be perfect.. Oh, and if it fit!

Last night I was sitting on the grass reading jacks book and a kitten decided to come and walk all over me.. SO CUTE! I am not a cat person, but cats are always ashryn people. It seems like it has been dumped and it is about the right age/ amount of time since Xmas for a child to have gotten bored of it. Stupid people. I don't want to have an animal to care for and get attached to then grieve over when it dies. Grr. What's the rules for feeding cats anyway? Raw meat only?

We have a bunch of workmen doing stuff at our house at the moment and its making me in a really bad mood. I can't swim in my pool, my backyard and front yard have been completely trashed, the is no place tompark my car because they have left all their shit over the front yard, there is poisonous fumes upstairs, the floor guy had the aircon down at 16 degrees with the windows open, the door to the room with the vent in it shut, and the front door wide open all day letting in flies and chewing through my electricity. When I tried to tell him how to use it he scoffed at me telling him that on a 36 degree day there's no point putting it down as low as 16 because it just wastes power, there is no way it will ever get the inside temp 20degrees lower than the outside temp.. ESPECIALLY IF YOU LEAVE THE DOORS AND WINDOWS OPEN YOU MORON! I wish I had said it in exactly those words now.

There isn't even a butt-sized patch of grass for me to sit on outside, and I don't want to sit in the hot dirty sand.

For breakfast I ate meat muffins with cheese and wakeme seaweed and mixed raw nuts, but clearly not enough fat because I was quite hungry by 3pm. Made it home and had dinner at 4.30 though, so its all good. I could probably make those muffin things with tuna and cheese and they'd be pretty good.

Bah. Fts. I'm going to have a cold bath with magnesium and salt and pretend I'm at the beach.
So,much for not having a bad mood in a while huh?

### ashryn — 2013-02-26T14:02:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-77746>

Just spent a couple of hours in the pool (which is half full of sand because of the landscapers) having a big long chat to DD. it's interesting that the older she gets the better we get along. Guess I'm not the nurturing type. She is learning to speak Spanish, and she was telling me she really enjoys learning -THANK SCIENCE! She said it was nice to be using her brain for something and that she feels like she's just been watching TV and thinking about TV for such a long time... I am so relieved. Since I have been studying something for the entire duration of her life, and I have such a passion for knowing thing and understanding things, I was devastated when she deferred her psych degree and then told me she wants to drop out. (I know she can go back. And there is plenty of time and I didn't want to pressure her.) but I was terrified she would go through life never getting an in depth and comprehensive understanding of something. It seems like a crime to me, when education is available and there and subsidised by the government not to get the best education you can. Perhaps her new diet is letting her brain cells wake up and function more efficiently so learning isnt such a struggle. Perhaps it's about her making the choice for herself to go back to study. Who knows? I'm just happy she is extending her brain.

### ashryn — 2013-02-28T15:47:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-78298>

So... Last day of summer... Roll on winter!
i am only half way through the latest blog, but I did spend part of my work day listening to last nights QA.. Thanks so much for recording it! It was very interesting.

it seems the forum trolls have returned, full of helpful advice about low fat diets and exercise...

I am REALLY looking forward to this weekend's camping trip. It has been such a long time since I have been camping. And the place were going is awesome forestryness in the heart of hippie country.. Yes that still exists here!

Spent lots of time in the sun today getting the photoelectric effect on my skin (as much as was exposed anyway) and then in the pool as the sun set.. I am interested in what JK was saying about there being an optimum time to be in the sun.. How would I work out what this is for me? Is it to do with where you were born? I am 3 generations western Australian, prior to that we were all over Europe.. Germany, Ireland, Wales...
Died my hair fire truck red this afternoon.. Who knew that red over hot pink makes the pink even more fluouro? Be interesting to see what it looks like when I wash it out

Today at work I just couldn't be arsed doing anything productive, so I used up some of the time they owe me and wandered off down the street.. Found a hairdresser and had a haircut.. Came back to work feeling much happier.

I have a new plant on my desk to catch the EMF from my screens. It sits between me and the CPU, so hopefully its doing some good

i've been thinking about stuff lately.. (What's new, right?) mostly I have been thinking about my unthinkning connectedness to the earth.. I am barefoot whenever I can be, always at home, often at friends homes too. I go outside often, feel happiest at the beach, or on camping holidays, (provided we're not holidaying with electron vampires who suck the life energy out of me) When I had a second job editing a music website, that needed me to be online a lot and use my phone a lot, I was less happy.. I liked the job, for the live music, nd for the good i was doing for the local scene, but I had a phone that was so unreliable most people didnt bother calling me.. And I liked it that way. Now I have a reliable phone, but I have trained the people so they don't call anyway!

I think I am epi genetically lucky in that my mum and grandmothers were reasonably healthy.. Ok, grandmothers both had cancer, breast and bowel and pancreatic.. One died of pancreatic, the other of a stroke.. But apart from the end, they were very robust. My mum also has had breast cancer (despite breast feeding 4 of us) but seems to be doing ok ish now, 5 years on. Though she is looking like a candidate for diabetes these days. She's never been sick before that, she always has her hands in the earth gardening and whatnot. Mums sister also had breast cancer 20years ago.. It isn't looking too good for my little sister and I, is it? But it kind of explains the stupid hormonal crap I have always had, and the PCOS... I am hoping the improvements I have seen in that area might head off the inevitable. (Which reminds me, I have an overshare for the oxytocin thread, if I remember) I can kind of see a pattern in the women in my family, like the disconnect took its toll on the hormonal parts, but because we lived here.. With ocean close by, and swimming daily (mum swam in the ocean with her dad daily till she got married, dad swam daily till he got married too, then he and I used to swim daily (yes even in winter) for a couple of years.. Then I did it on my own for a couple of years too. (Incidentally, Caroline, swimming at 5.30am is a whole new experience.. And the kinds of people who love the ocean so much they drag themselves out there every day year round are OUR kind of people.. Seriously, go get to know them!) perhaps it took longer for the damage to show up..
mums mum's decline began when she got a microwave, and then after the health scare of breast cancer a couple of years after that she started following the CW eating plans and exercise and using sunscreen.. And inevitable decline.. Same with my dad's mum.. Got bowel cancer, had it removed, stopped eating so much fat, (she used to cook with dripping, and lard all the time.. Always had real butter) and started following the CW diets recommended to her... Died of a stroke about 10 years after that. (Interestingly, my brother had a resection due to colitis ...to avoid it developing into cancer....at the age of 30... Time speeding up) his 8 year old daughter has exactly the same iridology signs of systemic inflammation that I saw in his eyes years before his colitis became a problem.

I started off saying I think I am epi genetically lucky... So what is my point here? I think these illnesses my family has/had, are like first stage illnesses. They look like they can be fixed with not too much effort.. I think that the nature based lifestyle choices (situations actually, since they probably wouldn't have chosen them excepts that's just how life was then) have slowed down some of the negative effects I might have had to live through myself otherwise.

Thank science I found JK when I did!

Oh.. The journal update... Same as usual, nothing new to report..

### ashryn — 2013-03-05T12:58:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79052>

Oh... What am I doing wrong?

Just spent 3 days in the nature, barefoot and surrounded by hippies.. Surely I would have done some electron replenishing and lost some belly.. Nope.

Actually, I did an experiment on the way home (a dangerous one as it turns out) I ate a pie from a bakery.. First grains in several months, within 2 minutes of beginning to eat it, my stomach started making crazy growly noises (no idea what that was about) and it took all of about 30 minutes to KILL my energy completely. I had another 2 hour drive home and I was worried I would fall asleep at the wheel. Damn. A little bit of me was thinking that since emf matters so hard, and I had been out in the nature without it for 3days, I could prolly get away with it. Not so much. And it wasnt even delicious.

It was tricky to find things I could eat at the festival, but I took tins of tuna, bacon, eggs and prawns and other meat which I BBQed, so apart from the idiot decision on the way home, the weekend should have done me some noticeable good.. I wish.

Wke up with a headache this morning and felt really flat at work today, but I'm not sure if it was the fumes from the new floor, or a carb hangover (do they exist?)

apart from sitting in the pool which is cold enough to give me goosebumps, but probably not that cold, I've been slack with the CT lately. I don't know what that's about either. So I bit the bullet and strapped ice on to do this update. Once I am done i will finish off the latest blog and then go back and read all the CT blogs again and see if I can get my cold mojo back on.

Breakfast I ate 290g (~60g protein) tuna and bulletproof coffee, lunch I ate salad, oil and meat because I was hungry, dinner I ate beef and mushrooms with a couple of eggs to soak up the fat. And coffee with 1/4cup coconut oil and 10 g d ribose. Took a pile of mag and CoQ10 to see if I can clear some of the bloating away.. Can you take too much of CoQ10?

took lots of photos of the band, but processing them means sitting at the computer and I seem to have developed a phobia of it lately.. As much as I will myself to go in there and do some work, I just can't seem to make myself do it. It's the wifi room. I need shielding first. When the pool landscaping is all finished and I can finally clean my house, I will be able to drag the laptop outside and put my feet on the ground whilst I work in the sun.

Bought some funky hippy pants, all cottony patchwork stripes.. These are the kind of pants I have always loved but never found any in a size that fit. I also found some more flares, so my collection is getting vast... I was worried I would be forced to wear skinny jeans if I couldn't find any others.. Society has been saved from such a frightening spectacle however.

\*sigh\* I don't know what to do next.. Keep on truckin is the option, but I must be missing some vital part of some rx that's keeping me inflamed and not losing.

Apparently australia has just had the longest hottest summer on record... I wonder if we had more diagnoses of diabetes here this year as well.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-05T18:02:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79113>

You ate a PIE?! Are you nuts? (said with a great deal of humor, laughter and concern) Have you NOT read Wheat Belly??? Do I have to come down there and have a word with you?! (still giggling)

I don't care how long you sit in the sun or on the ground, wheat will kill ya. Rat poison would be faster but just as unpleasant. Don't eat that, either.

Fun in the sun sounds wonderful. But let's have a chat about that laptop, shall we?

And I'ma gonna tell you again. This template is not a "lose 10 pounds overnight" kind of thing. Did you get Jack's book yet? Have you started reading it? HIs blog, the book, everything he's done is a giant treasure hunt. He gives you the questions to ask, you have to go find the treasure (your health). Trust me when I tell you that for most of us it will NOT be an overnight sensation.

now. Here's a {{{hug}}}. Hope you get past that carb hangover (yes, there is such a thing) soon.

### ashryn — 2013-03-06T11:09:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79274>

Krista! Wine makes your belly flatter? I'M ON IT! \*grin\* I don't miss pie.. And it wasnt a craving, it was an experiment I tells ya! No, really.. Dunno why I did it, but I am kinda glad I did. It was interesting to see the immediate effect it had on me. What I miss most of all is milk chocolate.. With Turkish delight or that Black Forest one.. \*wistful sigh\*

Darleen, I am not expecting overnight! I just figure I must be doing something wrong because it has been months.. Yes, I know how long you have been patient, but clearly you are a saint in your patience  and I am a demon.. Because I want it now already. (Or, you know, something to keep me motivated) I even posted my labs in the hopes that someone will look at them and spot something I could do to fix me.. Let me whine.. It makes people remind me to shut up and eat my fish.. Or get in an ice bucket...

... ironically I started reading wheat belly on the weekend away.. Gotta say I was a trifle bored (like, outta my head) because... Preaching to the choir. I haven't eaten bread nor sugar for close to a year, and wheat products and any other grains for 4 months.. Lesson learned i suppose.. Does wheat belly get more interesting? Like, does it tell me something I don't know already?

Also, yes I have jack's book and I got about 1/2way through it before I had to stop and read the new instalments of the blogs.. Nicely written, but so far nothing not in the blogs and some things kinda contradictory to what he has been saying lately. Guess it depends on the target audience.

Hey! Whats your issue with the laptop? It won't have wifi, and I'll be grounded using it... How the hell else am I supposed to post photos of DH's band? (What if I just think really hard about posting them, and they will quantu ly appear?) \*smirk\*

If it will make you happy, I'll make a faraday coat to wear.. It will make very unusual tan lines though

carb hangover is all gone now too.. I did wake up last night but that was because I had slept 7 hours and my brain forgot I had gone to sleep real early last night so it thought it was time for breakfast.. Went back to sleep once I realised what time it was. (Had to get up, find clothes, go downstairs to know what time it was though, I have to get up when it's dark these days.

Ayhoo.. Going to soak in the pool before wandering over to my parents house for dinner.

### ashryn — 2013-03-07T13:21:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79445>

Hi hope, funny you should quote me after I have been sulking about not losing any weight lately hehe thanks for reminding me I did good at the beginning.

I just made my home made chocolate with equal parts cacao dust and cacao butter, and about 1/3 of that total in coconut oil.. Add some crushed nuts (cashews or almonds I like best because they are a bit sweet) and a stack of coconut flakes (also for the sweetness) and the tiniest bit of sea salt. if its too bitter you can chuck in a bit of stevia, but I generally don't because I think even fake sugar makes me spike insulin. Mix and mix and mix to make it smooth.. The pros who are less lazy than me pour some mixture on the bench top, fold and fold and fold with a spatula till it is almost set, then add some more warm chocolate to soften it and repeat till all mixed. This makes it smooth and creamy.. I am too impatient for that stuff so mine isnt as nice as it could be. Press it into a tin, score it with a knife to make it easy to cut when it is set and keep in the fridge.
I have heard that vanilla bean helps the smooth and creamy taste too.. Bt I haven't tried it because I don't know how to use vanilla bean!

I assume it is low carb because no sugar... I just realised I don't think I have checked to see what carb levels cacao powder is.

I worked out the protein fat thing using a website called paleo track ...because I needed to learn how much meat would give me 50g of protein for the BAB. As part of it I was entering all my foods, and that's what I came out as. I wasn't counting calories, just making sure I got the BAB right, and eating till I was sated other meals. I also wanted to make sure I was getting enough vitamins and minerals in my food to avoid supplements..

These days I just guess, based on knowing how much I need for 50 grams, I do suspect I might be eating too much protein now though, so I may need to go back to tracking for a bit till I learn what an appropriate amount is. (For keto, you need 1g protein per kg body weight, so it reduces as you do, the idea is to add fat if you're still hungry... As far as I understand it.)

### ashryn — 2013-03-07T14:49:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79459>

Caroline, you'll be happy to know I am all wrapped up in ice as I write this, with my glasses on, the ipad colours inverted, and the tens machine sending a Schumann frequency into my calves. (Well soles of my feet is just a pain in the arse, and my calves were unusually tight today)

I got reflexology done today, and all I could think of the whole time was that I was sucking up the electrons of the poor masseuse. Imagine doing that every day?

Based on some ideas in my hack my labs thread, it seems I too have the thyroid monster. At least I have an answer to the stubborn weight now.

I am probably in denial about it, but I plan to stay here for at least 3 months whilst I do all the good things I have been doing plus some extra things for thyroid care..
Iodine... I haven't been able to find any here except betadine... Will that do? Not sure if I can ingest it, but someone was talking about putting it on the skin.. Anyone know?

Vitamin b12 think this is probably ok with all the meat, but I shall start eating mushrooms more again too just in case. It's also in my multivitamin.

Selenium.. I have been eating more brazil nits for the selenium the last week, (and have a bunch of pimples on my face because of it! Calling this detox for now, and I'll give it another week.)

Milk thistle.. I doubled the dose today, but must check to make sure it is made from the seed and not the whole plant to avoid the oestrogen effect it can have.

Iron... Mines high anyway, so I shall leave it that way and hopefully it will sort out the F3/rt3 ratio.

CT... I'm back in the ice.. As I said. Actually, the ice is back on me, since there's no chance of an ice bath here, the pool and ice packs will have to do.

All these things are in addition to the earthing, and the reset, and increasing my fish and minding the light.. Etc.

I have only drunk spring water from a local spring for the last 5 days.. But it occurs to me that leaving the water on my desk next to my monitors might decohere it, then I thought about all the stuff the water is exposed to during shipping and storage.. And I'm thinking I'd like something I can treat the water with to re-cohere it.. Is there such a beast? Failing that I might rig up some kind of faraday cage with crystals and wire to use as a water jug cover.. I could make it pretty and protective all at once..

And lastly, some goodish news, (or hope springs eternal, or wishful thinking whatever you want to call it) my colleague commented today that "those pants are "baggy" on you now...all your clothes are baggy on you now" interesting.. Those pants are meant to be tight and flared.. And whilst they haven't been tight for a long time, I wouldn't have called them baggy.. They are still close fitting, the fabric just doesn't have to stretch at all now. Come to think of it, I do spend a fair bit of time holding them up.. Heh. Scales still say no change, but if women at work are noticing, yay.
And also.. Damn. I have to go do dreaded work clothes shopping. Why can I rarely find anything businesslike that is ashrynlike? Perhaps I'll go for an all black wardrobe this winter

Man.. I've written another kruze length post..

### Kiera2 — 2013-03-08T04:13:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79591>

Ashryn, you can buy it here, from your chemist. They may have to order it in; ask for David Craig Galenicals Aqueous Iodine Solution APF. contains Iodine 5% potassium iodide 10%. I pay $20 for 100ml. You can also buy Lugol's from a number of online alternative medicine sites; there's one in Sydney and one in Byron Bay, but there must be more. But they charge a ridiculous amount for a much smaller bottle.

Oh, and it comes in a bottle marked 'poison' so I don't let on that I put it it my water!

### ashryn — 2013-03-08T10:59:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79619>

Thanks coriander, I'll go have a chat to my chemist.. D you know if you can just use betadine?

### Kiera2 — 2013-03-08T11:44:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79624>

NO!!! Don't use betadine for anything but topical application, it has other crap in it. Lugol's and it's equivalent (Aqueous Iodine Solution) are the only ones you can ingest, and then only as a few drops in a glass of water.

### caroline — 2013-03-10T02:25:39+0200

### Kiera2 — 2013-03-10T03:30:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79896>

Yes, you can get a landline, and tell Telstra you want internet wired in, not wifi. Then you should only use your computer at home. But you don't have to use Telstra. Maybe talk to iinet first? Just be sure to tell them you don't want wifi.

### ashryn — 2013-03-10T14:27:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79931>

Yeah, talk to iinet.. They will sort you out with a landline rental and Internet access that's not as evil as Telstra. Just give them a call, you shouldn't have to go in to see them.
Yes, using your phone's Internet on your laptop is called a hotspot, but so is any place you can pick up free wifi. It is the same, no worse than wifi, but I guess it depends on what bit of you the signal is hitting too.
I have a "bob" from iinet, and it is wired in directly. Just tell them you want to use a cable and not wifi. You won't find anyone advertising it, but plenty of people don't use wifi, it is still very common. My computers at work are all hard wired in... It gives a more stable connection since plenty of other things interfere with the wifi signal. It also improves the speed to be plugged straight in.

I don't use a grounding mat at work, taking off my shoes isn't really practical since I am up and down all day long. I have a plant.. And I make sure I go stand in the sun every couple of hours for 10 minutes (with my hands on a fire hydrant to amplify the effect- no idea if that helps or if I am being superstitious  )

### ashryn — 2013-03-10T14:45:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79935>

Today's update...
i think I may have overdone the seaweed and have given myself some iodine detox.. Can't think what else it could be anyway.. Yesterday I drank a couple of glasses of the seaweed water and was feeling quite unwell in the afternoon.. Thought I was going to be sick, slept for about 90 mins and struggled to eat any dinner

I also painted iodine on me both days.

When I woke up this morning I felt fine, and drank one glass of seaweed water with the supplements after breakfast. About 2hours later my hands were shaking, my heart was beating too hard and I felt weak. Drank some cold spring water and felt ok after a while. Then about 5 hours from drinking the seaweed water I felt nauseous again and my heart started pounding again (not beating faster, just harder if that makes sense) I had to go lie down again. Felt really hot and couldn't cool down. Had a cool bath with sea salt and Epsom salts, still couldn't cool down. Lay under the aircon which was fine till I stood up again. Couldn't eat at all tonight except Brazil nuts.

Read the iodine thread again.. Tried the pink salt water trick and ate a handful of Brazil nuts and felt better very quickly...
I am assuming I have plenty of fluoride and bromides in me to get rid of, but I didn't think seaweed water would give me too much.. Perhaps coupled with 300g tuna for breakfast it is too much?

So the question is, do I power on with one glass per day, or dilute it by half and see how I go with that?

Its also day 1 of TotM... Again! 5 days early by my count. Lets hope it is a short and painless one. So far no painkillers needed.

I have not drunk any fluoridated water for 4 days now, but I can't tell if I feel better for it.. Cold spring water is definitely more delicious to drink!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-10T16:12:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79938>

Ashryn, I'd back off on the seaweed water. I tried it months ago when Inger first described it, and I got detox reactions (tight throat, headache) just like when taking too much (for me) iodine.

Now that I have slowly built up tolerance, I'm going to try the seaweed water again. I meant to try it again already, but it slipped my mind.

Perhaps half a glass per day for you would be ok? I think the seaweed water has a lot of iodine in it.

### Inger — 2013-03-10T19:44:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79969>

Yes.. slowly is good. I would not paint with Iodine at all if using seaweed water and I would always choose natural Iodine from seaweed too. Just go slow. Otherwise do like the Iodine protocol maybe? Just replace the Iodine with seaweed water...

### ashryn — 2013-03-10T23:15:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-79994>

you know me, impatient.. And grizz repeatedly said you wouldn't absorb what you didn't need from painting.. My brain tells me I don't need detox, but I guess body has a different idea

# Thread: Vit D levels up with naked sunbathing, also question about CRP
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-levels-up-with-naked-sunbathing-also-question-about-crp.5325/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-01-03T06:39:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-levels-up-with-naked-sunbathing-also-question-about-crp.5325/post-69110>

I had my vit D levels (with other labs) tested back in Sep and they were at 70 nmol/L range (50 - 150). This was just coming out of winter (southern hemisphere). I had my vit D tested again 3 months later, and it had gone up to 102 and that was with only sunbathing naked for about a month and not every day (obviously only when it was sunny and when I wasn't working). I thought that was quite an impressive increase? What are other people's experiences with naked sunbathing? I've been catching as much rays as I can.

Also, I wasn't able to get hs CRP tested in September, but I had CRP tested which was VERY high at 10 mg/L ( < 5 ). So I had CRP tested again last month and it had gone down to 8. Can anyone tell me if CRP is a good indicator of inflammation in the body or do I have to have the hs CRP test done to show a true indication? In that time I have lost 15 kg (33 pounds) and I still have about 25 kg to lose. So the CRP levels may correlate with the weight loss? Thanks.

### Inger — 2013-01-03T07:49:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-levels-up-with-naked-sunbathing-also-question-about-crp.5325/post-69113>

I did not had money to test my vit D levels this past year but the previous year they were great! And I sunbathed naked then too. I have been doing it for a few years already.
I absolutely love it! I have a balcony where no one can see.. or I do it at a lake close to where I live, there are so few people around so you always find a totally lonely place.

I am 100% sure it does wonders for our hormones. I sometimes feel a very slight tingling in my nipples when the sun hits them.. it is so special!
I take care to let the sun shine directly at my women parts too. It kind of gives a wonderful energy.
I am so happy you have found out it does you good too. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-03T17:21:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-levels-up-with-naked-sunbathing-also-question-about-crp.5325/post-69209>

> **amberNZ said**
>
> I had my vit D levels (with other labs) tested back in Sep and they were at 70 nmol/L range (50 - 150). This was just coming out of winter (southern hemisphere). I had my vit D tested again 3 months later, and it had gone up to 102 and that was with only sunbathing naked for about a month and not every day (obviously only when it was sunny and when I wasn't working). I thought that was quite an impressive increase? What are other people's experiences with naked sunbathing? I've been catching as much rays as I can.
> Also, I wasn't able to get hs CRP tested in September, but I had CRP tested which was VERY high at 10 mg/L ( < 5 ). So I had CRP tested again last month and it had gone down to 8. Can anyone tell me if CRP is a good indicator of inflammation in the body or do I have to have the hs CRP test done to show a true indication? In that time I have lost 15 kg (33 pounds) and I still have about 25 kg to lose. So the CRP levels may correlate with the weight loss? Thanks.

Amber, hi! (I've been overseas.) I think that the CRP just isn't accurate enough from what I've read and understood. The fact that it's going down is a good thing, but you really need an hsCRP to give you a window into your inflammation levels. If you google 'hsCRP' you can get some good info as to why it's different than a CRP and perhaps some of that will help convince your doctor why she needs to write a script for the hsCRP.

I also believe that as your inflammation comes down your ability to tolerate the sun will go up. My very pale skin used to burn after 10 minutes. I can now lay out in our NZ summer sun for over an hour with no problems at all. Naked! (Caroline, I live in a city, too... I figure on peek over into my garden from the neighbours will cure them of it happening again
