**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-levels-up-with-naked-sunbathing-also-question-about-crp.5325/post-69325>

> **ashryn said
>
>
> > amberNZ said
>
> **
>
> > Haha!! sometimes its best not to visualise. I hear what you say Inger about discovering how good naked sunbathing is... I can't wait to get out there when it is sunny, but its very hot in the midday sun and we have a hole in the ozone layer over NZ - I guess we get MORE vit d?? I can only do about 10 mins on each side and not get burnt. I love the feeling of the sun hitting my skin, to me it feels like a lovely gentle massage! My 2 teenage daughters have joined me and the 3 of us sunbathe naked, luckily we live in the countryside where no neighbours can see us!
> > But my question about CRP - can anyone help me out there?
>
> Wowee.. you people are having a strange influence on me, thats for sure. there I was, in the midday sun in the middle of summer, under the ozone layer hole sprawled out naked for 30 minutes on each side in potential eyeshot of the builders next door and directly under a flight path.. Oops. Wonder how many people copped an eyeful from their plane
>
>
> Amber, Not sure it felt like a massage to me, more like being a porn star...
> Sunburned my belly a little I think... Now I'm looking for opportunities to do it again! Maybe we'll start going swimming at the nude beach this summer!
> Update...
> Ouch! So despite lotsa vitamin d, coconut oil and animal fats, bits of you that have never seen the sun before don't really know how to act when they're out in public.. And ice on my new sunburn hurts a whole lot. Not looking so hard for more nuding up in the sun any more
>
>
> I read a thread a few weeks ago about preventing sunburn.. Is it possible in Australia too, or is the ozone hole just letting too much in?
> I should have listened to you Amber :/

HAHAHA!! This visual made me laugh so much! So glad your feeling like a porn star. I find the best thing for sunburn is tea tree antiseptic cream - its great for sunburn. I did about 40 mins in the sun yesterday with my 9 year old daughter this time. She had really dry skin so I put coconut oil all over her first and then we naked sunbathed! I think my backside is browner than my front - I like to lay on my stomach. You might have to wait a few days now before you attempt to sunbathe again!

I met with him through a pilot program through LEF. I dont know how to catch up on all the steps up to this point. Im 27 yo M and have had 2 consultls with jack. I weight 155 lbs and on my most recent labs my T and T3 plummeted. My lipid panel is a mess and my T3 was 1.8.

The doc sent me this list of supplements, I am going to get my bloodwork redone in 5 weeks and see where I'm at. I already started supplementing dhea and just recently 10000 IU of D. I am going to try to get most of those supplements on the list. I'm epi paleo and wear my blue blockers at night. I guess thats a start for now. Ask me any questions you want!

### Awainer1 — 2013-01-03T19:04:47+0200

### Patty Cakes — 2013-01-03T19:24:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-t3-journal.5326/post-69226>

If you have a thyroid problem you may have an Iodine deficiency as well but not always. One thing that can happen is that your iodine receptors get clogged up with halides (ie fluoride, bromide, chloride) and you body sees the receptors as "full" and it thinks it's fine. But the reality in this situation is that you are iodine deficient. Be careful with iodine as some people don't do well on it. Read the iodine thread here as there is a lot of good info on there about what to take with iodine if you decide to take it. What you are getting in your seaweed just might be enough for you. You can test at www.hakalalabs.com.

T3 is my drug of choice for Hashis hypothyroidism right now. I cleared all my rT3 and fixed my adrenals at the same time. Everyone is different and we all need to find out what works. I wish you lots of luck and hope you feel better.

### Awainer1 — 2013-01-09T09:54:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-t3-journal.5326/post-70043>

OK so I started taking the T3 last night. I got the prescription and realized its generic cytomel which is fine I guess. I can already feel a difference! Like my thyroid has kicked in after years of being useless. I feel warmer and more energetic and my back which has given me problems for years feels a little better. The vitamin D I think has helped bump up my hormone levels too. I havent started with the other supplements yet as I am waiting to get paid to order them but I'm excited about the results already with the T3. Hopefully it just keeps getting better. Haven't had a chance to look into the whole iodine thing yet but I am planning on it. I dont have the money to get the testing done yet . Great results so far though.

I havent started on the supplements yet they should be here tomorrow. I just listened to the January Webinar and now it seems like I have new supplements I have to go out and buy. I really do not have money for all this so it might take some time to save up. At least I know what I should strive for. I am taking ubiquinone right now because I bought the wrong one at the grocery store. Figure its better than nothing. Also my job is wearing me down pretty good and I hope its not doing damage to my health but I'm kinda stuck in it. Still eating massive amounts of seafood had a big sushi lunch today and squid for dinner. Saturday is oyster day because I get them cheap at the farmers market. All for now.

# Thread: My New Year's thanks to Dr. Kruse
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-years-thanks-to-dr-kruse.5329/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MamaGrok — 2013-01-03T12:38:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-years-thanks-to-dr-kruse.5329/post-69141>

Yeah, I know I already have a success story thread, but this one gets its own page.

1.5 years ago, I had lost almost 40lb on primal, but then my BED got me again and I had gained 10 back, with no end in sight. I posted a desperate plea for help on MDA, and just a week later, discovered Dr. K's new monster thread. This is the result. (I was about to say "end result," but nah, it's not over yet.)

(this is where I tried to post the pic, but it's not letting me!)

The next bit comes from something I wrote a while back in the depths of frustration, and then an addendum thrown on during a powerful moment I experienced Sunday ... while watching a children's book at the theater.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

8 years ago, after my 3rd child was born, I bought the entire Lord of the Rings DVD trilogy. I had never read one page of any of the books, not even The Hobbit (although I was subjected to a horrible cartoon version in the 5th grade), nor seen one minute of the movies in the theaters. I had written them off for geeks, forgetting, of course, that I am one. However, one day I read an article talking about Tolkien's Catholic worldview and realized from it that I would not just like, but love these movies. I sat down with my newborn and watched them one after another, then every minute of the extra scenes and behind-the-scenes clips, for days on end. I then read every word of the Lord of the Rings, the Hobbit, and even the Silmarillion.

The films were full of beauty, the intensity of the good & flaws of humanity, but it was at the end of The Return of the King that I had my most profound contact with the God behind the Lord of the Rings. It's at the Battle of the Morannon, the Black Gate of Mordor, when Aragorn, the long-hidden king, and a small army led by the Captains of the West march on the front door of the vast land of the evil lord Sauron in a selfless attempt to buy time for Frodo's mission to destroy the Ring before Sauron could use it to rule the earth. They, even with their great captains and King, have no hope of winning or even surviving the battle, because behind the gates are legion upon legions of Sauron's orcs and other hideous creatures.

There is no reason to hope.

Yet Aragorn hopes. He knows that there is no other option for him, because this is the only chance for Frodo, and thus, all the races of Middle Earth. He knows it is the right thing to do, even if they all die, and there is no reason to imagine they will do anything but die. He hopes, because as Tolkien tells us through the old hobbit The Gaffer, "Where there's life, there's hope (and need of vittles)." He hopes, because he serves Eru, "The One," who oversees all and will only allow the evil exercise of free will to occur when greater good can come from it. We see the action of The One in The Hobbit when Bilbo "finds" the One Ring, and when Elrond reads the map to Erebor on the exact night of the year it must be read; we see it countless times in The Lord of the Rings, as in the Resurrection of Gandalf the White, the awakening of Theoden, and numerous just-in-the-nick-of-time rescues (trees coming to Dunsinane, anyone?). Aragorn knows that if it's the will of The One that he should live, he will.

And so, exhausted from fighting countless battles before with many losses, and with no reason to expect success, he fights again. Aragorn, whose name given in childhood to conceal him from his enemies was "Estel" - "hope" in Tolkien's invented language - hopes without hope. He doesn't know that Frodo is destroying the ring at that very moment, that eagles are on their way to rescue them, or that the tower of Barad-Dur, which gives inordinate strength to all the evil creatures they fight against, is about to crumble. But he fights on.

When I saw this scene, I was battling against overpowering cravings for food, especially grains & sweets. I had been fighting it for nearly five years, and making no headway at all. I lost on a daily basis. I thought I had a serious problem with self-discipline, and couldn't understand why I couldn't make any gains, no matter how hard I tried or how much I prayed. But as I watched this heroic king rally his men and not just defend, but charge forward to meet the enemy, I saw the spiritual truth behind it: I can never give up. I can never stop praying; I can never stop trying. It's not like going back, quitting, doing the wrong thing willfully, is an option. There is only forward. There is only fight another day. And I committed right then to keep fighting against this, knowing that either one day God would save me or I would die trying.

\*\*\*\*
I was reminded of this today, when I saw Peter Jackson's epic production of The Hobbit in the theater. There, after numerous close encounters, death is now imminent. The dwarves, Gandalf, and the little hobbit cling for their lives to trees, set the surrounding woods on fire to deter the werewolves below, and have nowhere to go. The flames climb higher, and just as death is about to close in, from nowhere comes unexpected help. The eagles save them at the last moment possible, taking them precariously through the air at massive speeds to the peaks of the mountains.

It was then that I realize that Jack Kruse is my eagle. My white wizard at the end of hope. My crazy gollum saving me from myself when it looks like all is lost. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

In 2011 - after more than a decade of fighting a losing battle unarmed - you came and gave me a sword and a shield and an army. Like Gandalf as well, you didn't fight for me, but gave me a bit of knowledge & help here and there, so that my own efforts were magnified, and finally made fruitful. I have begun winning the war, and victories are far more frequent than defeats these days.

My prayer of 2004 has finally been answered, and you were the vehicle.

Thank you.

Beautiful rendition of a success story! Dr. Kruse has been a "light shiner" and life saver for many of us. He has used his light to shine on us so we can find our own abilities to save ourselves. Thanks for sharing.

### caroline — 2013-01-03T15:25:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-years-thanks-to-dr-kruse.5329/post-69185>

WOW!! amazing tribute to your courage and strength and perseverance..... and to the guy with the flashlight. We are all incredibly lucky to have found our way here.
Now you are the beautiful person on the outside that matches who you always were on the inside! ..... the whole package ....doesn't get much better

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-03T15:26:25+0200

### [email redacted] — 2013-01-03T16:34:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-years-thanks-to-dr-kruse.5329/post-69204>

Wonderful analogy MamaGrok! I also feel like I have found the shining light in the darkness. BTW My Mum's horse was in LOTR.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-03T17:55:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-years-thanks-to-dr-kruse.5329/post-69216>

Love reading about your journey! Thanks for sharing! And thanks to Dr. Kruse, especially for bringing the BAB to my life. It's been a major factor in helping me overcome my bingeing.

### Jude — 2013-01-03T20:37:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-years-thanks-to-dr-kruse.5329/post-69236>

" I saw the spiritual truth behind it: I can never give up. I can never stop praying; I can never stop trying. It's not like going back, quitting, doing the wrong thing willfully, is an option. There is only forward. There is only fight another day. And I committed right then to keep fighting against this, knowing that either one day God would save me or I would die trying."

That is one massive amount of grace you received......but those 5 yrs of struggle also attest to the fact that your prayers were being answered as well....'gold is tested in the fire'......you stayed the course!..... you could've given up but somehow you didn't.........You stuck to the truth of the metabolic science that was being flash lighted to you .......... Love TLOTR as well.....it certainly depicts well those noble characteristics we love in true heroes: (courage, fortitude, meekness, honesty, prudence and justice) and all that is necessary for we mortals is that we try to emulate them and we can become heroes as well ....for God, our families and ourselves. A life long struggle!
Congratulations!....on an heroic performance......

# Thread: How to eat optimal with less money? Share your tips..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2013-01-03T13:40:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69156>

I am sure there are other that have also made some stupid mistakes in their life and therefor have a little less money.. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

and eating high quality foods is not the cheapest.

I have figured out if I am really short of money, the pet food store saves me. It sound weird but it is a great thing. I can get reindeer meat minced and frozen, elk bones with marrow.. minced game and organs and beef.. all for very cheap! One pound costs about 1,5-3 â‚¬. That means I can easily use only 4 â‚¬ / day for super healthy foods!
2 years ago in winter I was so out of money I often had a pet food mix with organs and game,
I ate it raw and hold my nose because it was not too delicious IMHO.

Before I did it - I called the number on the package (because it said on the package, for pets only) and explained to the women who answered my call, I have a dog I love so much I give her only the best foods, the same I eat too (I lied a little here because I have no dog.. but I figured these are situations it is allowed to lie..) and I wondered why it says not for human consumption on the package? What is dangerous with it? She said it was nothing, and perfectly safe, it just had to be labeled so because it is sold in the pet food store. It is very clean and handled just the same way as that what goes for human consumption.
I thought, fine, nothing to be scared of.. I felt great from it too. My brother once made meatballs from it and said it tasted fine..lol I preferred to eat it just raw as I feel better from raw meat. I did not liked the taste either and it is much easier to swallow raw for sure!

I buy the cheapest frozen wild caught fish, it costs about 2,60 â‚¬ for one pound. Then.. frozen cooked mussels. Very cheap too. Often they have a special offer, a local small fish, fresh and wild caught, for 4-6 â‚¬ kg, I eat he whole fish, make MHS from the heads. Sometimes they have really great offers on grass fed lamb, fatty and very delicious.

When spring comes.. I collect wild edibles, like nettles and such.They are great! All for free. Or mushrooms and in fall wild growing berries.

Organs are usually pretty cheap! Heart is much cheaper than muscle meat and I like it much better too.. it is amazing!
The fattiest meats are mostly the cheapest also. A win win!

If I lived near the coast I would collect seaweeds for sure! I am planning on taking a trip to Norway this spring and visit my siblings who are living there near the coast and then collect seaweeds and dry them there and take lots of them with me home!

So it is possible to live pretty cheap, and optimal too!

Then when I occasionally have a bit more money.. I use it on oysters and stuff...lol

### fitness@home — 2013-01-03T14:20:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69169>

Inger,

I have never seen any "unprocessed" meat at any pet stores here. I sometimes wonder about the additives they use in the refrigerated pet food mixtures.

The best investment I made was a chest freezer. It allows us to purchase meats and fish while on sale and store until needed. I also split a side of grass fed beef with a co-worker. Cheaper price per pound and you also get all the bones, organs, etc...

I also love growing my own veggies and herbs. Was much better at growing things to maturation in Michigan...especially tomatoes. Still learning how to grow them successfully in Texas

Asian and farmer's markets are two of my favorite places to food shop.

### diane — 2013-01-03T14:26:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69170>

Here it's mostly raw bones then processed "raw" - which means added veggies and not sure what else. And now the pet food is way more expensive than buying it on your own. Almost anything for pets is more expensive than for humans.

I too bought a freezer on kijiji for $50 - best investment! Then I get a 1/4 side of grass fed beef from a friend - which definitely saves. Lots of great cuts, and I know where it's coming from. Actually getting another 1/4 today - and got an extra 4 hearts too! I also ask for any extra bones for both the dogs and now my bone broth.

Agree - asian markets are great too. And lots of people join raw meat co-ops so they get cheaper meat. I haven't yet - but it's on my list! Befriending butchers is also a great idea!

Here mussels are very cheap. And there is fish on sale at various markets. But I'm lucky living in a big city in that I have lots of choice and places to go as well.

### Inger — 2013-01-03T14:41:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69173>

Too weird about your pet food! Here it is only plain, no additives at all. It has gotten very popular to barf dogs and pets so there are many good things to find. Funny how different it can be in different countries!
I have to go to an Asian market too, I never did yet as they are only in big city, not close to me and I have no car so it makes it a bit complicated.. I might find some treasures there though...

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-03T15:24:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69184>

+1 to a chest freezer being an essential and huge money-saver!!

I have relatives in the US. I see that US Wellness meats has something called 'pet burgers' that are a fraction of the price of the other meat and is all grass-fed with the added bonus of offal ground in. Sounds like something I'd be buying if I were there!

I also bought, for $20, a hand-operated meat grinder. This is another money-saver and means I can buy offal and other cheap cuts and make mince simply, while controlling the ingredients.

### diane — 2013-01-03T15:26:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69187>

Lauren - I've been looking at meat grinders lately. Good to know the cheap hand ones work - I was definitely favouring that over the pricey electric ones!

### endless — 2013-01-07T22:58:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69813>

I have a big upright freezer, and I order beef and lamb by the side or quarter in the fall. I am a fan of frozen cooked mussels too, they are pretty inexpensive. I buy prawns when they are on sale. I make it known that I am always up for extra fish if someone has a good day of fishing, and in the fall I ask that hunters save me the organs if they get a deer or elk.

### furface — 2013-01-08T12:12:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69860>

One of my best friends is in the process of buying a boat, and he does a lot of fishing in the coastal B.C. waters, so I should be in line to get a lot of salmon, dungeness, and snow crab, and possibly even some halibut, as well as any other types of fish or seafood he may catch. Even if I have to pay a small amount for some, or even go fishing with him. Should help to save a few shackles!

So once again the old adage, "It's WHO you know!" comes true.

Also I don't know if this would be feasible, but a lot of stores sell produce that is reaching it's "best before" date for extremely bargain prices. If it still looks good, and has no signs of deterioration etc, it might be a good way to save some money...esp if you cut out any "bad" parts, and make a big soup, or stew out of the products in question, and add some inexpensive GF meat, duck, game, or seafood, and make a "complete meal" out of it. That should last a few days, and could even be frozen.

### caroline — 2013-01-08T16:12:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69907>

At my fish market I am able to buy 500gr. packages of Salmon trimmings - I think they call it salmon mince. It is the trimmings from all the beautiful salmon pieces in the display. It is really good stuff. I usually just lightly saute with garlic and ginger and onions and tumeric or whatever and it is great!

# Thread: German Chocolate Mocca Torte with loads of fat
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/german-chocolate-mocca-torte-with-loads-of-fat.5334/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Charlotte — 2013-01-03T15:44:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/german-chocolate-mocca-torte-with-loads-of-fat.5334/post-69195>

ok Bobbi, here goes, just took it out of the oven to cool down.
You need for the cake:
16 eggs
400 g dark chocolate (possibly with Stevia as it brings down Carbs)
550 g peeled almond flour
peel of one fresh lemon and orange (substituted this for candid lemon peel)
2 tablespoons of cinnamon (any other spice is great too such as pumpkin pie spice but I like the cinnamon)
Stevia

separate 11 eggs and add 5 whole eggs to the yolk. Beat until very fluffy and add Stevia, (as much as you see fit) lemon and orange peel and cinnamon. Before prepare chocolate by putting it through a shredder or grater. Then add the chocolate, keeping a little bit for decoration (3 tablespoons)
let it mix for a while and then add the almond flour, 500 g. Let it stir until the batter gets dark. Meanwhile prepare the pan (european style with the side to take away) by greasing it well with butter and take 50 g of almond flour to cover the floor and the sides.
Now beat the eggwhite and fold under the batter by hand, careful not to break the eggwhites.
Put in the pan and stick in the oven. Bake at very low temperatur (max. 150 Â°C) for app. 1,5 hrs.
Take out, take away the side of the pan after a few minutes, put it on a grid to cool down. After cooling down wrap it up and keep it cool over night.

I calculated the Nutrition facts for the cake: 622,65 g of fat, 346,15 g of protein, 63,2 g of carbohydrates

For the frosting you need:
700 g of butter (Kerrygold is best as is soft without salt)
300 g of dark chocolate (same recommendation as above)
500 g of Mascarpone
Stevia
2 tablespoons of freeze dried coffee

Beat the butter until real fluffy with Stevia. Melt the chocolate in a waterbatch and add to the butter, then add the Mascarpone and the coffee. (Instead of coffee you can use any aromas, that you like such as Nutaroma, vanilla, etc.)
Let the mixture cool off a little and meanwhile cut the cake into 3 layers.
Then spread the cream onto the layers, putting one layer on top of the other and keep some for the outside. Then spread the cream on and around the cake still keeping some for the decoration. Sprinkle the chocolate bits left over from the cake on top and on the sides of the cake. Now put the remaining cream into a Piping bag (am not sure if this is the right word) and decorate little stars or whatever you like on the cake. Decorate with Mocca beans or real coffee beans.

Nutrition information for the frosting:
Fat: 910,1 g, Protein: 31,58 g, Carbs: 47,05 g.

Total Nutrition information: Fat: 1.532,8 g, Protein: 377,73 g, Carbs: 110,25.

This cake makes easily 20 pieces as it is very rich. So per piece you have: 76,64 g fat, 18,9 g protein, 5,5 g of carbs. Ratio of fat to protein: 75% fat and 25% protein.

### suse1023 — 2013-04-12T18:40:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/german-chocolate-mocca-torte-with-loads-of-fat.5334/post-83911>

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-12T19:30:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/german-chocolate-mocca-torte-with-loads-of-fat.5334/post-83913>

Ingredients such as glycerin or honey attract moisture so its possible either might make a moister cake. Leaving the unfrosted cake overnight in a tight container with a wadded up damp paper towel might increase the moistness.

There's a cake called La Leche Cake (iirc) in which the cake is soaked with milk before serving. I wonder how that would turn out with coconut or almond flour.

# Thread: All our success stories
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-our-success-stories.5335/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 31
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MamaGrok — 2013-01-03T17:17:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-our-success-stories.5335/post-69208>

I'd love to see all our successes, in very short form, in one thread. And I'm interested, for my own inspiration, specifically in successes that came from following Jack Kruse's protocols. I mean, if I listed all the positive health changes I've had from paleo, it would take six pages, but it's the tweaking based on the Optimal Living protocols that have really made some major headway in some long-term issues. So I'll start!

Leptin Reset - Ended my decade-long battle with out-of-control binge eating disorder. The war is 99.5% won now. Removed ALL cravings for carbohydrates 99.9% of the time, and made eating a totally non-emotional decision, based solely on what's nourishing for me, 99.9% of the time. The BAB has saved my life.

CT - spot-CT on belly definitely removed some belly fat
\- biweekly 52F baths seems to be making my cycles more regular. I think.
\- Cold tolerance is \*phenomenal\* now. This is a HUGE benefit

Labwork - I've learned about my hypothyroidism, autoimmune disease, likely Celiac's, premature entry into perimenopause, and much more

Educational Consults - I've learned how to interpret all those labs, and what specifically I can do to change the problems I have, and how to evaluate progress going forward. I've gained the courage to overcome my med-hatred and get on some desiccated thyroid, finally!

Adding seafood - I don't have any changes notable here yet.

Dark after sunset - So far, just noticing I get sleepy at a reasonable time, which is a big improvement for me.

Circadian T3 protocol - feeling less like death every morning, adrenals must be improving. Not a Dr. K protocol

Iodine - temp has gone from 97 to 98.6 in just two weeks of very slow increases

I especially am interested in seeing what changes y'all can tie \*specifically\* to CT, seafood, dark, mito, & EMF protocols. Like, when the only thing you changed was adding seafood, what improvement did you see after that? I'm doing these things, daily, but I need to do them more intensely, and I need some success stories to rouse me into action!

### vkiernan — 2013-01-03T20:09:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-our-success-stories.5335/post-69230>

MG, I can tell you that adding seafood definitely helped my gut issues. I can tell I am making great progress there. I have been drinking kombucha and eating bubbies pickles along with adding turmeric and black pepper to almost all of my meals for a long time; but, when I started eating sardines every day for lunch and adding in more seafood, the gut really started to change. I also add mustard and turmeric to my sardines to get that extra boost. I had been constipated my whole life and I don't need mag citrate to make things work anymore. This is truly an amazing change for me. I believe when the gut starts working, everything else will follow suit since they are all tied.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-03T22:33:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-our-success-stories.5335/post-69250>

Fixing the gut is a gateway cure to helping the brain and helping the immune system. The tie between both systems is all about flora hormones methylation and mitochondria. Some of this we have yet to cover. We are 18 months in and I think you will see the road to optimal has many way points for you to stop and smell the roses of your journey. I love the idea of one thread to share stories. Do it here. If someone posts other things in here I will delete it.

### maggie — 2013-01-04T13:51:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-our-success-stories.5335/post-69315>

Thanks, Mama G, for starting this thread. I look forward to hearing about others' successes. Here are three of mine:

1\. After starting epi-paleo, I dropped 10+ pounds and about five inches from my waist without noticing. I was moving across country, forgot my scale, and only found out a couple of months after moving. I wasn't technically overweight at 5'2" 124 pounds, but bloat-y around the middle. I feed my elderly dog epi paleo too and she has been seizure free and off phenobarbital for four months. She's my canary in the coal mine. When I feel discouraged I think of her amazing cure after 13 years on meds, and tell myself I just have a bigger brain to manage!

2\. At Dr. K's suggestion, I tested for antibodies to dairy and found I had a lot, even though I was almost, virtually dairy free (just some half and half in morning coffee). A couple of months completely off dairy, and my chronic sinus pain is gone. After all these years it feels weird to feel air passing freely through my right nostril. I'm looking forward to thyroid improvements because of this change.

3\. There have been emotional/psychological shifts; those are a bit personal to mention, but are very real to me. One I'll mention is that I just wrote a strong email to a bHRT plan that I subscribe to. I tend to just shrug and go away if things aren't working out but yesterday I wrote very clearly about what was bothering me and asked for answers to specific, bulleted questions. I received an acknowledgment in one day--it often takes a week! This is new behavior for me and I don't know where it came from--kind of out of the blue, like the weight loss.

So thank you, Dr. K, for sharing your brilliance. After listening to the Mitochondrial Rx, I'm eager to see what 2013 brings.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-09T14:50:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-our-success-stories.5335/post-70063>

Today, I walked out of my doctors office with the biggest grin on my face you have ever seen, We spent 1,5 hrs. going over my labs and the results are WOW.
There were more than 150 tests done and it would be far too much to post, so here just the most important ones and their improvement-

Q10 was off, now good 879 (range 750-1200)
Leptin was 3x too high, now within normal, not even at the high end (8,8 (3,7-11,1 range))
HbA1c 5,8 (was 12)
LDL Cholesterol 156 (range 10)
Hormones, all normal except for DHEA
2-OH/16-OH-Estron ratio 8 (range >2,39)
Progesteron low, but within range
same with prolaktin, free DHEA, 2-Methoxy-Estron, + 4-....,Cortisol, FSH, LH, Pregnenolone. All at the low end, but within range.,
Methylationactivity 3,3 (range >0,74)

and so on and so on.
Now the bad new if you want to call it that (I do not as to me that is now also just a matter of time)
SHBG 180 (range 17,1 - 108,2)
DHEA 34,9 (range 40 - 200)
CRP 0,81 (still some inflammation, but was 5,0 before (range <0,3)
and it seems that I have had EpsteinBarr without ever noticing, so the Igs are way off. But the Doc said that the immune system will take care of that when it gets stronger, So lots of CT and he recommended Autographis which is a very bitter plant that is an Immunmodulator, Lysin and Colostrum (why do Doctors just almost never take their own medicine???)

So there is not really much wrong anymore and what is, is only a matter of time, considering that I only started on 25th of August. Loosing all that weight surely helped tremendously.

But the best was that he said to me that I can live to be 120 and he meant it and I will never have cancer and that I am his best ever patient. (he did say that before though). My mom is 93 now, still wearing high heels and goes shopping on foot, never missing her hairdressers appointments either. So her neighbour told me. She still doesn't talk to me, but she is stubborn. Maybe one has to be stubborn to become that old??? Guess what, who cares? I got the best of her already: HER GENES!
What I like best? Imagine, I am 60 years old now, if I live to be 120, what all can I do still? That blows my mind. Another 60 years, I can build an Empire (or whatever else comes to my mind).

My doc and I really had fun looking and comparing today. The patients in the waiting room didn't though.

So guys, I am a really HAPPY GIRL today.

Charlotte.. just WOW. I would love you to live to 120!!! That is what I am aiming for too, btw..lol love how one can feel just younger and younger even if one gets older! Most days I do feel like a child somehow.. much better than in my teenager years for sure. Weird.. even if I am much older now!

Caroline.. too old...? Mom is turning 61 and she goes every morning in the icehole, dad is 65 and he goes too (most days..lol) but he always goes when he is in the sauna, 3 times for dipping in the icehole in about one hour!). Caroline I guess we never get too old.. maybe we need to do it a bit carefully in the beginning for sure, but huh.. then, life all of a sudden is so fun!

I am not always feeling like going in my hole either. But I just go out and tell myself "just for a second". No need to stay any longer, but you MUST get clean (as I have forbidden myself to use the shower..lol) and then I just go and say loud.. oh come on, just DO it! I am just DOING it (and I say the doing really loud) and then I step in. I never regret it! I am my own health couch all the time. I sometimes get lazy.. eat too much cooked or not the optimal stuff and then I need to talk to myself. Do you want to feel better? Yes? Then start eating everything raw! And I do and feel better so fast!It is all very easy when one knows what to do.

The bad hair day that was mentioned here.. that is so cool. I used to have bad hair days all the time.. Now, I still have thin hair that always look terrible before I use my hair straightener. But now it is shinier and it keeps the form much better! It really is a difference! IDK if it is all the seaweeds I am drinking and eating or what.. but I like it! I am not doing anything else different. I still wash almost daily with ghassul mud, and use my hair dryer too.. as I have done for years.
I cannot wait how my hair will look in one year.. I guess great!

### caroline — 2013-01-11T17:30:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-our-success-stories.5335/post-70335>

Inger - you say things so beautifully .... you always give me so much inspiration! Thankyou. No - you are right - I don't think you/we are ever too old - I am just feeling overwhelmed I think - I don't know where all the years have gone - they were great years - but now we/I have to follow the light! Have your parents always done the ice hole thing? or is that your superb influence?? I really can't wait to see you when you grow up

I do intend to swim in your icehole one day

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-12T08:14:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-our-success-stories.5335/post-70380>

> **caroline said**
>

>
> I do intend to swim in your icehole one day

This quote really touched me this AM.......it speaks to another human being being touched by another who had announced her presence........by painting their own picture and running their own race. It is very Starfish like. You can see it in the post before you even feel it............

Do you have to see IT, before you feel IT? Presence is the result of confidence, and it can be felt, even before it is ever expressed to the world. The confidence comes from knowledge of self, and other information absorbed due to a willingness to learn, explore, and discover, then share. When the knowledge, sharing, and energy come together in one person, it is presence. It says here I am. Your work begins to take on a certain gravity or magnetism. New discoveries and new opportunities begin to be pulled to you. Things become easier for you because of the energy you share with others. Presence is more than just being there. It is not just about being physically present in a certain place. It is about who you are, your character, values, knowledge, your essence, your beliefs, your emotional state of being, your ability to connect with those around you and your confidence. With it, you begin to create your inner masterpiece by continually painting your canvas and others canvas, with big bold colors and with large brushstrokes. Yes, it is time for all of us now to paint.

> [MamaGrok said:](/goto/post?id=69208)

> I'd love to see all our successes, in very short form, in one thread. And I'm interested, for my own inspiration, specifically in successes that came from following Jack Kruse's protocols. I mean, if I listed all the positive health changes I've had from paleo, it would take six pages, but it's the tweaking based on the Optimal Living protocols that have really made some major headway in some long-term issues. So I'll start!
>
> Leptin Reset - Ended my decade-long battle with out-of-control binge eating disorder. The war is 99.5% won now. Removed ALL cravings for carbohydrates 99.9% of the time, and made eating a totally non-emotional decision, based solely on what's nourishing for me, 99.9% of the time. The BAB has saved my life.
>
>
> Labwork - I've learned about my hypothyroidism, autoimmune disease, likely Celiac's, premature entry into perimenopause, and much more
>
> Educational Consults - I've learned how to interpret all those labs, and what specifically I can do to change the problems I have, and how to evaluate progress going forward. I've gained the courage to overcome my med-hatred and get on some desiccated thyroid, finally!
>
> ...
>
> I especially am interested in seeing what changes y'all can tie \*specifically\* to
> \- switching to unprocessed foods
> \- switching to paleo/primal diet (Wolf/Sisson) as recommended by Dr. K)
> \- leptin reset
> \- recommended labwork
> \- educational consults
> \- CT
> \- seafood
> \- dark
> \- mito protocol
> \- EMF protocol
>
> Like, when the only thing you changed was adding seafood, what improvement did you see after that? I'm doing these things, daily, but I need to do them more intensely, and I need some success stories to rouse me into action!

Spell them out! (if possible) If you saw X changes after starting CT and Y changes after starting to be mindful of EMF, say that; don't say "since reading Dr. Kruse, I've had X & Y changes."

I'm really looking for \*specific\* inspiration to help me focus on the bits where I'm weak. I'm donig great on diet, labwork, seafood, etc., but need to get pumped up about getting serious about lights after dark, so (other than getting sleepy at a reasonable hour), what changes have you seen that you know started when you got serious about lights after dark? (And you know it was the light b/c you didn't also start 5 other protocols at the same time) That kind of thing...

### caroline — 2013-01-31T01:16:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-our-success-stories.5335/post-73401>

for me - the really big thing is - no electronics after dark. I sleep so much better. If i have the tv on for awhile I just close my eyes and listen and no laptop in the evening. If I read on my ipad in the evening I will have a terrible sleep. I also was using some liquid stevia in my green tea and I will be up 3 or 4 times during the night - going to the bathroom. It is like my kidneys are irritated or something. If I don't use it I will be up only once and sometimes not at all.

# Thread: Dr Doug McGuff, MD - Anyone follow him ?

### Thor — 2013-01-03T23:47:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-doug-mcguff-md-anyone-follow-him.5336/post-69255>

.
not offend anyone but just wondering any thoughs about Dr Doug McGuff, MD ? Sure seems very knowledge about Paleo & how it ties into exercise. I could be wrong but he seems on point.

To me he seems on point to exercise correctly - train to gain. His book is called "body science" - how your phenotype is expressed in exercise. IL-15 gene is one type and how it shows in your body type. Bio-chemistry and how global metabolic conditioning matters. Dr Mcguff, MD - really gives a deep geek lecture. Here's a video to explain this better than I can.

\[video=youtube\_share;2PdJFbjWHEU\]http://youtu.be/2PdJFbjWHEU\[/video\]

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-doug-mcguff-md-anyone-follow-him.5336/post-69322>

I've been following his workout prescription for more than a year. This is in the context of coming off a career of amateur bicycle racing and a very stressful job that has led to trashed adrenals and circadian mis matches. I've seen significant strength gains without siginifcant size gains doing HIT style lifting once every 7 days with little or no other exercise.

Tom

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-23T09:18:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-doug-mcguff-md-anyone-follow-him.5336/post-72491>

I only want strength gains without size. It's best to be un assuming...then when needed you can whip out your powers. lol

### [email redacted] — 2013-01-23T10:15:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-doug-mcguff-md-anyone-follow-him.5336/post-72494>

> **Zorica Vuletic said**
>
> I only want strength gains without size. It's best to be un assuming...then when needed you can whip out your powers. lol

Don't worry about that ZV, you simply are not equipped to get size. Just like if you took steroids your testes wouldn't shrink. So far, he has the most accurate descriptor of what exercise actually does to the male body, and most of the principles can be transferred to how it will work with Women as well. It stops short of exploring/explaining the additional and subsequent reactions with female chemistry.

There is nothing out there now, short of unreadable scientific publications, that does a great job of exploring exercise and female physiology.

The net result is most of what he says does work the same in women, it's just that there is OTHER stuff that happens with women and it's not clear what the downhill/unintended consequence is.

ZV if you are eating epi paleo you will not build too much muscle. Only when you start doing drastic things to re-engineer all your sex hormones and brain signals can women even put on bulky muscle. Compact and useful muscle fibres in conjunction with female hormones is tight and compact. It's a whole different texture than bodybuilder bulk.

### hellojtm — 2013-01-31T00:42:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-doug-mcguff-md-anyone-follow-him.5336/post-73397>

I followed the BBS workout for several months a couple of times. I never gained any size but I always progressed in strength. I'll make a couple of edumacated guesses about why that is, order of importance.

1\. I wasn't eating enough to gain size (independent of macronutrients).
2\. The workout is fairly low volume, unless you do even lighter weights. Higher volume would be more anabolic, IMO.
3\. The workout is infrequent, giving you many days of "extra" rest before the next stimulus. This may favor your fast twitch muscle generation, but
4\. If you want to get bigger you probably shouldn't worry about fast/slow twitch muscle, with a couple weeks off you will start to express the fast twitch.

All that said, I did gain 2.5lbs of muscle on the program (per DEXA), while losing 17.5lbs of fat (from paleo lifestyle). By going to this program I probably reduced my gym time from 3 hours to 25 minutes a week. I was going to do it again starting in January and simply add a ton of calories to the equation. In fact, I started it, but a friend convinced me to transition to a more traditional strongman method including squats and deadlifts. I like the BBS and have taught it to a couple of co-workers with varying results. I'll always have it in my back pocket, but for now I really like the feeling of picking up heavy weights.

Would be interested if anyone HAD gained size on this program, with no other alterations than overeating.

best,
jesse

### shah78 — 2014-07-17T19:56:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-doug-mcguff-md-anyone-follow-him.5336/post-134756>

The idea that only strength gains , not mass gains can be derived by a combo of BBS + low carb paleo+CT is inaccurate.. I've spent approx.46 hours in the gym over the last 40 months and gained over twentyfive lbs of mucle while losing around ten lbs of fat. I'm am 58 yrs old now and recovering from CFS/FM/Hypothyroid/adrenal fatigue. . ...My new biohack it going to lower the time in the gym for the next three months to forty minutes a month and add lots more saunas keeping my Cold Therapy at ten hours a week. ....Essentially, no one in the" real world" believes me.  Even people who watch me work out and then spend 90% of my gym time, siting in the sauna, think I come back some other day and workout again. But no, just more saunas. Hilarious. I feel like Jed Clampett, walking around Beverly Hills .or driving around in the "truck"., except I have Doug Mcguff riding upfront where Jethro usually sits, and Jack Kruse is sitting in Granny"s rocker. Pretty remarkable and pretty funny.

### kovita — 2014-07-17T20:22:32+0300

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-07-20T02:40:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-doug-mcguff-md-anyone-follow-him.5336/post-134955>

Centration is the ability for the body to be maximally congruent and balanced
Ie joint centration

When we observe the infant in its first year of development we see the installation of all motor patterning with full centration
Ie saggital, ipsilateral, contralateral in that order.
This is why i always laugh that people in gyms constantly load the saggital plane, the most basic of all patterns.

# Thread: The love of your life ....... part deux...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-01-04T02:39:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69262>

Do you think it is possible to have more than one ........ "The Love of your Life"?

On one hand ...It seems like it should be possible I guess... but ... on the other hand.....I don't really think it is....
Thoughts???

### ashryn — 2013-01-04T02:54:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69263>

I think it is possible, because we grow and change over time, I am not the same person I was 10 years ago at all.. Then again, if the love of your life grows with you and you both encourage the changes in one another, I think it is also possible to have one LoYL for your whole life... If you have one, why would you look for another? I suspect if for whatever reason one love has gone, that another may appear... Sometimes they may appear before the LoYL has physically gone but maybe he/she is spiritually gone from you.. And I also think LoYL2 may appear to teach you what you have forgotten to love in yourself, and may stick around just until you have re learned the lesson. I think lots of couples who go through an affair and come out the other side still being each other's LoYL are experiencing this.

At least that's how it looks to me..

### caroline — 2013-01-04T03:15:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69266>

I think when someone is "The Love of Your Life" then you have been growing and changing and learning and loving and laughing and crying together and supporting each other and putting them first.

### caroline — 2013-01-04T03:18:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69267>

### ashryn — 2013-01-04T03:19:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69268>

### caroline — 2013-01-04T04:01:28+0200

### caroline — 2013-01-06T20:06:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69659>

very cute

Are you trying to tell us something??

### Charlotte — 2013-01-07T11:12:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69735>

To be honest, I am not sure, how similar or different we are from other animals, but when I look at the many kinds of different animals, there are surely differences also about their "love life". The grey geese for example chose their partner and then they stay together for the rest of their life like the albatross also, then there is the females spider who tries to kill the male after the act. Then there is the bowerbird who only builds a nest to impress the female, but never intends to live with her there, in the world of the hyenas, it is the females who reign, sometime they even develop male genitals. Most whales are downright promiscuous like penguins, shimpanses and some other species. And lions kill the offspring of their former rival, so the female can get pregnant soon again.
So I think like in nature, also humans are different. There are people who meet and stay together for all their life, many just can never be faithful (male and female alike), some can raise their offspring together, but that is it, then there are others who only like the same gender, and so on.
In nature, all is possible and actually happens.
Before the age of Romantisism in Europe marriage was not intended to be a romantic venture but a way to keep wealth together, to make sure, girls get married off into a good family and the man marries a woman who can represent with him their social status, in short, they were marriages of convenience. Some of these marriages were quite successful and many "arranged" themselves around the inevitable.
How it actually happened in the paleolithic age I do not know, I even do not know if such a thing as marriage even existed. I tend to believe it did not. The act was performed and the child was a consequence of it which was then reared as part of the clan.
I realize that my view is a little detached as I am not a romantic person and I do not intend to destroy anybodies romantic feelings about the topic, but I think a little less romanticism is quite healthy. I tend to view this topic unemotional or prosaic.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-07T11:50:43+0200

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-07T17:22:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69777>

> **Most whales are downright promiscuous like penguins.**

This does nothing to address Caroline's original question, but furthers the point of diversity among animals. Turns out, not all penguins are promiscuous. Here in New Zealand (on the Otago Peninsula) there is a sub species of penguins called Yellow Eyed Penguins that don't live elsewhere. You can visit their colony and view them from a distance. They are observed, only, not interacted with or influenced by the ornithologists who document them. These penguins are strictly monogamous. In fact, there is a male homosexual pair there who have been together for years. Several years into their relationship, they adopted a chick whose mother had died and have successfully raised this baby to adulthood together. Life is fascinating!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-08T16:11:11+0200

As to the OP ... yes I believe you can have more than one "love of your life." We're all different and we love different people in different ways.

The first man I truly fell in love with and whom I thought I would marry, broke my heart into so many pieces I never really recovered. He was the first "love of my life" and is still. My DH is also the love of my life but the love I feel for him is completely different and not just because we've been together for (holy cripes) 39 years.

Open your heart to not only life but to all possibilities and you will discover many profound truths.

# Thread: More on Factor X:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-factor-x.5338/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-04T11:57:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-factor-x.5338/post-69297>

Mammals make body heat.......Why did this help them after a bollide hit the Yucatan 65-67 million years ago?

The Evolution of Body Heat?

When oxidized, the great majority of our food and stored energy goes to the production of HEAT. What governs this? It is multifactorial but adrenaline, thyroid, cortisol and mitochondria quality are just a few \[2\]. Active tissues contain more mitochondria. Heat makes us mammals and birds. We have hot bodies, precisely 37C for the great majority.

In the 'Hot Brain: Survival, Temperature, and the Human Body' the authors theorized that temperature gave us advantages over eukaryotic infections (yeast, fungal origins -- we are eukaryotic) which plagued bird/reptile species which were not armed with high 37C temperatures or fever-inducing capabilities \[3\]. It is a very interesting theory. Control of thermoregulation (heat loss v. heat gain) is believed to have evolved in the brain of therapsids. Our sinuses are larger. Mammalian brains have a Circle of Willis where 4 arteries (internal carotids and vertebral arteries) provide a complete internal brain circulation with collaterals, such that despite blockage of one or more of the 4 major arteries circulation in the brain and to the body remains intact. Unfortunately only 25-33% of us have a 'perfect' classic circle of Willis; others have degrees of narrowing or asymetry in certain areas or another. Photos courtesy: Hot Brain, pp. 44, 142.

Recently a microbiologist, Casadevall, from Albert Einstein had the same theory that the rise of mammals can be attributed to 'endothermy and homeothermy \[which\] are thought to contribute to mammalian resistance to mycosis by creating a thermal exclusionary zone that inhibits most fungal species. The remarkable resistance of mammals to mycotic diseases is probably a combination of a vertebrate immune system, with both innate and adaptive arms, and elevated body temperatures... The currently favored hypothesis for the demise of dinosaurs and end of the age of reptiles is a bolide impact approximately 65 million year ago with the possibility that other events, such as increased volcanism, contributed to disrupting the cretaceous ecosystem. That ecological calamity was accompanied by massive deforestation, an event followed by a fungal bloom, as the earth became a massive compost. Although one cannot know which spores were present at the time, the likelihood that pathogenic fungi existed at the K-T boundary is enhanced by the finding that the potential for pathogenicity probably arose independently several times in evolution...'\[3\]

'Although we do not know the timeline for the recovery of the planet climate, it is estimated that photosynthesis was shut down for 6 months and climate cooling persisted for at least 9 years, and the occurrence of a fungal bloom sufficient to have left fossil evidence implies that surviving animals were exposed to massive numbers of fungal spores. The darkened skies and cooler temperatures that accompanied the K-T cataclysm would have shielded the sun and reduced the ability of ectothermic creatures such as reptiles to induce fevers by insolation, a necessary activity for protection against fungal diseases. Hence, it is reasonable to posit that ectothermic creatures unable to induce behavioral fevers and in weakened states from environmental stress would have been at a severe disadvantage relative to small mammals with their innate thermal exclusionary zones for fungal growth. Further complicating the situation for reptiles is that eggs can be vulnerable to fungal attack, whereas mammalian progeny would be protected in placentae.'\[3\]- From Dr. B. G.

This energy management system allows eutherian mammals to fight off the fungal explosion in the environment through fevers, thus eliminating our susceptibility to those organisms. Three questions for you: so, how does the Cold Environment of the post KT event world leverage Heat Shock Proteins? is this part of the process or is it a side process as a result of the Cold Environment? How does the epigenetics and circadian mismatches that Factor X leverages make us more vulnerable to similar organisms - such as MRSA or candida overgrowth? It's not just the carbs that's contributing to this vulnerability - our environment is also at play with regard to our susceptibility, correct? Thus our mitochondrial in-efficiency, as a result of electron loss/leakiness through consumption of carbs is limiting our ability to fight infection through endothermic regulation.

# Thread: What is the best way to cook/prepare duck?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2013-01-04T14:17:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69317>

Just wondering what is the best way to prepare duck? I've never had it before, but one of our largest grocery store chains...Real Canadian SuperStore, has utility grade duck on sale this week for $2.38 lb. so I thought I might try it. I've read on here that the fat is very healthy for you, and since I haven't been eating any chicken or turkey while on the Leptin Rx, I thought some duck would be a nice change of pace from bacon, seafood/shellfish, and GF beef & lamb. Not saying I'll buy duck on a regular basis, but I wouldn't mind trying it out.

Thanks in advance. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-04T16:04:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69335>

I've de-skinned it and rendered the fat from the skin, and make cracklins for crispy goodness to sprinkle over top; you can roast it. or braise it. Joy of cooking has some great recipes that you can modify to meet Paleo standards.

I butchered mine up - and then braised it w/ mushrooms, and bockchoy in a beef broth seasoned w/ tumeric, corriander, cumin, garlic, black pepper and salt in a dutch oven. quite tasty - said cracklins were sprinkled over top for xtra crunchy goodness when served

### David Michael — 2013-01-04T16:06:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69336>

furface,
Here's how we cook it (assuming this is a whole duck):
\- We usually put it in to cook in the morning and let it roast all day.
\- Thaw in the fridge or a water bath.
\- Leave at room temperature for about an hour. Cut off the wing tips and save for stock.
\- Lightly score the skin in a criss-cross pattern; do not cut into the meat.
\- Pierce the fat layers along the back, thighs and wings.
\- Rub with olive oil, salt and pepper.
\- Place on rack and then into a roasting pan.
\- Roast at 400F for 20 minutes; turn oven down to 300F; roast an additional 40 minutes; turn oven down to 180F.
\- After a couple of hours, insert a meat thermometer probe between the thigh and body. Get it down close to where the leg joins the body.
\- Cook until the thermometer reads 170F. If it is getting close to the time you want to eat and the duck is not done, increase the oven temperature to hurry it along.
\- Let it rest for 15 minutes, then carve. Save all the fat as if it were liquid gold (it is). Save the bones and carcass for stock, it will make delicious soup.
Good Luck!
P.S. Except for scoring and piercing, this is the same procedure we use for a chicken or a turkey as well.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-04T16:34:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69341>

This is how I make it (Ã¤hm, used to make it) Now I do not do the pineapple anymore.
Put some fat (GHEE is perfect) into large meat pan and roast the chicken on all sides, piercing it carefully with a fork to release the fat. (my Mom used to juice a little pineapple, put the juice in a syringe and inject it all over the duck (makes the meat very tender from the enzymes)
Add a little water and keep frying it, until you have a nice dark gravy with lots of fat in it. That way it gets tender without getting dry.
Then after 1-2 hrs. put it in the oven and roast it on all sides until it is nicely roasted, still pinching with a fork occasionally to drain more fat into a pan underneath. Add the fat to the gravy and take out the duck when it is done. Sometimes I baste it also with a little bit of orange juice (fresh) and orange peel. Gives it a nice flavor.

### furface — 2013-01-04T19:01:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69367>

Thanks everyone for all of your suggestions and instructions. I may try a combination of methods, and see what happens. I'm planning on having it either tomorrow, or on Sunday, so if you don't see my replying the next few days you'll know that something went terribly wrong.

### furface — 2013-01-05T17:36:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69494>

Well I've just finished my first ever duck meal, and it was...SUCCULENT!! Extremely Delicious!

I took a little bit from each suggestion/recipe, as well as a few youtube recipes, and roasted it in my convection oven @ 350 degrees F, for about an hour and a half until it reached an internal temp of 175 degrees F at the thickest part of the thigh. It was a 4.3 lb. young duck.

I used some garlic powder, sea salt, pepper, thyme, orange zest, one whole orange cut into quarters stuffed into the cavity, along with half a sweet onion, and a couple of sprigs of fresh mint leaves. (Don't ask why I put them in, but I thought since it smells so good, it might help the duck taste good. lol...which it did).

Anyway, thanks everyone for all of your help, and I not only have a delicious few meals, but some great bones for stock, and some liquid gold duck fat/juice. (Some of which I smothered over some steamed broccoli to have along with the freshly roasted duck!)

### diane — 2013-01-05T17:39:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69495>

Jason - you've just convinced me to get a duck! I almost picked one up the last time I was at the grocery store as they were on sale, but I passed. Now I'm kicking myself! Thanks for asking the question!

### furface — 2013-01-05T17:52:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69496>

> **diane said**
>
> Jason - you've just convinced me to get a duck! I almost picked one up the last time I was at the grocery store as they were on sale, but I passed. Now I'm kicking myself! Thanks for asking the question!

You're most welcome, Diane! Actually that's why I got one...Real Canadian SuperStore here in the Vancouver area, have young ducks on sale this week for $2.38 or so per lb. And after reading on here how duck fat is considered "liquid gold", and that it's a good alternative to chicken or turkey, I thought I'd try it. Glad I did. lol

Why I tried the mint, I have no idea, but I love mint with lamb, and figured lamb is "dark meat", as is duck, so why not? Good move, added a nice little bit of "sweetness" along with the orange, to the meat.

Hope you try it, Diane, and enjoy it.

Later I'm going to make duck stock, and duck soup/stew with the leftovers.

### Destiny — 2013-01-05T18:22:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69503>

Yum, this sounds so good. I have to get myself a duck!

### furface — 2013-01-05T18:35:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69506>

Just an additional note...I poked a number of holes into the skin (not the flesh though) with a wooden skewer before roasting, and ended up with about 3/4 cup of duck fat/juice from the 4.3 lb. duck.

I began roasting it breast side down, then after 20 minutes, took it out, flipped it over, and cooked it the rest of the time, breast side up. I also kept the cavity closed after I stuffed it with the orange, onion, mint, and spices with the wooden skewer.

After the roasting time was over, I let it sit on my cutting board, covered in foil for about 15 minutes before carving.

### furface — 2013-01-06T02:13:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69555>

Just finished a 44 minute cold shower, but before I stepped into the shower, I started a duck stock in my slow cooker. I used the giblets, the leftover cooked bones and carcass, wings, drumstick bones, added some celery, baby carrots, onions, rock sea salt, and black peppercorns.

I'm going to let it cook on high for a few hours, then turn it on low and let it simmer overnight.

Hope it turns out okay.

Under Dairy, of course, I put "Just say No". They said that in their reading, they read in some places certain dairy = yes, some place, no. I referred them to the Dr. Kruse forum and said (I promise I really said this!), whenever there are arguments on paleo forums about whether a food is good or bad, I use Dr. Kruse's advice because I feel he's studied it more extensively than all of them combined.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-04T20:01:12+0200

Am amazed at some of the responses in that FB thread...........

### hellojtm — 2013-01-04T21:56:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-69383>

I think your point about context is a major one. Glad to see it articulated. I think you may have beat Mat and Robb out of that thread with pure verbiage though

I don't think you guys differ too much except for the context.

My opinion means nothing but I think there is a lot of importance to the idea that something in your blood is bad for you, but how it got there is even worse for you. Matt's example of low levels of the peptide that dissolves BCM7 in combo with a leaky gut would put you in a situation where BCM7 is in the blood stream. This is enough to imply nobody should really be ingesting it unless you are regularly testing all your peptide levels and gut permeability, etc... I can't see the low likelihood of one having that biological situation be a good argument that we should keep drinking it. I see all the grain proteins in the same light.... it seems like if your gut is tightly sealed and your general immune system is not taxed then you'll probably deal with it fine, but we know leaky gut exists with some unknown myriad of causes and we know that brings the grain proteins into contact with the blood stream. Not to mention the immune response that must be going on in the gut at all times with those "foreign bodies" (see V's enterolab, i.e.).

OK, this is getting long but the point I \_really\_ wanted to get at was that the context door swings both ways, too often we (and by we I mean me) turn into hypochondriacs when we find out new information. Have to look at our context, if it's summer and I'm 11% body fat and feeling great I'm eating fruit!!

take care Dr. K.

jesse

### kathylu — 2013-01-04T22:21:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-69387>

> **Jack Kruse said**
>
> Am amazed at some of the responses in that FB thread...........

I'm not. Since casomorphins are biologically active peptides with opioid effects, of course people are defending dairy strongly. They are addicted. You see it from that first pair bond formation with momma (maybe that's why mother's milk has morphine like effects?) to adults who can give up any food except dairy. I also think that dairy decreases selective gut permeability...useful in infants who must be able to absorb huge protein molecules in colostrum in order to receive functional immunity from their mother...but definitely detrimental in adults. Yup, no dairy after three years of age makes the most sense. Another example that our evolved brains get in our way...humans are the only animal that routinely consumes dairy past infancy.

When people ask what's wrong with dairy, I always ask why humans are the only animal on the planet that drinks another animal's milk? I mean, doesn't that at least sound like a bad idea? You never see, in nature, one animal suckling off another. It's just not natural.

THANK YOU... Thank you for pointing to this facebook thread/discussion. Art de Vany's answer made something say "kilck" in my brain. Gotta love the truth.

I have been indulging in raw, organic, grassfed butter from the mountains of Austria. It sounds good, or? I thought too.
I ate a lot, maybe 200 gram or more a day. In my coffee in my food.

Then a few days ago (after about 1-2 weeks of raw butter feast) I realized something is amiss. My heart feels not right. Believe me I have a weak heart and I feel it there at first mostly. It feels a tiny bit like inflamed. I thought it must be because of emotional stress I had last weeks but then.. thinking more about it I come to the conclusion it must be the butter.

### [email redacted] — 2013-01-05T16:56:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-69488>

Well, I think that Mat Lalonde brought up some great points but I also think that you brought up some great points. I think that what you guys have in common is that you both want calcium and phosphorus but disagree on the source. Mattie ole' boy says why not milk? while you seem to be more in favor of sourcing it from possibly shellfish. At the end of the day I don't think anyone would argue with you that dairy is superior to fish/shellfish but I do think it is at least somewhat warranted to say that raw grass fed high fat dairy is likely okay for some people. Remember that the Sherpas eat straight butter while they climb Mt. Everest (from the podcast You did on fatburningman.com).

I also appreciate the difference in approach between you guys. You see more clinical cases where people with compromised health come to see you while Mat sees people probably in moderate+ health. For somebody with a totally compromised gut or AI milk is probably goign to be a super NONO, which shellfish is probably a super YES, and that makes perfect sense. For somebody with nice muscle mass, low fat, low stomach fat and very active though different rules are definitely going to apply.

So when someone says milk is good or milk is bad it is absolutely 100% necessary to finish that sentence and say milk is good for WHO, or milk is bad for WHO. See what I mean? Almost half of the US population is obese now and 2/3 are overweight or obese, many of these people are VERY unhealthy and have serious issues. Dairy is probably going to hinder them from recovery. But that doesn't mean that milk would compromise the health of an active, muscular low bf athlete. Context is key here.

### Linz — 2013-01-08T10:27:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-69848>

Lauren
Pigs have a digestive system similar to humans so they are very unlikely to be fed an ideal diet. Cattle and sheep are herbivores and it is not difficult to feed them a grass/forage based diet which they convert to good quality fat and protein but pig diets are probably no better than SAD. I think they are much healthier living with outside access for light/temp variation/exercise/stress reduction etc but they need more than grass. In the past in the UK they were actually fed human food as swill - left over food from hotels/hospitals etc which was all boiled up together - but it is no longer allowed due to disease risk. Without looking up any details I expect the feed used now is a mixture of cereal, soy and various proteins and oils to give consistent weight gains with little attention to 06/3 ratio or other long-term health aspects (they have short lives). If we can't even feed ourselves properly ...

I eat pork and bacon (preferably outdoor reared) but have reservations and don't know where an ideal source would come from other than wild boar in its natural habitat foraging for acorns, nuts, grubs, truffles! Your wild caught from the mountains sounds perfect.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-01-26T08:45:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-72713>

From what I've read BCM7 isn't something that forms in all milk. There is BCM7 in human milk but it isn't at all the same. BCM7 doesn't even form in milk from cows containing a genetic variation referred to as A2 with Proline at position 67 of the amino acid chain. A1 or A1/A2 mix genetics produce milk with Histadine at 67 which is what forms BCM7.

Getting a good source of only A2 milk might not be the easiest though. I've heard certain types of cows have more of one than the other such as Holstein is all A1 while Guernsey/Jersey have more of the A2 only. Of course the only way to know is to have a cow tested.

I really want to try good raw Jersey milk because I've heard of how great the nutrition is and what benefits it has. I've heard quite a bit of benefit can come from good clean milk. I don't think my ancestry would be bad at processing milk so I'd love to try some of the good stuff.
