### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-16T19:37:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-circadian-cycles-matter-to-humans-should-we-pay-deep-attn-to-them.5429/post-71136>

Tonight on the free Q & A we unleash the Optimal reset..............you read to to test the metal of the Mitochondrial Rx? You think your version of healthy is "more correct" Well test yourself and your beliefs with the Easy bio hack. I mentioned this last March at the Mastermind sessions that I use three labs for the quick bio-hack.........Tonight on the free Q & A hear why HS CRP, VIT D and DHEA is all you got to track to know why your mitochondria are on or off based upon your beliefs..............

HDL, DHEA, Vit D level Why? HDL tells me three main things.....overall inflammation in the body and at the mitochondrial level, zeta potential (how viscus blood is die to inflammation, EMF, artificial light exposure and how badly their body is lacking in electrons to make ATP at their mitochondria) , HDL levels how permeable the intestinal border is right now, and how efficient sleep really is. DHEA is the middle of the hormone chain, and tells me about androgen power and the immunity switch, so it gives me a decent idea of how metabolically efficient one is.......it tells me about the proximal chain (free T3 and pregnenolone) an it tells me what I should expect from the distal sex steroid hormones if this one is low........the most important thing DHEA tells me is sleep efficiency because of its 98% correlation to IL-6 production in the brain....DHEA is the second most powerful antioxidant in the brain..........from its level and the persons age I can extrapolate what their IGF1 and salivary melatonin are doing to circadian cycles. The vitamin D levels tell me two main things.......how bad immune system is off, and is there a potential slow methylation problem if both the HDL and Vit D level is low.........Then I can test the methylation pathways if I think the clinical story matches the history. Low Vitamin D levels also tell me about mitochondrial efficiency and how tight new synapses are or will be now based upon age and level of the lab.......if the age is above 40 and the level below 20 I know that this patient likely has a severe low brain dopamine level and low sex steroid hormones too. Those are my top three and why. Testing allows us to challenge our beliefs. Understanding the hormone panels and the labs is the Rosetta stone for understanding mitochondrial efficiency. We only are as good as our mitochondrial function. They are the batteries of our cells and much like a new Xmas toy that has bad batteries placed in it it wont work optimally. The same analogy is true for human mitochondria. We need to assess the exhaust from the mitochondria......CO2, H2O, and Tissue O2 levels. This tells us all about how good or bad our Ferrari engines really are running. We should define bioenergetics as the studying the flow of chemical bond energy within organisms. In a living cell, the principal reactions of fuel metabolism take place in the mitochondria, where food energy is released,oxygen is consumed, and water and carbon dioxide are produced. All life on earth utilizes the energy formed from water formation to power pathways and metabolism. Remember the Calvin cycle/photosynthesis, where carbs (glucose) are formed from air (CO2), and energy of the sun? In the mitochondria, the opposite reaction occurs. Energy from the exothermic reaction of water forming from air (O2) and the enzymatic burning of fuel (oxidizable food, glucogen, glucose, fat) result in HEAT and ATP. When one mole of H2O is created from one H2 (hydrogen) and half O2 (oxygen), 286 kJ of power are released (in other words, 68 kcal, which is about one small potato).... FROM FORMATION OF ONLY ONE MOLE OF WATER. So clearly, a high BUN/creatinine ratio on a chem 7 also tells us we are energy starved. This is why dehydration is a major clinical sign of mitochondrial damage and or stress. This is another reason why I ascribe to the mitochondrial theory of aging. We have no direct ability as humans to repair mDNA. It is our weakest link, and as I have said in the Brain Gut 5, we are only as good as our weakest link. Well, this is it for all mammals.

CO2 1/2 O2 = one mole H2O (~18 grams water = 3.5 teaspoons) = 286 kJ of power from water

With cellular respiration, instead of an enormous exothermic explosion (similar to hydrogen bomb), the electrons and protons are added to chain of beads instead a straw pipe made from Q10.......on a step-wise on a gradient known as the electron transport chain (ECT) in plants and animals. A biological mitochondrial 'battery' is created with the 'anode' on the inner mitochondrial membrane side and the 'cathode' on the outer layer. Heat is energy released when oxygen is the final proton acceptor and coupled to the enzyme called F1 F0-ATPase, that forms ATP, the universal currency of cellular energy in the body.

When the protons fall across the proton channel, ATP is formed. We use ATP as fuel every minute every day for all cellular work, then recycled back to ADP. In one day, it is estimated that our mitochondria may produce our own weight in ATP (1). Efficiency of the theoretical transfer of energy from oxidizable fuel to ATP and heat is shockingly good: 39% ATP and 61% heat \[1\]. This data show us mitochondria are energy giants. To give you an idea of how good they are read this: This is is from my blog on June 14, 2011 called What powers life and death? (www.jackkruse.com/what-powers-life-and-death/)

"Bacteria generate energy across their outer membrane. This limits their energy production due to geometry. Their energy production falls off due to a falling surface area to volume area ratio. Animal cells, by absorbing the power plant, now figured out how to internalize energy production and expand the surface area of the inner membranes with massive folding like we see in the surface of the brain. These evolutionary changes are believed to have occurred only once in the history of our planet. Endosymbiosis is rare in bacteria. Peter Mitchell was initially ridiculed for his theories on bio-energentics of mitochondria. He showed how effective this evolutionary maneuver was. He showed that this change caused a pH gradient as well as an electrical charge of about 150 millivolts across the inner membrane. This may sound like a small amount of charge, but consider this fact. The inner membrane is only 4-5 nanometers thick, so the voltage across this membrane is about 30 million volts per meter! For comparison, that is equivalent to the energy in a bolt of lightening. That one bolt has the power generate energy of 1500 three thousand square foot homes in one mitochondria. Each cell has hundreds to thousands of those power houses in them. That is the power surge that fueled evolution and complex life forms."

Obviously many biological factors determine true efficiency: iron status (cytochromes are composed of heme), ubiquinol (not ubiquinone) (huge and another test I often perform) , oxidative and inflammatory state (mentioned above in the three tests), thyroid, HPA axis function (DHEA as a proxy for other hormones) , hormones, etc.

Plant chloroplasts only get 3-6% efficiency from transfer of solar energy to the energy bonds of plant starches and fatty acids. Particular C-4 plants can get 7-8% (sugarcane) and one super cyanobacteria strain Chlorobaculum tepidum achieves 10%. Various modern fuel efficiencies are approximately -- for coal (~20-30s%) and solar (20%). This puts into perspective how important mitochondrial efficiency really is to optimal health.......without the ability to make ATP what do we call a person? They are called a cadaver. Illnesses are the train stops on the subway line between optimal and being a cadaver.......if your batteries are off it is a measure of your real wellness or illness in my view.

CITES:

1\. http://highered.mcgraw-hill.com/sites/dl/free/0073525502/930160/mad25502\_ch08.pdf

# Thread: Liverwurst basic recepe
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liverwurst-basic-recepe.5430/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Charlotte — 2013-01-11T15:00:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liverwurst-basic-recepe.5430/post-70319>

2 kg Liver (pork, game, beef, chicken, whatever you like or have)
2 kg Pork bacon like you use for breakfast bacon (not treated though or treated for different taste)
400 g porkmeat or any other meat (fatty)
400 g Porkfat or any other fat
4 onionsw
80 g salt, pepper, any other spice you like
majoram, garlic

Put the liver shortly in hot water to be able to remove skin, inside it should still be raw and bloody, Meanwhile cook the 400 g porkmeat in boiling water (makes a nice broth).
Cut all in small pieces and put through the grinder twice making it as fine as possible. Cut the onion (and garlic) in small pieces and sautee in the porkfat until soft and sweet. Then mix it all together thoroughly and use spices as you like. We have tons of different ranging from mild to very spicy, so suit your own taste or divide it in batches and give each one its own taste for variety.
Then fill in glasses and put them in Water, covering except for the lid. Let it steep there at app. 95Â°C for 2 hrs.
Take out, put the lid firmly on and you can keep it. Let it cool slowly so the fat does not collect at the walls of the jars.

Important is, to have it real fatty as otherwise it is difficult to spread. You can also experiment with other meats or less liver.

Just found a recepe for making rolls from eggs and psyllium husk flour which I will try with the liverwurst.

# Thread: Going Raw
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5432/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T16:49:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5432/post-70328>

OK, I've been edging in this direction veeerrrrryyyy slowly. Inger is a stunning testimony to the power of raw food so I'm thinking it might be time.

I asked for, and received, the food grinder attachment for my Kitchen Aid mixer for xmas with the notion I would try my hand at steak tartare. Even have a recipe downloaded.

Today I went hunting for info on a raw meat diet for humans and I found this blog: http://theprimalparent.com WOWZER. bookmarked!

Snagged a recipe for ceviche from food.com and am now thinking that sea bass that's been hiding in the freezer is destined for a bowl of lime juice along with with a handful of scallops.

So anyone else doing raw and do you have tips, tricks, recipes, suggestions. I am NOT ready to do high meat nor am I ready to eat lamb heart (I guess I'm not as predatory as I think because the thought of all those spring lambs cavorting out in the field just keeps me from eating it) raw or otherwise. Baby steps here folks.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-11T17:30:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5432/post-70336>

Darleen...my approach has sneaked up me. I didn't really set out to eat raw, but found myself ready to pop a few slices of this and that into my mouth....then I would cook the rest. Really thin slices is best for me! Steak in larger amounts is now no problem, but for the organ meats, I slice things when they are still partially frozen, that makes it easy to control the thickness.

I love horseradish and pickles, capers etc. They also have a lot of histamine, so I have to control myself, but the taste of something spicy really makes the raw stuff easy to handle.

The other day I bought some octopus and inspired by a documentary on the best sushi master in Japan....I ate it raw with some lime juice and wasabi. It was yum.

So...have fun and see what happens! Just the fact that you can think about it represents a lot of change doesn't it??

### caroline — 2013-01-11T18:51:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5432/post-70346>

The thing that really works for me as far as raw meat goes......I buy small pieces of rump steak -with no fat on it and then set it on a wooden board and just leave in the fridge - as I use it I just keep opening more packages. I am eating some now that has been sitting about 5 days I guess and it is nicely drying and is somewhat like salami. I can't even eat a big steak anymore - seems really gross to chow down on that much meat.....

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T19:17:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5432/post-70349>

MMM octopus with wasabi sounds kinda tasty, actually! Of course, good luck finding it here in Wyoming.

Caroline that's what I was looking for ... I'll try that too. Sounds like a nice little nosh about midday since I'm not real hungry then but if I don't eat a little something then by 4 I'm ready to eat everything in sight.

### Destiny — 2013-01-11T19:25:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5432/post-70351>

I need to try Caroline's "salami". I made steak tartare this week and it was yummy. My husband was just asking me if there was any leftover and I said nope, you are too late { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### diane — 2013-01-11T21:40:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5432/post-70363>

I made salmon ceviche and loved it. Of course I included a generous helping of cilantro - and I love it! I have also made steak tartare, but would love a really good recipe for it. I've also done raw steak with just a bit of balsamic vinegar (which had nothing added) and olive oil on it. Or raw fish with some wheat free tamari sauce (i use just a little bit) and pickled ginger.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T22:09:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5432/post-70364>

Fixed steak tartare tonight and made way too much, LOL. As cooked meat it would have been a good portion but raw ... only got about half down and I was done. I will say it was delicious. Ground sirloin, dijon mustard, capers, balsamic vinegar, olive oil, minced onion, egg yolk, salt and pepper. I'll cook the leftovers for breakfast with some bacon and an egg.

### endless — 2013-01-12T05:26:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5432/post-70376>

I'm definitely leaning towards more raw all the time...this morning I had 1/2 lb raw elk meat for breakfast. I would like to be able to eat raw meat at least once week...if I had the money I'd eat it everyday!

Hi All

I am trying to sort out my eczema with GAPS diet and have just started CT. Still at the face dunking stage about every second day for the last two weeks but my eczema has flared up quote a bit in the last two weeks. Could this be a result of the CT? Any experiences with eczema and CT out there?

Thanks

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-eczema.5433/post-70378>

### Destiny — 2013-01-12T17:44:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-eczema.5433/post-70442>

I have read this story awhile ago - I loved it. I often talk to a Greek man when I go to the rock pool at the ocean. Oddly ...he is one of the few people who is really interested in C/T ...altho has never heard the term of course ... and I can talk to him about cutting out sugars altho he loves Baklava! and he is so interested in being as healthy as possible. I first met him at the rock pool in the winter - he tries to swim everyday. I have convinced him to try ice baths for some very sore leg muscles. His Doc told him to use heat. It was like a revelation to him when I said to follow your own instincts and get more than one opinion when necessary ....\[Doc wants to operate\]. I talk to him alot about what his parents and grandparents and extended family ate in Greece ..... a much simpler life and much more healthy - a lot of seafood and wine { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. I just made a banana bread from Abel James' site. It was quite nice - maybe not as great as he says - but he is a guy! I was looking for things to make because my grandsons were coming for the weekend and I have decided not to participate in their unhealthy eating anymore. They are very healthy and very active but I want to see them stay this way..... so I bought lots of fruit \[in season here\] and dark chocolate and this banana bread ....they loved it and ate quite alot of it. If anyone wants it I am happy to post it...

### vlynnb — 2013-01-12T17:22:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/banana-bread.5435/post-70437>

Yes, I want it. Dealing with my grandchildren's usual diets graciously is a big problem for me. I have one set of grandchildren who live just downstairs from me so we eat together often. Another set I take care of on a regular basis in their home as their mother is on chemotherapy right now and I would really like to help all of them eat better but it is so hard to facilitate any change.

### janagram — 2013-01-12T20:29:35+0200

### Kiera2 — 2013-01-12T21:00:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/banana-bread.5435/post-70453>

Is this it?

http://archive.aweber.com/fatburningman/OqeQs/h/The\_Best\_Banana\_Bread\_of\_Your.htm

### caroline — 2013-01-12T21:00:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/banana-bread.5435/post-70454>

Banana Bread recipe

4 ripe bananas - mine were kind of average size I think \[this is aways a problem with banana bread recipes -they should specify weight\]

4 eggs

1/2 cup Almond butter or cashew butter

4 TBSP GF butter

1 tsp vanilla

mix all above together well in a cuisinart or some such appliance

mix together all the following dry ingredients and then add to wet stuff and mix well

1/2 cup of almond flour

1 tsp each of baking soda and baking powder

1 pinch of sea salt

1 TBSP cinnamon
\[could fold in some chopped nuts if you want\]

grease a 5" x9" loaf tin

add mixture and smooth and then bake at 350 for about 45 minutes if using a metal tin ...maybe 60 min if using glass \[or someone on his site made muffins\]

This goes together really, really fast and doesn't seem to be tempermental at all. I bet it freezes beautifully too

I am so impressed with my self - I have been having a lot of brain fog this past year or so and it turns out I remembered this recipe with no problem and I have only made it once!! If that isn't a win I don't know what is!!!! thanks yet again to J.K. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

If I wasn't explicit enuf please let me know!

### Claudia — 2013-01-13T00:01:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-and-women.5438/post-70467>

How was the weight loss and women webinar? I'm pretty much struggling with a lot, one of those being financial, and was hoping it might be discussed on here. Are the webinars discussed ever?

Would love some insights?

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-13T14:49:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-and-women.5438/post-70531>

I am still trying to make sense of that webinar (partly because I was seriously jet-lagged when I listened). I think my simple take-home message was that women are much more complicated hormonally and also much more sensitive to the environment (we inherit all of our mitochondria from our mothers, so survival depends on women being super-sensitive to the environment, encoding that and passing that down), which means a more complex journey back to healthy homeostasis if our previous ways of eating/living have created health problems, imbalance and/or disease. I think my major take-home was to keep going with what Dr Kruse has laid out, not to give up, and to expect time for changes to happen.

Just asked google.
.1. Hold the control button on your keyboard, and click the link to the webinar.. Then when you get a menu, choose "save as" or save target as or save file.. Or some variant that implies youre saving it to your computer.. Pay attention to where youre saving it.
2.When the computer has finished saving use your file menu to find the file you just saved, and click it to open it. You'll probably get a choiceof what you want to do.. Find the option that means play.. It could be run, or open.. You might have to choose what program you use to play the file.. But knowing apple, they will make the decision for you.. You should have a program called quicktime that should play it.

# Thread: Question about Reverse T3 Labs.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-reverse-t3-labs.5446/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-01-13T13:13:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-reverse-t3-labs.5446/post-70514>

11/4/11
rT3 24 (range 11-32 ng/dL) ratio with Free T 3-11.3

6/19/12
rT3 28 (range 11-32 ng/dL) ratio with Free T 3-10

12/17/12
rT3 13 (range 11-32 ng/dL) ratio with Free T 3-21.5

Without knowing anything about me, why would my reverse T3 improve? My TSH is 4.45 (range .35-5.50 uIU/mL). I have hypothyroid symptoms and yet my reverse T3 went down. I am not understanding it. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### vkiernan — 2013-01-13T15:51:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-reverse-t3-labs.5446/post-70548>

Maybe you have corrected the reason you had a high rt3 in the first place whether it be too much stress, surgery or low ferritin. Sometimes happens inside you that makes a high rt3 to slow your body down naturally. The tsh is telling your body to make more t4 which is supposed to make t3 and the fact that your rt3 is reducing maybe means you are trying to make more t3 to get into your cells. That is my .02 cents and that is about all it is worth.

### [email redacted] — 2013-01-13T15:55:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-reverse-t3-labs.5446/post-70549>

vkiernan, thanks for the .02 cents! It's very much appreciated! I am the caregiver to my 92 year old aunt and 91 year old mother. Both went into assisted living at the end of July and I have been very much less stressed.

### vkiernan — 2013-01-13T17:50:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-reverse-t3-labs.5446/post-70574>

That darn ole' stress wrecks havoc on the body and does things you would not believe. I hope you can do something to reduce your stress and keep up with the family issues!

# Thread: Thyroid confusion
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-confusion.5447/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndra — 2013-01-13T13:32:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-confusion.5447/post-70516>

I've been on low-dose Armour thyroid for several months and have been feeling progressively better (more energy, better mood). When I saw my doctor in mid-November, she thought I was going hyper (was feeling warm a lot and was losing weight without trying) so she had me cut my dose in half (down to 1/4 grain). At that time, my labs were:

TSH 1.67: range 0.45-4.50
Free T3: 2.6, range 2.0-4.4
Free T4: 1.0, range 0.82-1.7
RT3: 17, range 9.2-24.1
FT3/RT3 (calculated at STTM): 15.3 (goal is >20)

A couple of weeks ago, I decided I might be getting hyper because I my pulse was too high, especially at night, and I was too hot and sleeping poorly (though sleep is a constant problem anyway). I stopped taking my 1/4 gr Armour on a January 1 and then ran thyroid tests on January 4. Results were:

TSH 2.67: range 0.45-4.50
Free T3: 2.1, range 2.0-4.4
Free T4: 1.21, range 0.82-1.7
RT3: 9.2, range 9.2-24.1
FT3/RT3 (calculated at STTM): 22.8

So, the good news is my FT3/RT3 ratio is much improved but FT3 is still very low and TSH is fairly high.

As an experiment, after being off Armour for 10 days, I started up again 3 days ago. Ugh. Too hot during the day, waking up hot at night, and high pulse again, so I quit again.

Does it make sense that I would be hyper with low FT3 and high TSH?

I've measured thyroid antibodies 3 times since and they have been stable each time: TPO 9 (0-34) and Antithyroglob <20 (0-40) so I'm thinking it's not Hashimoto's.

I plan to check in with my doctor but am wondering if you all have any ideas?

### vkiernan — 2013-01-13T17:48:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-confusion.5447/post-70572>

I am wondering if you have a low cortisol problem. Hence the reaction to more thyroid med and poor sleep - too much for your system too handle.. Looks like your ft4 is turning into rt3, not ft3 like it is supposed to. Might be a good time to try t3 only since you won't convert that to rt3 once you clear your t4. Got to figure out why you are making rt3 in the first place. Maybe read the All Things T3 thread. Lot's of good info in there.

### Lyndra — 2013-01-13T19:42:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-confusion.5447/post-70600>

Thanks for the response - I know you've been through a lot with thyroid and have done your homework. I have been battling adrenal fatigue for a while now. My cortisol was at the bottom of the range throughout the day when I first tested early this year but was better when I tested a few months ago, though still too low in the AM and too high at night. I tried T3-only early on but it gave me migraines; I'll talk to my doctor about trying another formulation to see if that is better.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-13T23:32:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-confusion.5447/post-70618>

Stop the Thyroid Madness people also suggest looking at ferritin and iron levels if things are not working with increased thyroid.

### bigknitwit — 2013-01-14T11:23:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-confusion.5447/post-70661>

I am dealing with high pulse too (especially in the morning after breakfast, and sometimes at night just as I'm starting to fall asleep), despite being on a lower than optimal thyroid dose. I think it has to do with cortisol, somehow. I used to think it was low cortisol, however, during episodes of elevated heartrate, I've also noticed that I have far less blood sugar control than otherwise. This leads me to think that in fact my cortisol is elevated (which would inhibit insulin from doing it's thing). Just to compound things even further, on really bad days, I've taken 10mg of hydrocortisone, and it usually helps stabilize my heartrate... So I'm not really sure which way to call it. On days when I sleep poorly, I struggle much more with higher heart rates (I think cortisol would be higher on those days..?).

I will also mention that from my own experimentation, I do not tolerate Armour thyroid, no matter what the labs say. I am doing much better on a combination of T4 and T3 (cytomel). I think it probably has to do with the fact that I can't eat pork without a reaction...

One more thing to mention: I few times I saw d-ribose mentioned on these forums, so I decided to do a little research/reading about it, which lead me to the book, "Fatigued to Fantastic." I've been reading the book, which mainly tries to address issues people with CFS deal with. His perspective on the issues resonates a great deal with the things Dr. K talks about (like the hypothalamus being suppressed, and energy crisis within the cell). So I started taking d-ribose at the dose recommended in the book, and also started acetyl-l-carnitine at the LEF dose recommendation (2000-3000mg/day). I think they are making a real difference for me in dealing with all of this. I have tried countless different supplements over the years, and very rarely can I say that I think I can "feel" a difference taking one. The heart rate issues are diminished (not gone), sleep is a little better (I still struggle, especially if I'm woken up during the night), and my head feels much more, well, normal, as opposed to feeling like I desperately need so kind of stimulation.

I'm not sure if any of this helps, and I apologize if this is coming across as "all about me." I thought sharing about my experiences may help you figure out yours a little...

### Lyndra — 2013-01-14T11:44:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-confusion.5447/post-70664>

BKW - thanks so much for your thoughts. I am also wondering about my cortisol levels and hearing your experiences is very helpful. I thought my adrenal fatigue was doing much better so I started exercising more a couple of weeks ago. It went well for a few days and then big crash - lots of fatigue and poor recovery so I backed off. You and V may both be right about the cortisol situation. Based on 23andme results, i have methylation issues (waiting on a consult with Tim Jackson to learn more) and have a few mito Rx supps on order. I'm thinking this is a complicated situation and there is no one simple answer to improvement. What a way to work on becoming more patient...

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-14T13:11:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-confusion.5447/post-70678>

Bigknitwit...I was wondering how you were the other day...good to hear from you and great news that the supps are making a difference.

# Thread: Website v2.0 - a Candid Review and Ideas for Improvement
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-v2-0-a-candid-review-and-ideas-for-improvement.5449/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 53
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-01-13T14:06:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-v2-0-a-candid-review-and-ideas-for-improvement.5449/post-70519>

It has been almost 2 months since the forums have transitioned to "JackKruse 2.0". As Dr. Kruse says "question everything". As a former webmaster, below are my observations.

Postive changes of website v1.0 to v2.0 â€¦
\* Spambots have virtually disappeared
\* Allowed questions in "Ask Jack" to be limited to premium members â€¦ Important for Dr. Kruse's time management

Negative changes of website v1.0 to v2.0 â€¦
\* Considerably slower webpage loads
\* Less user friendly for quickly identifying threads with unread posts â€¦ the old format of bold vs. normal text was a very easy way to indentify unread posts
\* Volume of posts appears to have diminished considerably â€¦ some of the previous high volume Forum posters have clearly stated they stopped posting due to frustration with website v2.0.
\* JK has stated many times he wants more starfish â€¦ but version 2.0 is not user friendly, so posting volume has declined â€¦ which probably means the growth in the number of starfish has slowed
\* Annoying social media links on every post â€¦ which slows page loads â€¦ A thread contains a max of 20 posts per webpage. So on a full page of posts you have at least 140 social media links (7 medias x 20 posts)
\* In contrast other popular paleo forums such MarksDailyApple or PaleoHacks have much faster loading webpages â€¦ but they don't have tons of social media links
\* I discovered that disabling the "Cloudfare" plug-in on Firefox makes the annoying social media links go away â€¦ but in order to post, I must reactivate Cloudfare â€¦ so I am much less likely to post in the Forums
\* Search of keywords and user posts was much easier on website v1.0
\* Members state that new posts are disappearing before appearing in the Forums
\* Part of the reduction in number of posts is likely due to lack of "TapaTalk" for people posting on their phone

In summary, I believe Dr. Kruse's prime mission is reach as many people as possible. In addition, for people to become starfish, they must participate. Unfortunately, it appears website v2.0 has failed in both areas. The goal of this website should be share valuable information is an easy to use format â€¦ lets save the "mind bending" for JK posts (not learning how to use a new software program).

Ideas for improvement â€¦
\* Reduce the number of social media links â€¦ max of one FB, Twitter, etc. link per page (vs. current of one per post) â€¦ MDA is a prime example of a high volume website that uses zero social media links. â€¦ IMHO social media is way over hyped by marketing companies â€¦ Many news items, videos, etc. went viral long before social media.
\* Post a survey such as "Survey Monkey" to find out member opinions about the new website â€¦ the marketing company that created website v2.0 has a definite opinion â€¦ lets find out if the majority of members agree with their decisions?
\* Set a deadline for making significant improvements to v2.0 â€¦ if SimplePress will not allow those improvements, IMHO, a decision to return to the old forum style should be implemented. â€¦ One thing I admired about the blog is that several different formats for comments were tried â€¦ and several were inadequate, so a decision was made to return to the old format... until the present format was implemented.
\* A laser like focus on a friendly, easy to use format should be the number one priority.
\* The percentage of Internet access via mobile is rapidly increasing â€¦ to maximize success, the website needs easy posting via phones.

My vision for website v3.0 â€¦
\* Return the Forums to old format with exception of "Ask Jack"
\* Move "Ask Jack" out of the Forums and to different part of the website (password protected folder) â€¦ this change would continue to restrict access to "Ask Jack", but would not hamstring the Forums to a certain program like SimplePress.
\* Appoint several more Moderators to remove all posts from spam bots that might result from returning to the old Forum format.

You'd probably be better off cancelling one day and suscribing the next. But there are so many ways that plan could fall apart. Good luck!!

Another kink: I have discovered that I miss a lot of posts because the 'topics with unread posts' box that pops up tells me how many new posts there are on each thread, but if I don't have time to read them all, the next time I go there the 'number of new posts' notification button has dropped off of each thread. So I only know that there are unread posts, not how many there are. I can click on the latest post, but not the 'latest unread' post. I have missed whole pages without knowing, and this adds significantly to confusion and a feeling of being quite lost in here. Keeping notes of when I read what, and what number post I'm up to in each thread is not an efficient way to go.

### danrmorris — 2013-01-14T20:15:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-v2-0-a-candid-review-and-ideas-for-improvement.5449/post-70762>

Hello Jack Kruse enthusiasts!

I'm the web guy on Jack's team. Thanks for creating this thread and adding great comments about what would make this community better. Jack asked that I add to this thread about our upcoming plans, which I think is a great idea seeing as how sincere you all are about making this community thrive.

So we've heard you loud and clear on the forum. Moving it to Simple Press didn't create a better user experience in the end. Our original goal was to eliminate the need for you to have to log-in to two different places. We also wanted to be able to pull forum snippets into the site using what's called "Short Code". And from a data standpoint, having the same audience on two separate sites made it very hard for us to see what kind of content you really were gravitating toward so we could better serve your needs. None of those benefits trumped the usability of the old forum. So we're moving back to that, and hope that improves the experience. WooHoo!

As to a timeline. . . we'd like to have it done today. But the forum is big. Part of the issue with creating a new forum is porting all the threads, users and comments back. There's no software that does this instantly. It will likely be an overnight thing. We've engaged the guys who do it best and should start on that this week.

Another topic on this has been the search bar. I agree, it's not the best. We're actually on our third "kind" of search algorithm. What makes the search interesting is that Jack's blog topics are so content rich that often time a blog's title is less relevant than the content of a post with a different title. And then there's the completely random results. We're still actively looking for a better way to parse the blog posts to return better results.

As to the ease in which specific posts are found. . . well that's just a toughie. There are currently 144 blog posts on the site. Providing a link to all 144 on the home page just isn't practical for anyone. So then which blog posts do we put a link to on the home page? Or how do we make every post easily findable in two clicks?

Specifically the most recent comments have been around the Top 10 Paleo Supplements post. To get to that you can either press the Nutrients and Supplements button in the sidebar and it's the fourth or fifth result on that page - two clicks. There's a link to it on the Epi-Paleo Store for people who were looking to buy Supplements - two clicks. And I just added a link to the Quick Links section to make it even easier - one click. Yes, an improved search button would be good. We're working on that.

I will be more active here in the event you have questions. Prior to today I've been reading, considering and implementing the changes and issues that have come up but haven't chosen to join the conversation. Part of that is because I didn't feel it was my place to join your private chat. The other part is. . . I just should have anyway.

So we're listening and making changes as we hear your voices. Thanks for participating.

### [email redacted] — 2013-01-15T00:48:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-v2-0-a-candid-review-and-ideas-for-improvement.5449/post-70812>

CAVEMAM IN THE HOUSEEEEEEEE.

That's me. Typing this. I'm number 2 poster - still.

Really, the slowness is from social media buttons? Whar the logic of sending members (many paying!) AWAY from the site to googles for search, making them log in even though the user is clearly recognized, making the user jump through all kinds of hoops like doing "tickets" is just hunky-dory with the site but the line is drawn at making them figure out how to find Jack at facebook or twitter?! FOR THAT EPIC PROBLEM LETS MAKE THE SITE GO PAINFULLY SLOW. Nobody thinks the social media buttons are for the users. It's for clickygoodness.

Fixing this forum is NOT a job for marketing people! I can say that because I used to be one.

Also, "social media" sites are out. Forums are in. You did it backwards.

I love ATL-Paleo for bringing this up. I must say I'm a little giddy about the return to the old format and tapatalk.

For me, a former prolific poster, the changeover caused a few issues that made it difficult for me to post:

1\. format issues brought up in the other posts here.

2\. I was charged for membership after cancelling and requests for repairs were ignored or denied (not sure which). Part of me is sure my $250 was not material to Jack but the net result was I was basically donated $250 to Jack and lost two consults. That was a personal and pretty big de-motivator for supporting this site - and possibly Jack if he even knows anything about it. I wasn't a cheap person trying to get free stuff. I bought all the cookbooks and had super memberships and purchased extra consults and the lack of assistance with that for a membership that had no rules spelled out at the time of purchase, and half the benefits never materialized is just, well, lame.

3\. A change of tone in blog posts as well as what was going on from the site management eroded the balance here. This is a personal preference, but I'm not the only one with this opinion I suspect. It went from Science heavy to woo-woo fast. Although I'm not motivated by a pithy phrase or quote, I get that others are. However, content went from mostly science and things to research and learn from to lots of motivational posts. I think the mix reversed and I think that caused atrophy in some the thought provoking posters.

Man, glad I came here today and got that off my mind. You can follow my latest goings on at my personal blog. . cavemam.com

I hope to see the forum come back together - I totally miss the feel of community.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-15T09:51:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-v2-0-a-candid-review-and-ideas-for-improvement.5449/post-70854>

The sky is above me, the ground is below me the fire burns within me. Happiness is not the absence of problems, it's the ability to deal with them.......the fire within hardens my resolve, it is the momentum of the mission, constancy of commitment. Many people start, few finish, many have a dream, but few see it through. People who finish the job are just more dedicated to the outcome.

There are two kinds of people in this world: people who are involved and people who are committed. People who are involved give some of themselves to the cause they are believing in and people who are committed give all of themselves to the causes they believe in.

Throw a log on your fire and become committed to the outcome.

We like feedback but we need you in the game. If your not in the game we can't listen to you cause you ain't here.

That being said, re the forum, I'm pretty adaptable, so I figured my way around the old forum, I figured my way around the new forum. And will go back to the old way - whatever it takes. I'm getting way more out of interacting with the people here than to let a change in format stop me.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-15T15:09:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-v2-0-a-candid-review-and-ideas-for-improvement.5449/post-70895>

> **SueC said**
>
> "CAVEMAM IN THE HOUSEEEEEEEE. ... A change of tone in blog posts as well as what was going on from the site management eroded the balance here. This is a personal preference, but I'm not the only one with this opinion I suspect. It went from Science heavy to woo-woo fast. Although I'm not motivated by a pithy phrase or quote, I get that others are. However, content went from mostly science and things to research and learn from to lots of motivational posts. I think the mix reversed and I think that caused atrophy in some the thought provoking posters."
> I gotta agree here. I'm still reading, but I like the science better than the philosophy.

They must come together for you to get the progression........it is simple as that. There was no philosophy. That is how your brain perceived it. That is part of the problem. It shows you things.......specifically the Rolex in your head.......it is not sync'd and may never get that way because of your own personal N=1. To explain it, we need to get you to a place where you'd understand implicitly that what you think and feel are really endocrine secretions from your neurons and they are built upon the amount of good and bad electron flow to your mitochondria to make ATP efficiently or not. ............that is precisely where we are headed and it is 100% science way over most peoples heads. To get you to understand it well you need to ride the journey the way I am laying it out. Your confusion maybe due to that you dont just do not know about the complexities of where the science lies ........and because of that misconception, you think the blog's are philosophical when they are not.......they are 100% science.

The journey to ride is yours and your alone........but you ain't the author of the journey and explaining very complex science to lay people. I am that author and I think I know how to lay it out for everyone's perspective and not just the outliers.

I have lots of people I have to consider.

### caroline — 2013-01-15T15:55:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-v2-0-a-candid-review-and-ideas-for-improvement.5449/post-70899>

I totally agree with what Diane said above......A few technical issues is not going to keep me from getting Dr. K"s message - this is obviously a labor of love for him and I - for one- am sticking like glue! There is so much science here that goes way over my head - but that isn't going to stop me either - I keep re reading and getting more of the gold each time. It is really amazing when I come across something that I think I haven't seen before and then I come to realize that I have ...but now somewhat get it. But I love all the other stuff too - everyone helping and caring for one another ......learning about someone else's take on things and the lovely stuff and the fun stuff and the silly stuff. If this forum was 100% science - it wouldn't feel like home. I love learning about and seeing everyone's personality and dimension and struggles. It makes me feel like I am not alone. Those of us who don't have a lot of money to spend are learning to develop our intuition and apply it to our life. What could be more important than that? I love this journey that Dr. Kruse is taking us on ....... this is the ride of a lifetime and we get to go along...... I feel like I have gotten the pot of gold at the end of the rainbow. Once again .... just saying thanks is not nearly enough.....I hope we can all make you proud...

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-v2-0-a-candid-review-and-ideas-for-improvement.5449/post-71187>

Hey Dan, I reckon the two clicks thing is overstressed in web navigation.. So long as the user knows they are heading in the right direction an extra click or two won't hurt so long as they find what they're chasing...

I'd love to see a chronological list of all blogs too.. I want to read from 'cover to cover' as it were, and I am perfectly happy to work my way down one long page to read them (or from the bottom up, whatever

)

If it helps anyone, I open all the forum posts in a new tab one after the other, and then i don't have to wait for pages to load.. I do this with blogs i want to read too.. Then i don't forget to read them.

Also, for anyone who has been getting the 'can't save - too many links' error, copy your post, (drop it in notepad if you're worried about losing it) then cancel your post. Hit reply and send a one word reply.. I use test. Save that then go back to your post and edit it... Paste in your original post, and it saves! Yep, pita, but a workaround until the old forums are live again.

Also, i like science in blogs.. Forums are especially good places to put woo woo imnsho

# Thread: Educating Doctors?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educating-doctors.5454/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-01-13T23:05:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educating-doctors.5454/post-70616>

I am thoroughly discouraged. I've been through 5 Drs and I feel like I'm in a time warp. I'll tell about the last one and maybe someone can tell me where I need to improve my communication. I went to the one and only large university hospital. We talked about why I thought a certain prescription would be helpful. She said, "you have a point, now let's see what the literature says about this." She clicked away at her screen and read, " this (drug) is not recommended for your situation." I peeked around at the screen and pointed out that the information she was reading was 35 years old. We discussed 'best practices' and she felt she had to follow them to protect her license. She also didn't seem to understand that the drug from the 35 year old info was a different chemical make up than the current one I was discussing. I am moving out of state shortly. Can I ask to see the office's best practice guidelines before I pick a Doctor where I am going? I probably shouldn't pick a large university hospital either as they have to all follow 'the party line'. Right? So I had to pay her money to 'educate' her and she wasn't going to do anything anyway because of 'best practices'.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-14T17:09:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educating-doctors.5454/post-70725>

offices do not have best practices......guidelines. It is called staying up to date with CME. Clearly your doc is not.

# Thread: Meal timing  late evening training in Amateur Athletes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing-late-evening-training-in-amateur-athletes.5455/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lauren_r — 2013-01-14T02:20:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing-late-evening-training-in-amateur-athletes.5455/post-70625>

I was wondering if anyone could shed some info about meal timing in amateur athletes who are leptin sensitive.
I have been re-reading the sleep info and was wondering how someone who trains at night, perhaps for an extended period, at an elite level might counterbalance the impacts of prolactin release, GH release during sleep, or make best fo the situation.

What impact does eating in the evening less than four hours before sleep on an LS individual?
What if they are optimally healthy?

In regards to training in the evening for extended period 6-8pm, the individual will experience hunger and say eat a late night 8:30pm meal.
How great a negative impact does this have?
If a meal must be eaten, what are the ideal macros?
Any other ideas or experiences?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-14T18:38:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing-late-evening-training-in-amateur-athletes.5455/post-70743>

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-22T20:19:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meal-timing-late-evening-training-in-amateur-athletes.5455/post-72397>

Wish me luck when I go to my parent's place...they eat dinner way too late... I miss them so much but I have to admit I'm concerned for this. It really does affect me and I really do need to eat 4 hrs before going to bed.

Also I don't want to go to bed later than 10pm either to accommodate later dinner. Sometimes they honestly don't eat until 8pm! To go to bed at might night is too hard for me to do...and besides we're supposed to go to bed before midnight anyway. Even my brother (with problems) complains of eating too late. Hmmm.

The thing is my dad does not get home until later so you have on the one hand the desire to eat as a family...but on the other the bad timing. ...

# Thread: Mitochondria Phosphorylation Efficiency
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-phosphorylation-efficiency.5456/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glen PDQ — 2013-01-14T03:03:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-phosphorylation-efficiency.5456/post-70631>

http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21284982
Cell Metab. 2011 Feb 2;13(2):149-59. doi: 10.1016/j.cmet.2011.01.004.
Dietary inorganic nitrate improves mitochondrial efficiency in humans.
Larsen FJ, Schiffer TA, Borniquel S, Sahlin K, Ekblom B, Lundberg JO, Weitzberg E.
Source
Department of Physiology and Pharmacology, Karolinska Institutet, 11486 Stockholm, Sweden. [email redacted]

"We recently demonstrated that dietary nitrate reduces oxygen cost during physical exercise, but the mechanism remains unknown. In a double-blind crossover trial we studied the effects of a dietary intervention with inorganic nitrate on basal mitochondrial function and whole-body oxygen consumption in healthy volunteers. Skeletal muscle mitochondria harvested after nitrate supplementation displayed an improvement in oxidative phosphorylation efficiency (P/O ratio) and a decrease in state 4 respiration with and without atractyloside and respiration without adenylates. The improved mitochondrial P/O ratio correlated to the reduction in oxygen cost during exercise. Mechanistically, nitrate reduced the expression of ATP/ADP translocase, a protein involved in proton conductance. We conclude that dietary nitrate has profound effects on basal mitochondrial function."

How can we increase our ingestion of bioactive nitrogen oxides safely? Would dietary nitrogen oxides help mitochondria outside of exercise? Does it only help skeletal muscle mitochondria or could it help mitochondria in brain and other organs, too? - Glen PDQ

### Glen PDQ — 2013-01-14T03:23:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-phosphorylation-efficiency.5456/post-70632>

http://www.pnas.org/content/107/41/17716.full

Dietary inorganic nitrate reverses features of metabolic syndrome in endothelial nitric oxide synthase-deficient mice.
Mitochondrial Biogenesis
Glucose Homeostasis
Blood Pressure

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-14T15:49:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-phosphorylation-efficiency.5456/post-70703>

> **Glen PDQ said**
>
> http://www.pnas.org/content/107/41/17716.full
> Dietary inorganic nitrate reverses features of metabolic syndrome in endothelial nitric oxide synthase-deficient mice.
> Mitochondrial Biogenesis
> Glucose Homeostasis
> Blood Pressure

If this is where you are going.........you are going to love where I am going soon.......remember the juice surrounding the release of CT 4, CT 6, CT 5 and 6? Well what I got coming makes them look like a the minor leagues.........2013 I am out to slay some big dragons that the big boys in paleo think are foundational. They aint and I got the ticket to ride that ride.

Also it increases bloodflow and that is always good when wanting to reapir skin and get it more alive and youthful.

The secon thing I would use are seaweeds! I have started to make me seaweedwater for my face these days, I use it as a tonic. I just smear it on my face mornings and evenings and let it stay. They have all the minerals and stuff that skin needs to repair. So I would just make extra strong soaking water from seaweed and put it on every place that is saggy, mornings and evenings. Maybe after dry brushing? Seaweed baths are so good but I guess to put concentrated seaweed water on the skin does the same!

I am not thinking those two things makes the saggy skin go away over night.. hmmm.. but in a year or two I guess it will help big time if doing all the other stuff that Jack suggested, too!

### maggie — 2013-01-14T12:19:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saggy-skin-question-in-ask-jack.5457/post-70669>

I also started using seaweed in my bath a couple of weeks ago and I find it really refreshing. I make an infusion in hot water with bladderwrack and a ton of epsom salts and toss that into the cold tub after it cools. I like the bladderwrack because it comes granulated and I feel that that works better in a tea. Feels great afterwards. I'm going to do it with face dunks now-- thanks Inge, for another brilliant idea.My fridge is full of jars of green tea w/ kombu and dulse thanks to you.

Another thing I started yesterday after listening to the latest webinar -- I added the contents of a couple of caps of CoQ10 + some D Ribose to about 1/4 cup of coconut oil and have been putting that on my face. I was googling around for info on D Ribose last week and stumbled on a patent application for a face cream and some info on tests that indicated the efficacy of D Ribose. So that gave me the idea.

Then Jack mentioned in the webinar that CoQ10 falls off a cliff at around age 70, and my face is a testimonial to that fact! I turned 70 in Sept and in the months leading up to and following the date, my facial skin kind of collapsed. It was shocking, so fast and dramatic. So I added CoQ10 to the mix. It mixes very smoothly. I'll report if I see any changes.

It is nice to put on your face t feels great! I am so confident you could use it on the whole body! Seaweeds are really really good for skin! They have all the minerals and stuff that skin needs to repair. So I would just make extra strong soaking water from seaweed and put it on every place that is saggy, mornings and evenings. Maybe after dry brushing? Seaweed baths are so good but I guess to put concentrated seaweed water on the skin does the same!
